### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-11T16:59:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excellent-lectures-on-electron-chain-transport.22280/post-252899>

One big problem with this lecture is that it is filled with dogma. So let's update this for the Black Swan in training

1\. He says ATP is the dominant form of energy in a cell. Nope See Gilbert Ling's work and how Pollack extended it.
2\. Because he believes what he said in #1 he makes error two......says the charge inside the matrix is not as high as the intermembrane space. Not true either. The matrix is loaded with H+ by 1000 fold because this version of Hydrogen is compressed by electric and magnetic fields generated by the movement of electrons from NAD+ to oxygen. He is only reporting the molecular biology to you and not giving you the full story. Why? Is it because he is an ass? no. He is regurgitating what he was taught and I know that because it is what I was taught too. This is a very left brain thing to behold. The education system focuses teaching us about details of things while not allowing us to see the whole working in unison. To fully grasp the ETC you need to understand the physics at play. The interaction of the incident light on the water created creates a massive charge in water and this increases the EZ and redox.........he never mentions that at all. Where does the incident EMF come from? For those who saw my Vermont 2018 video you know, there are two ways it can happen......one is from incident sunlight whose energy and info is carried by hemoglobin to NAD+ and then there is a nuclear effect of the electric and magnetic fields on the compression of H+. That liberation of ELF-UV light extends the size of the EZ in the water made by the matrix at cytochrome 4. He whiffs on that too.
3\. At the 10:50 he mentions the link of NADH and glucose and ATP........here is why Gilbert Ling was brilliant. He figured out pretty quick that with the kinetics of a cell it was physically impossible to make enough ATP for a cell to do what Peter Mitchell said and what this guy regurgitates back.........the energy is provided by light's interaction with water to split it......and make 4 more electrons as photosynthesis does when it splits water into H+ and oxgen. What happens in the matrix is the big black box of biology. Biology has guessed ATP and the physics does not support this.......
4\. He mentions the connection of cytochrome 2 to the TCA cycle but never realizes that this connection is not about the movement of electrons from NADH to CoEnzQ10......ALONE.....it also is critical in how the dehydrogenases in the cytochromes strip Hydrogen from the carbon backbone of foods to make sure they eliminate deuterium at certain carbons of the glucose backbone to make sure DDW is made at cytochrome 4 (Big miss) He mentions that complex two is not a proton pump.......but he never says why. It is because it is a PROTON selector to limit deuterium from ruining the ATPase channels for H+. This is how Krebs bicycle kisses the inner mitochondrial membrane to meet up with the urea cycle in the cytosol just across the intermembrane space where it resides.

Now you have a more full picture in which to learn from.

# Thread: Jen's Optimal - Recovering from Myalgic Encephalomyelitis (M.E/CFS), Depression & Anxiety...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jens-optimal-recovering-from-myalgic-encephalomyelitis-m-e-cfs-depression-anxiety.22281/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 28
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jenlou — 2018-10-11T12:53:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jens-optimal-recovering-from-myalgic-encephalomyelitis-m-e-cfs-depression-anxiety.22281/post-252879>

Hello,

I’m Jen. I’m 37 and from the UK.

I’ve been here for around a year now, on and off …more so recently, as my health has improved enough to allow me to learn more.

I’m hoping to update here fortnightly, on the progress I’m seeing, questions I’m pondering, and insights I’m gaining.…

I’d really like to start with a big heart-felt thank you to Jack and this community for all that it offers, I can see it is genuinely changing lives in very big ways. Seeing the comments/results that people are experiencing has been so inspiring to me. I find it difficult to write and articulate what I want to say as I have poor cognition problems… so for now, I’ll just say thank you to you all and I hope to be able to engage with everyone more as I make more progress with my health.

My health history is at the bottom of this post... as I’ve tried to summarise, but it’s still very long (I’ve just copied it from elsewhere and will try to edit/shorten as I improve)

Much love, Jen.

**NOW/ RECENT**

-   Slowly recovering from M.E (Myalgic Encephalomyelitis, also known as CFIDS Chronic Fatigue Immune Deficiency Syndrome).
-   Prior to M.E and during onset, I also suffered from severe depression and anxiety. Due to nutritional interventions, I am mostly recovered from depression and anxiety symptoms (occasional symptoms around menstruation each month).
-   I’ve progressed from 10 to 40 on this functionality score (with some short periods at around 60) over the past 4 years; [http://www.drmyhill.co.uk/wiki/CFS\_Ability\_Scale\_-\_a\_rough\_measure\_of\_how\_disabled\_you\_are](http://www.drmyhill.co.uk/wiki/CFS_Ability_Scale_-_a_rough_measure_of_how_disabled_you_are) I’m am mostly housebound and have to spend much of the day lying down.
-   Support from a nutritional therapist for the past 4 years, including a low oxalate Paleo diet, and supplementation.
-   We’ve worked on: supporting adrenal, reducing inflammation, reducing oxalates, supporting SNP’s / methylation/detoxification pathways, supporting mitochondria, correcting nutrient deficiencies such as Low Vit D, B12, folate, Vit C, supporting neurotransmitter function, parasite cleanse, biotoxin protocol, and I’m currently tackling Candida.
-   SNP’s I have... Heterozygous: MTHFR A1298C, COMt V158M, COMT H62H, MAO-A R297R, MTRR A66G, MTRR A664A, BHMT-08, AHCY-01, AHCY-19. Homozygous: VDR Bsm, CBS C699T.
-   I am two months into a ketogenic diet / Jack’s Epi Paleo RX advice. Attempted the same in 2017, but really struggled. Doing well on it now as my adrenal results and sleep are in a much better place, so I assume that has made the difference.
-   Past month I’ve felt very drowsy in the evenings as the evenings are now dark. Asleep by 9.30/10pm and waking naturally around 6.30am.
-   Felt strong enough to start a Candida / gut protocol in late July. This is currently playing out as repeated immune responses (sneezing, sore throat, thick head, stiff neck, diarrhoea, highly engorged veins (a sign of high inflammation for me), a thumping, a very strong heart beat when I stand up and after I eat, and breathlessness.
-   During better spells, I try to visit the sauna a couple of times a week.
-   I’m taking extra magnesium in the form of 2 x magnesium chloride flake baths per week.
-   I do some very gentle simple Yoga asana practice when I can
-   I’m incorporating both Vagus Nerve exercises from the book: ‘The Healing Power of the Vagus Nerve’ and some basic Somatic Experiencing movements daily. They seem to help reassure my nervous during times of Increased stress..

**Throughout 2018 I started incorporating the following;**

-   As much sunshine as possible (summer and autumn in UK has been very sunny) - my storage Vit D levels are now 104.3 nmol/L
-   I spent much of May-Sept in my garden, grounding on bare skin
-   Recently switched my fluoridated tap water to Evian bottled water
-   Blue blockers for TV
-   Hardwired our internet, devices and Apple TV
-   Phones are turned off at night. During the day we turn WiFi and data receivers off on the devices.
-   Device screens turned to Red for any evening use. My husband has FLUX on his work computer.
-   We don’t have a smart meter
-   I get an hour of morning sunlight between 9am-10am, after my daughter has gone to school
-   Re-positioned our bed, so on days when Im not well enough to get out of bed or outside, I open the east facing window wide and rest for 2 hours in the sunshine (when it's sunny!)
-   Started CT again (face dunking) which I couldn’t tolerate at all earlier this year (it completely wiped me out). I can now tolerate 45 seconds of ice water face dunking, which I’m doing daily.
-   We’ve turned the thermostat down for the winter and are leaving the windows ajar at nighttime.
-   Using candles only after dark, now that the nights are drawing in. My daughter, Maya has a red night light in her room for reading bedtime stories
-   Daily meditation practice which I stick to as it helps me so much, and we practice gratitude at bedtime with Maya to help us keep focused on what really matters / oxytocin.

**Current supplements:** magnesium citrate, SAMe, adrenal bovine concentrate, L-carnitine, B12, Coenzymated B1, Marine collagen, COD liver oil (for Vitamin A\], Vitamin K2, Vitamin D3, Vitamin C, Liposomal Glutathione, natural progesterone, pregnenolone, Vit d, Sodium butyrate, Lactoferrin, L-carnatine, liquorice root, activated charcoal, grapefruit seed extract, Pau de arco, molybdenum, goats milk kefir.

**Next stages / questions I have / research to do….**

-   Starting to think about tincorporating either homemade mackerel head broth, or raw fish head smoothies.(Thank you, Inger!) Supermarket fish counter is as fresh as I can get, as I live 2 hours from the nearest coast.
-   I’m adding extra iodine to the broth in the form of dried nori, seaweed and dulse – but the smallest amount seems to give me diarrhoea, so will monitor how that goes
-   Hire an EMF meter to check for dirty electricity / nnEMF levels in our home
-   save for, and research a reverse osmosis water filter system...how to get fluoride out of our shower/ bath water?
-   how to offset UK winter low sunlight effects? – would a infrared device be helpful, or too much EMF?
-   how can I remain grounded as possible during UK winter? Have considered a magnetico but for now it’s way beyond our reach financially with the postage costs.
-   consider a winter sun holiday … canary islands perhaps? or would a magnetico be a better use of the money?
-   worried about WiFi effects at school on Maya
-   is having a warm bath twice weekly, offsetting any benefits I will see from the CT (face dunking) I assume so?
-   consider some functional testing for Maya, as I am concerned that she is at higher risk of serious illness, given my history / mitochondiral issues.
-   read comments on blog posts
-   continue reading forum advice
-   read through Jack’s FB posts
-   consider paid membership if financial situation improves / supplement costs decrease.
-   look at concerns about deuterium levels in supplments and consider decreasing them / learn more about deuterium
-   It has been confirmed that my area of UK will be one of the first to trial 5G

### Jenlou — 2018-10-11T12:53:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jens-optimal-recovering-from-myalgic-encephalomyelitis-m-e-cfs-depression-anxiety.22281/post-252880>

**MY HISTORY / FAMILY HISTORY**

\- Maternal grandmother took her own life in 1958 when my mom was 4 – suspected Post Natal Depression.

· Mom had no family support in raising my brother and I. Dad was pretty hopeless. always very tired, stressed and abused alcohol a lot, which resulted in violence at home. I remember being home felt very stressful, and not much fun.

· Diet and environment pretty awful - I grew up in the 80’s in an urban area, with lots of sugary cereals, white bread, Vegetable oil spreads, processed foods, refined carbs.

· Mom worked cycles of night shifts, then day shifts, as a Nurse. We were always up late to collect her from shift, and up very early with Dad to take her in.

· Water supply in my area is fluoridated.

\- Glandular fever at 15

· I smoked (approx. 5 a day) between the ages of 15-32

· Hormonal imbalance started in early twenties, repeated visits to doctors with complaints of feeling low and tired, with engorged breasts and PMT at menstruation.

· Severe lack of self-esteem... I believe caused by home environment due to my dad’s behaviour (aggression, violence, negativity and alcoholism). Both parents had/have self esteem issues. I was also physically and emotionally bullied at school between the ages of 12-15.

· Early-mid twenties - constant low level anxiety, and the complete inability to know who I was, or what I wanted from life. Simple things like shopping became impossible ...completely overwhelmed with choices. Didn’t know ‘what was me’, or what wasn’t. I hid it well and was only really just then starting to become aware of how I actually felt, because this is just the norm. I detested my job in corporate business…I’m good at it, but it felt mind numbing and soul destroying, yet I felt worthless to do anything else. Started uni, but dropped out. Started other courses/ interests but dropped out, because I had no belief in myself. Self loathing started. Ended a happy long term relationship without ever really knowing why… other than the constant feeling of discontent and ‘needing’ to try other things, never settling, always wanting something else, a feeling of trying to escape my life.

· Hormone problems worsened, started feeling very angry and upset most of the time and acting out against people I loved, just like my dad used to. I tried birth control pill after birth control pill, in an attempt to help the symptoms. Doctors tell me it’s normal for women to have these symptoms. I’m diagnosed with fibrocystic breasts and given yet another hormone pill to try.

· Bloating in stomach after meals, awful wind and repeated thrush infections - treatment from acupuncturist for Candida overgrowth and allergy. Doctor dismissed this, tellingly me I’m wasting my money, “no such thing as Candida overgrowth”

· Lifestyle at weekends is heavy drinking and smoking and occasional class A drug use. I feel awful for it, but completely ignore my body. This is normal behaviour within my main social group, and other people’s opinions of what I do/ how I act far outweigh anything else.

· 28 - Quit job to go travelling around the world with my partner, Tom. Thought I’d feel better for doing so didn’t. I can’t enjoy anything, almost like I’m not living in my body and having these experiences that should feel incredible. Whilst away, sought counselling for depression which didn’t help, other than it felt good to have someone to talk to.

· Received 7 travel vaccinations for the trip, which seemed to cause repeated headaches and body/joint pain. Symptoms dismissed my doctor.

· Whilst travelling took 3 months worth of anti-malarial tablets (which I later discover are anti-biotics ‘Doxycycline’). I now have constant vaginal thrush, fungal toe nail infections, dreadful anxiety and depression, constantly bloated stomach and a increasing dreadful hangovers from the smallest amounts of alcohol. Often, I wake feeling ‘hungover’ having drunk no alcohol for weeks.

· Diagnosed with a Candida overgrowth and an allergy to Candida by two separate alternatives health practitioners whilst abroad. Again, doctors dismiss this and tell me there is nothing wrong, “perhaps IBS”…for which they have no advice for. “Is it nutritional, or can it be helped with nutritional interventions?” I ask. Definitely not they say.

· Only at this point, I started to reject what my doctors were saying and look at what might be going wrong in my body myself. I start a regular yoga practice and start to redevelop a long- suppressed interest in nature, biology and spirituality… for the first time, I begin true self- inquiry and reflection.

· Hormonal issues are almost now intolerable. Severe PMS / mood swings , sore breast for 10 days each month. Unable to exercise post-ovulation as breasts are too swollen and engorged. I spend 2 weeks of every month dosed up on pain killers and the only thing I find that brings a little relief is a homeopathic remedy.

· We arrive home (UK) in May 2012, and I fall pregnant in September 2012 - hormonal and depression symptoms dissipate during pregnancy apart from the very sore breasts which remain for the duration. I have ‘morning’ sickness throughout and feel exhausted. I’m in bed for 6pm most night…so tired I often have pull the car over on the way home. My iron is low and the tablets from the doctors don’t seem to bring levels up enough. During pregnancy I practice yoga, meditation, Hypnobirthing and for the first time, I start to get more of an idea of what knowing myself and listening to my body might look like.

· A good and completely natural labour, with the exception of gas and air.

· My daughter Maya being born connects me much deeper to my own spirituality. I’m in awe of the biology, the wonder of it all and the utterly overwhelming experience of this amount of love is unfathomable.

· I put the exhaustion I feel down to bring a new mom. I struggle deeply with breastfeeding - my body just doesn’t seem to have the resources to make much milk at all, which is a huge disappointment. I blame and shame myself and push myself to do more...staying up until all hours trying to pump more milk. I’m not coping physically, one lift of the baby car seat, or trying to hold Maya for breastfeeding is exhausting and brings me to tears.

· My right elbow and knees ache deeply, I start having migraines, my blood pressure keeps plummeting, and I’m increasing depressed and anxious again. I attend anxiety counselling, but it doesn’t make sense to me... I know intuitively that there’s something wrong inside, this isn’t just a case of changing my thought patterns or behaviour.

· A rheumatologist tells me that my elbow and knee pains, are likely from picking my daughter up a lot, or my pregnancy hormones settling.

· An endocrinologist suggests this is chronic fatigue syndrome but is very vague about the whole thing. A short synacthen test we do causes me to sleep for 16 hours straight.

· 9 months post-partum - I’m having day-long severe anxiety and huge panic attacks. I’m having thoughts that don’t feel like my own and they are spiralling. I’m in a constant state of dread and having feelings of terror. I don’t feel like I’m in my own body, like nothing is real. I have two very strange out of body experiences whilst out with Maya - I feel like I’m above her and the oncoming traffic is too close...my rational thoughts are like little blips in a sea of panic, upset and confusion. I feel like there is part of me that will hurt her in some way.

· Twice, and once on an occasion when I’m alone in the house with Maya, I pass out.

· I’m starting to really struggle cognitively; simple admin and maths are tricky. My spelling, handwriting and punctuation is poor, I have to re-read sentences countless times and still don’t retain the information. I loose so many bank cards within the space of 7 months, that my bank tells me they need to run further fraud checks on me.

· My nervous system is frazzled, I start getting electric shock type sensations at loud noises or when I sense the slightest danger, like a car driving too close. I’m jumpy at everything, I can’t watch the news or anything scary or slightly distressingly as I get a hugely overemotional response, my spatial awareness is warped – Im constantly walking into door frames and curbing my car. I leave it unlocked, forget to lock the house when I leave. I worry constantly that I’ll do something to harm Maya.

· I try different types of anti-depressants, but I can’t tolerate half of the smallest dose. I can’t lift my head off the pillow, my neck feels engorged and so painful, my lymph nodes are swollen, my brain feels like it’s misfiring and the pain in body increases. I am told, that because I feel suicidal with a small child, that if I don’t persist with the anti depressant dose, Social Services will have to be notified. I don’t return back to my doctors, or the NHS, what’s the point.

· Around this time, I’m starting to have massive energy crashes. The slightest activity seems to bring this on ...1/2 hour of housework, a walk to the shops and back. I start some homeopathic remedies which help a little with the depression the anxiety, but I feel so poorly all of the time now… constantly hungover, always feeling like I’m stuck somewhere between having a virus and trying to run a marathon without any training. I’m constantly dehydrated, the skin on my hands looks wrinkly. I lose three toenails to fungal infections. Putting Maya to bed is impossible, I try to read her a bedtime story, but my arms hurt so much from the fatigue and pain, I can’t hold the book up or my arm up to give her a bottle. I go to drive home one day and cannot remember the way.

· I feel suicidal and I cannot go on this way. I’m caught between sleeping 20 hours a day and long periods of complete insomnia... Maya has to stay with Grandparents 3 nights each week, as we can’t cope. Tom has to resign his job, and go self-employed so he can be home more to take on the housework, care for me, cooking etc. With the help of my mom and Tom, we research everything we can about my symptoms. I try to learn as much as I can about fatigue, pain, inflammation, the gut... I quit smoking and alcohol completely.

· My doctor agrees that I have M.E, although simultaneously states that it’s not a real condition! I take his referral to the local CFS clinic, that my husband has found. By the time our appointments come through 5 months later, I’m too poorly to leave the house for them.

· All of my existing symptoms are huge now. I also feel nauseous and have diarrhoea much of the time, my muscles twitch and cramp constantly, hormonal symptoms now stretch to 16 days each month, and the cognitive impairment means that often, one sentence in a book is unachievable. I recall not being able to remember my middle name or the year I was born when asked when trying to register at a new GP’s. I give up driving. Noise is becoming a real problem and causes the other symptoms to worsen. When I can get out of bed, I burn pots and pans regularly…I see them burning on the stove, but it’s like my brain can’t compute and tell me what I need to do to turn the stove off.

· We find a nutritional therapist online that specialises in fatigue issues. I am naïve…. I assume that now I’ve found someone who might be able to help, I’ll be better soon. Give it a few months and I’ll be back...I’ll be a ‘normal’ mom, and wife.

· Some tests reveal that I have systemic inflammation to my brain and central nervous system, my immune system is struggling, I have problems with methylation, adrenal/HPA axis issues, a very leaky gut, intolerances to numerous foods and sulphur, I am hypothyroid, my progesterone, pregnenolone, serotonin, vitamin c, vitamin d levels and b vitamin levels are next to none existent.

· The crashes after any exertion now have a paralysis to them and can last days or weeks. Each day, I stir from a crashed half sleep /half dream like state and my body doesn’t move. I have no idea what day it is or what’s going on. I come around slightly. Have I remembered to sort birthday gifts out this month, or have they passed? There is brief moment of panic...almost like I can’t believe I’ve survived another crash and I’m still here. Each one is terrifying and surreal. “My god, where is Maya?” ..and then then relief with the realisation that she’s safe with her grandparents, combines with the despair at her not being here again… and then it happens again, and again, and again.

· Over the next four years, I waver between completely bed bound, house bound, being able to pop out for an hour or two, and very occasionally, a slightly brighter spells where I can manage, or rather push myself through a day out or a weekend away during a ‘good’ period.

· We are very lucky to have practical and financial support from both sets of parents, and ironically (aside from Tom), it is my Dad , who having worked through some of his own issues, is my greatest source of emotional support.

The last two things you posted are most important.

If this is true.....you got to move.

And you might need DDW if you continue to live this far from the equator.......closer to it will improve your redox fastest to reverse what ails you.

5G will discharge the voltages you've gained.

I believe a purpose is something for which one is responsible for cultivating and bringing forward to the rest of the world to see. Why do you do what you do? I believe we’re the creators of our own experience – comprehending this every day and remembering this in every experience we have while living our lives from this perspective empowers us to do great things that others think is impossible. Do people make fun of you and call you crazy when you think that what you're building might change the world in some way? My advice is not to worry about what any 'obedient idiot' thinks about you or your work on social media. Do what you are passionate about doing. When people get in your way eliminate the unnecessary. Removing them will improve the rapidity of change you seek in the world. What you are doing might not make sense to the rest of the world until they see the wisdom in why you are doing it. I

Black Swans who have a very distinct why they do and live as they do, become able to bear almost any how the world throws at them. This is the way of the mitochondriac. The failures I have learned about my profession since I left training have taught me the greatest lesson of my life to date. You can’t just sit there and wait for people to give you that golden dream or promise you thought you were getting in life or your job. The reality is you must put your best ideas to work with actions that can really make a difference to the people who you are trying to help in ways they might not understand on the surface. You do this by putting yourself out there and make it happen for yourself and them.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-15T20:37:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jens-optimal-recovering-from-myalgic-encephalomyelitis-m-e-cfs-depression-anxiety.22281/post-253322>

Why we should avoid sunflower oil: It is an oil made by the sun but processed badly by man.
The hydrogen source of ANY lab created food does not control for its elimination as a chloroplast does.

We have a very detailed mechanism in our bodies that keeps deuterium at a certain level tied to the light in the environment and our location on Earth that works to do things cells need to program amino acids. Our blood is one concentration and our matrix has another more stringent requirement. When you keto snacks you stress the latter and if the latter is already fucked you've made things that much worse. See Jimmy Moore and his keto snacks as an example. Sunflower oil is another.

This is why fruits contain all the deuterium from a plant whereas the green stuff is much better off. If it does not grow around you-you have no business eating it at your latitude. For those who live at a high latitude, we can handle the fruit for a short period of the year if we're in the strong sun but otherwise we're toast because of the mechanism mentioned above.

**Update 26/10
\- lymph system struggling somewhat.. hugely painful and swollen lymph glands in neck and groom for the past two weeks.. I think since the introduction of lactoferrin.. so assume this is a biotoxin issue.
\- am going to trial slow induction of rosemary and garlic to try to assist biotoxin issue
\- sensitivity to noise has increased slightly, can’t manage sitting with Maya and Tom at breakfast..it’s too much.
\- try gentle rebounding on better days, and self massage to help with lymph movement
\- re-read Epcot RX
\- research SAPS and uncoupling and what that means for me
\- have booked a week’s winter sun holiday to canaries in early December
\- Tom has done research on a whole house RO filter..hopefully fitting it in December
\- Diet going ok, hunger between meals seems to have reduced with increase of C8 oil
\- listen to jacks podcats from ovulation- menstruation, when I’m at my worst congnitively and physically, I can’t manage reading the blogs
\- sleeping deeply from 9pm- 6.30am, dreaming every night, still feel absolutely wiped out after waking
\- another episode of very dry skin and eyes
\- tolerating face CT much better, and cold for remaining 5mins of shower feels really good
\- iodine is no longer causing diarroeoh
\- I’ve lost a little weight.. no idea how much, but my lothes are more loose
\- the more I learn, the more there is to learn..! As always, continuously working on finding that balance between learning / seeking answers and surrender / true rest.

**

Just a thought.
Dr Boros (who knows his stuff) and is sick.
He eats lots of fat, because (his explanation):
hydrogen molecule from fat combined with oxygen from the air
makes (de novo) water within matrix of his mitochondria.
Other that all other goods that it does
the process of water creation provides (additional) high energy
that energy is a heat that heals what needs healing.
That energy is about the same as the heat part of sunlight.

So, to benefit from that process one must eat lots of fat.
The question may become what kind of fat?
I noted that dr Boros eats pork fat and drinks olive oil.
He did not mention other fats.
Pork fat and olive oil have lots in common.
Both have very high percentage of oleic acids.

Another observation.
People with neolithic diseases have (as a rule) messed up fatty acids.
Yours is just one of many of those diseases.
Some fatty acids are very high, some very low,
when compared to expectations.
(It is complicated, I do not know most of what is involved),
those who try to help usually concentrate on lowering very long fatty acids that are excessive (and blame defective peroxisomes for not cutting what needs cutting, while doing it).
How about employing idea of building up what is low.
(Like I guess is what dr Boros is doing).

But not everybody who is sick is low on oleic acids.
There are other fatty acids in foods we eat.
How about checking finding out what is missing
and load up on those fats.

This is basic
Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum
there are more involved tests too.

All fats have a hydrogen atoms available for water production,
making new water from fat would always result in healing energy
but you would additionally help in ordering fatty acids in your body.

A thought.
When we drink, we are "preventing our body" from making new water.
Thirsty person is able to make up to 10liters of water every hour using his body fat or the fat he eats.
So do not worry about hydration.
Worry more how to stay thirsty next to big bottle of nice cold water.

Likely, if you are missing on oleic acid or not
you should get helped by
reducing your drinking water (but to no less than 1/2L/day)
and eating or drinking fat (no big need to do that if you are currently fat)
You would just be little better of when using type of fat more aligned to your needs.

Note;
All above is on top of
major Jacks teachings
get more sun (you being naked no shoes or glasses)
better when closer to equator.

### David Tedman-Jones — 2018-11-03T05:18:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jens-optimal-recovering-from-myalgic-encephalomyelitis-m-e-cfs-depression-anxiety.22281/post-254938>

Hi Jenlou,

I'm new to all this, I haven't even posted anything yet, however I came across your thread and your journey just struck me as if I was reading about myself. Upon reading through it all it would seem that you have more severe symptoms, however, I can relate to much of what you have been experiencing.

In 2014 I had a health crisis where I essentially ceased to function. No sleep - zero - for days and days, constant hypnic jerks (electric shock type sensation) every few seconds throughout the day and when trying to sleep were even worse. Adrenaline surging throughout the day, impending sense of doom, gut churning, hunger constant, even immediately after eating. I also felt like my nervous system was misfiring and my gut was constantly on fire. Anxiety was spilling out into everything, alternating between anxiety and depression. I had a chronic sinus infection for over 3months+ during this time and had multiple courses of antibiotics. I had the typical adrenal fatigue symptoms accompanied by almost zero aldosterone and testosterone. I urinated constantly. Tried a tiny dose of an SSRI for one day and had my first suicidal thought which overcame my body with icy cold indescribable fear. I stopped working (which was good because I worked till late under bright lights), I stayed in bed or at home all day, crying and being scared of sleep. Couldn't sleep for days and days. It was hell on earth. Interestingly I also had glandular fever around 2010, which hit me hard and I'm not sure if I have ever quite recovered.

It was September-ish ish 2014 when this was happening (coming into the summer luckily - I'm in Brisbane which is also relatively lucky as it is quite sunny), and I came across Kruse's epi paleo rx, leptin prescription and adrenal fatigue rx. I followed it closely (although I should have concentrated more on light and nnemf in hindsight), did CT almost everyday, seaweed, sardines and canned oysters every day, no carbs, sun, blocked blue light etc. I still do all of this to this day with the exception of CT and now I don't mind to add carbs. Still drink flouridated water, however I plan on changing this.

Long story short I came good - better than ever in fact. Initial progress came quickly, but it took months and months (probably a year) to make a 80%+ recovery. Today, I haven't had a single gut issue, sleep issue, nervous system misfire or twitch, or anxiety or depression episode in probably 1.5 years now. Actually interestingly, I had a couple twitches writing this - almost as if I'm rebooting those old pathways.

Recovering from the fear was the best part of the journey. I used to go for walks in my neighbourhood and everything triggered a fear response in me, and then imprinted on my mind. Everything in my life ended up being literally infected with fear. But this has all gone! I also began to trust that the body doesn't do things by accident. A bad night of sleep or a bad day usually meant I had done something wrong, but over time I became more resiliant, and my health was less sensitive to small lifestyle fluctuations. I began to trust that my body would do the healing and I just had to give it the time and the right conditions. It is truly amazing what we can recover from.

I just wanted to wish you well on your journey to recovery, and even though I don't exactly know your situation, I can empathise with a lot of what you have been going through - and I feel confident that you can make a full recovery.

Hang in there!

Thank you for the info r.e fatty acids.. I haven’t tested as funds are so low, but I’m on a very clean Keto diet and measuring as being in permanent ketosis so that’s a start and good progress I feel, as I’ve really struggled with Keto in the past.

Serendipitous that you wrote to me about not needing additional hydration when we are using fat as our main dietary fuel, as additional to Jack’s info, I caught a YouTube video this week with Dr Que Collins and Dr Lazlo Borus talking about the very same!

Thank you so much David for sharing your story and for taking the time to get in touch. Sorry it has taken me so long to reply.

So pleased to read that you’ve made such wonderful progress and reclaimed your health.. recovery stories always give me such hope that I can get there too.

Your journey sounds so familiar in terms of dealing with fear, trust and self belief... this year has been huge for me in terms of finding refuge in being able to let go and in giving up the internal battle against the symptoms and what impact this has had on our lives. I have a different perspective now and, (for the most part, at least!) I’m able to see this whole experience as just part of the adventure of life. I have learnt so much from all this, and much more to come which is exciting. I’m able to appreciate the ‘good in the bad’ and to drop the resistance to whatever life throws up, much more easily

Thanks again for your well wishes. Sending the same to you.

I will hang in there!

### Jenlou — 2018-12-06T15:00:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jens-optimal-recovering-from-myalgic-encephalomyelitis-m-e-cfs-depression-anxiety.22281/post-257375>

Update / notes / etc..

\- Introduced Red Light Therapy 660/850 wavelength 3 weeks ago.
Building up slowly as initially caused huge increase in tinnitus and left me wired at night. Tolerating two sessions of 5 mins well
now, at mid morning and early afternoon.
[https://redlightrising.co.uk/product/the-target-light](https://redlightrising.co.uk/product/the-target-light)

\- Keto going well. Hunger between meals and ‘desperation hunger’ in the evenings has completely gone.. so has the feeling of being permanently stressed (stressed feeling only occurred after ovulation and until menstruation last month)

\- noise tolerance still a big issue, particularly first thing and later in the day

\- sleeping really well.. still knocked out upon waking with usual paralysis type feeling in body

\- not tolerating very cold water well for face dunks.. brain freeze and if I submerge my hands..lots of instant pain.. need to read more on CT in general, or prehaps ease off a little for now.

\- Pain and swelling in lymph nodes has settled.

\- Tolerating rosemary and garlic well..

\- Considering swopping Grapefruit Seed Extract for Oil of Oregano (for biofilms)

\- Hormones were much worse last month..big increase in cramping pain and bloating from day 10 of cycle, through to day 3 of the next one.. severe back pain and other constant random stabbing type pains, mainly in extremities, legs and neck, which died down as my period ended. Feeling extremely stressed during this phase. I am back to wearing maternity clothing.. the Christmas party gear will be gathering dust for another year it seems!
\- considering a DUTCH Test to look into hormone situation more closely.

\- Deuterium. Deuterium. DEUTERIUM! ...constantly calling my attention and is where im headed

\- In the process of cutting out around half of my supplements..

omitting slowly: magnesium citrate, l carnitine, bovine adrenal extract, b12, b1, licorice, SAMe, molybdenum, potassium, multi vitamin, pau de arco

Keep for now?: Lactoferrin, vitamin C, vit D, vit K2, pregnenolone, grapefruit seed extract or oil of oregano, charcoal (when experiencing detox symptoms), progesterone drops,

\- thinking to hold fire on RO system and purchase some DDW instead.. have looked at Preventa but confused on which PPM to get. do I go as low PPM as I can afford? Have assumed so. Consider Qlariva too.

\- look at SNPs and SAPs

\- currently feeling more sleepy tired during the daytimes.. almost a little ‘drugged’ like I’ve taken a sleeping tablet.. worsens through the day and I’m needing bed by 7pm.

Hey matt, I'm West Mids .. so about as land-locked in the UK as you can get. The only fishmogers available locally are within supermarkets - my dear mom has tried numerous ones, both locally to her and to me, but with no joy as yet. We don't have a indipendant fish-monger locally unfortunately.

thanks for the reply

### Jenlou — 2019-06-12T13:13:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jens-optimal-recovering-from-myalgic-encephalomyelitis-m-e-cfs-depression-anxiety.22281/post-272496>

Update:

**Deuterium**

So I bit the bullet and got my Deuterium levels tested at the Centre for Deuterium Depletion
\- intial test levels (early March 2019):
Breath 150.3PPM
Saliva: 153.1PPM
Depletion factor: 1.8% (ideal is 7.5% and above)
ATP production factor: 10.8 (ideal range is 50-70)

..so pretty grim (as expected! ). Dr Q advised I go for more blood work at my GP's to look for cancer markers. This has been ruled out.

Advised to drink 25PPM DDW + deuterium depleting breathing mask and high fat diet, which i've been doing for 2.5 months now
due to retake the Deuterium level test in early July.

**Ketosis**

Caught a virus early January which took me 6 weeks to get back to my baseline health levels and threw me out of ketosis - have found it impossible to get back in - attempted until end of April but was concerned with the amount of weight gain (2 stone in 4 months), so am now on a LCHF diet and the addition of some carbohydrates seems to be suiting me better. I do think the pyschological stress of our situation / dealing with these symptoms and trying to implement and maintain a keto diet whilst remaining bedbound didn't help - too much adrenaline / cortisol maybe.

**Progress / Changes**

\- Have moved from bed-bound to mostly housebound again. I feel like the DDW is giving me an edge.
\- I dropped a load of supplements but did add in ActivAMP - which I also feel is helping
\- recovering much quicker from crashes (post exertional malaise)
\- continuing with Red Light Therapy twice daily and as much sunlight, esp early morning as possible
\- Somatic movement / feldenkraise / gentle yoga daily - are helping my stress levels considerably
\- noise tolerance is a little improved
\- 'brain' tinnitus is less extreme
\- some episodes of extremly high inflammation / high histamine (slump phases) which affect my sleep (wired at night) but these do not last a long (2-6 days now, rather than permanently being in those states with hardly little relief)
\- still feel very knocked out in the mornings
\- aiming for 2 saunas a week and am tolerating 20 mins well now, with a cold shower afterwards
\- tolerating cold water in general better - I eased off and built up slowly with the face-dunks and shower temperatures
. Hormones still a nightmare - cramping and back pain from around day 10 through to my period at day 29 - although for the past two cycles, I've felt much less 'stressed' during this phase. Still have episodes of overwhelming depression / low motivation and feelings that I cannot take it anynire - but they are definately reduced somewhat.
\- cognitive symptoms still poor - confusion / forgetfulness / memory
\- still need to look at SNPs and SAPs but struggling too much cognitavly to make any sense of what I do come across
\- increase in other brain symptoms - vision is often fuzzy / wobbly and i'm having strange tingling / tickly sensations shoot through my brain
\- unable to tolerate hardly any screen time ..double-edged sword really .. body can't cope with it, mind wants to as I signed up to Jack's patreon, but am unable to read it
\- big increase in feeling 'hungover' ovee the past 3-4 weeks
\- huge amounts of gas and wind in the afternoons / evenings for the past 5 weeks - am considering some digestive enzymes

### David Schreiner — 2018-10-11T18:41:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d2o-heavy-water.22282/post-252909>

howdy,
Has anyone had any experience with the John Ellis E5 distiller?
It’s supppsed to deplete Deuterium and therefore change the bonding angle of Hydrogen to Oxygen.

According to HYD LLC, the Hungarian company that exports Preventa to the US, when you distill water, you remove 1 ppm of Deuterium. When you double-distill water, you remove an additional 2 or 3 ppm.

Laboratories sell Double Distilled Water for various scientific testing purposes. If that water starts at 150 ppm, then it would be 146 or 147 ppm once processed.

# Thread: Diagnosing RF increase - 5G or something else?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diagnosing-rf-increase-5g-or-something-else.22284/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx

### Andrew H — 2018-10-11T22:49:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diagnosing-rf-increase-5g-or-something-else.22284/post-252925>

Recently I have been feeling horrible EMF symptoms and general inflammation. Lo and behold, RF levels in my apartment are now 0.05-0.2 V/m. Just several weeks ago they peaked at 0.05 V/m. Measurements were taken with a Cornet ED88T Plus meter. I am trying to figure out the source.

So I am in Houston and 5G rollout from Verizon began on Oct 1. But according to Verizon's [site](https://www.verizonwireless.com/5g/home/), my address does not yet have 5G service available. The rollout was only in certain neighborhoods, though Verizon is not explicitly disclosing which ones.

Given the timing, I suspect 5G. But the fact that service isn't available in my neighborhood, plus the fact that my bedroom Faraday cage has held up well (I'm very sensitive to EMFs during sleep but still sleep great) makes me wonder if there's another source of general RF causing the radiation increase, rather than 5G specifically.

Maybe a new cell tower, though according to Antenna Search the nearest ones are just under 1 km away and I do not see any new ones pop up super close to me compared to a few weeks ago. Plus there is a construction zone across the street, with lots of equipment like cranes, etc. The crane is one of those super tall ones made for building high-rise buildings in cities.

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-10-12T02:55:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diagnosing-rf-increase-5g-or-something-else.22284/post-252946>

Ditto here.

I've noticed a significant uptick in measured microwatts/sq meter where I live, even though 5G hasn't officially launched here.

Downtown area where I used to work was measured yesterday at well over 20,000 microwatts/sq meter and off the meter scale at over 6 V/m. Unbelievable.

My RF meter (Acoustimeter), although a very fine piece of equipment for low end of RF meters, doesn't have a direction finder capability to detect signal strength changes with direction accurately (some of more expensive consumer products have this valuable capability).

**I strongly suspect there is some stealth testing/trials of advanced 4G or incipient 5G networks going on, with higher frequencies and more bandwidth.**

Remember, not all telecom antennas are placed on conventional cell towers (masts). Some are mounted as panels on taller buildings (sometimes disguised, usually not). They can be masquerading as faux rocks or trees (supposedly for 'aesthetic' reasons in some communities). They are known to be hidden inside church steeples too (steeple and faux objects, e.g. fake vegetation are constructed with plastic type material transparent to RF signals).

### Andrew H — 2018-10-12T04:47:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diagnosing-rf-increase-5g-or-something-else.22284/post-252949>

Aha, now there is a possibility!

The question is - how do I shield it? If 4G, can use standard methods. If 5G, well... the one company that's gotten back to me about 5G shielding basically said oh we'll just have to wait and see. But I'm not the type to wait and see, while I'm in pain at the moment.

I'm contemplating putting a naturelle Faraday cage over my desk with a shielding ground mat underneath, since that seems to work well for my bed.

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-10-12T18:26:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diagnosing-rf-increase-5g-or-something-else.22284/post-252976>

I don't know if 5G mm wave forms will penetrate shielding cloth in common use now (you know, the silver impregnated cloth fabric people are using as canopies for their bed as a sleep sanctuary, etc.). I strongly suspect that small waves can easily slip through these mesh fabric apertures.

Anyway, the true 5G mm wave beams are coming later, according to my information. It seems that 5G networks that are launching all the time now are souped up 4G RF with higher frequencies and more power density. That is my take, anyway.

In any case, 5G RF, like 4G, will still be attenuated (greatly reduced) for the most part by solid metallic shielding and dense material like concrete.

I am extremely concerned about people building Faraday cage type enclosures for protection. Unless you're sure no RF is leaking in through any cracks or seams you run the risk of creating a mini microwave oven. RF beams that slip in could ricochet around the enclosure until they are absorbed by your body. Please be careful.

Another factor to keep in mind is that in a _5G_ world we have another worry about mw RF shielding-- **jump conduction**.

Dr Kruse has mentioned this 5G phenomenon numerous times. From way he discusses it I assume jump conduction is when an electrical current from 5G wireless radiation is capable of flowing through air to a conductive material or a ground--- depending on circumstances it may electric shock.

That is why he recommends against clothing designed for EMF shielding that might facilitate jump conduction in a 5G environment. I would assume the same applies to Faraday cage solutions.

He has hinted of some serious design work he is engaged in to develop shelter solutions in 5G infested environment.

He also recommends selling your current house soon and building one of these safe houses when they are commercially available.

As a personal side note, perhaps you should sell your house relatively soon. Howdy, all. Citizen Scientist here. (Not my real name.) Located in FEMA Region IV, the heartland of American Deuterium. Nor is it the ideal altitude or latitude.

If we ever meet in person in a Mexico or a Vermont, you'll find I'm the nicest person. But here, on this forum, you'll find me focused on the grim pursuit of eternal life.

We're all prematurely-aged these days, and I am starting from square one. No SNPs for me. (Do you know _who_ started 23andMe? Do you know _why_ they started 23andMe? The more you know.) Which goes double for Facebook.

This group of people on this forum is unique. You [starfish](https://www.urbandictionary.com/define.php?term=starfish) are determined to survive, to thrive, to optimize. And we're here to help each other. I've seen the opposite in the world of commercial health, where those people won't help but will charge. But while I'm here, I'm here to help you.

Science should be in the hands of the citizens. No individual, who just wants to live, should be held hostage by supplement makers, charlatans, lunatics, podcasters, doctors, and device-makers who, somehow, need a ten-thousand-percent mark-up. If you can do-it-yourself, do. Knowledge is power. (Didn't Jack once say, _"Ignorance is a burden to others, and a prison for oneself."_)

So I'm here to learn, challenge myself, and optimise.

Most interested in the discussion around deuterium depletion for water right now.

But wanted to introduce myself first.

# Thread: KREBS BICYCLE.........What is it?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krebs-bicycle-what-is-it.22292/>

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-13T15:28:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krebs-bicycle-what-is-it.22292/post-253027>

What is Kreb's bicycle?

It is where the TCA and urea cycle meet in your mitochondria. "Krebs bicycle," composed of the urea cycle below, which meshes with the aspartate-argininosuccinate shunt of the citric acid cycle on the left. They meet at the enzyme fumerase which adds water to both cycles. The water it adds is also quite special because the water added comes from the mitochondrial matrix and not from the extracellular fluid compartment. Fumarate produced in the cytosol by argininosuccinate lyase of the urea cycle can enter the citric acid cycle in the matrix of the mitochondrion and is converted in several steps to oxaloacetate. This is how these two cycles operate in unison.

When the two wheels of this bicycle do not rotate freely we get collateral effects in other cycles. One of those areas of collateral damage is in the methionine cycle which controls the flux of homocysteine and the sulfur laced amino acids that make up the major anions of our blood that interact with sunlight. One oxaloacetate accepts an amino group from glutamate by transamination, and the aspartate thus formed leaves the mitochondrion and donates its amino group (nitrogen cycle) to the urea cycle in the argininosuccinate synthetase reaction. Intermediates in the citric acid cycle are boxed in the picture below. When this process is broken reactive nitrogen signal is awry in a cell and it leads to more mitochondrial damage. What breaks it? Anything that is capable of slowing the spin rate of the cycle or its substrates. Deuterium's kinetic isotope effect is the number one offender in illness states. [https://www.patreon.com/posts/18925873](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.patreon.com%2Fposts%2F18925873&h=AT0peAt-xEdGdPwf9kuEcY_-ywCGt6oLnlGxRXF_suBBHAJciUOFkAvM2YxLriMcMzOl-dgSQOfbNQjrjxkvmKGJuAy85CGmVfZeGDbADKJMN9Q44myr7WxDe3l1dnnZeChHd2ra95NGbUIt9Aw5evSexQMLutIKdVc6wphYTE3ELRL2u53Em2os_m2nfao9CygXM2EVasFPV5EqamkAouj9Uxtpn29QwJbZIHAY9ODQM1Tv3IH_bDjJOEiGUstMAe1i0Jcz3t9U5XUJwkRxUG1pneuQKGna_Tkdy44W2jd70ph2MhH_dYpuugAHx2P2pJRqfVrzNAqyHpndUCEG-2om4_O-vZZWWxx0jxf-pAoFyLuHa1WGTku44RWEXnfaXpM_gYIWRSXx8gyuxECycpmYim8nIPaME78)

Melanin is also adjacent to most mitochondria in most organs, which suggests its function with mtDNA UPEs and its own are powerful for some reason. Melanin is also paramagnetic and this makes it sensitive to the magnetic fields created by the spinning Fo head of the ATPase.

Melanin paramagnetism varies with its hydration status by the heme proteins such as CCO. Hydration Status and Paramagnetism: Melanin’s paramagnetism is hydration-dependent.

Water sorption shifts the comproportionation equilibrium (hydroxyquinone ↔ semiquinone ↔ DDW, with lower deuterium, alters this equilibrium differently than normal water. Deuterium affects hydrogen bonding and proton mobility, so DDW enhances proton release in melanin’s redox reactions, increasing semiquinone radical concentration (the primary paramagnetic species).

Studies on hydrated melanin show interfacial water layers (3–5 layers) dominate its structure, with possible proton cation (e.g., Zundel ion) signatures. **If they did we'd have more answers for AD and ALS. This is why the 20 yr failed amyloid paradigm has set us back in AD science. What are the implications of all these biophysics for demyelinating diseases, like MS, ALS, BPD, AD, and PD? Inquiring minds will find this blog fascinating.**

Greg

### jeff taylor — 2018-10-14T23:01:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/supplementing-light.22308/post-253176>

So I am starting on my own personal N=1 journey. I am realatively healthy so I am looking for optimization. My diet is good, high in seafood, low in garbage. I drink at least 2 liters of high quality water per day. I have a magnetic bed cover on order. Been bathing in sun this summer, but as we enter fall and winter a question arises that has likley been gone over many times here. what is the best way to supplement light. Here is what my plan is and I am interested in feedback and thoughts

1) I am considering getting a led red light panel, body sized (probably joovv) and using 10 min/day
2) In looking at Jack's work, it seems to me that this needs to be balanced with the right amount of UVA and UVB to optimize. My thinking is that we can use the sun to estimate how much one should be getting based on the amount of red light. (my understanding is that Red light is 41% of the equation, not sure about UV) Very interested on peoples thoughts on this, as I suspect getting this ratio correct is the key to not making one sick.
3) I plan to get to Hawaii 2x this winter to get as much natural sunlight as possible.

I think there's a lot more to it and that its significant that under drinking isn't part of the leptin reset. If I look at the extremes of the animal kingdom such as the camel. The camel can go days with making enough water from it's hump fat, but its kidneys and gut etc are adapted to recycle that water far more than humans. It will also drink a massive amount of (deuterium loaded) water when it next gets a chance. The other thing is that the camel is _able_ to burn fat to make metabolic water .. One of the problems of obesity is the level of efficiency someone has to burn stored fat. Less efficiency = less metabolic water production. I think it could potentially screw up my circadian rhythm of ADH. My n=1 "sufficient" water varies seasonally and with what I'm doing, my environment etc. I think in the wrong context under drinking could potentially lead to thermoregulation and sweating problems, constipation, brain fog etc. It could also impact sleep. I've still got weight to lose, progress is slow, but under drinking is not something I'd attempt without understanding the wider issues in relation to my n=1.

4\. Repeat process by putting ES-problem area of the body next to wall for a few minutes wearing a pendant or crystal

5\. Say ouch a lot again

6\. Back away from wall so levels are back down under 1 milliGauss

7\. Say ahhhhhhhh

### axel brandt — 2018-10-16T04:38:40+0300

I'm 45 and have some trouble realizing that I cannot do some type of efforts anymore. One year ago, I had to quit running and playing tennis for 6 months due to bilateral hip labral tears. I went to see an orthopedic surgeon who offered me an arthroscopic surgery, but decided to try spontaneous recovery. By that time I started to read this blog and with the help of the epi-paleo diet, CT, and eating gelatin on a daily basis, I resolved my hip pain. Returned to play tennis, and started to run, without problems. Unfortunately, I started two months ago with a terrible back- pain after digging a big hole in my backyard, but didnt quit doing sports. Last two weeks I had to stop, and barely walk since then. I suspect to have an herniated disc, and will have an MRI done this week. I started to take a lot of sun on my back, with ice, 24 hs fasting, gelatin, and also dailyTENS (transcutaneous electrical nerve stimulation) with high Frequency and low intensity. It's incredible the ammount of new things that I learned here, beside being an extraordinary good scientific divulgator, Jack is a good teacher. Any other suggestions to treat my lumbosciatic pain are welcomed !

### James Dang — 2018-10-16T20:17:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/puting-the-blog-knowledge-to-work.22313/post-253415>

> [axel brandt said:](/goto/post?id=253382)
>
> Living in buenos aires outskirts, semi-rural area, 34° south latitude, my winter (july) vit D level is 27, summer 42.HDL 68, LDL 182, total cholesterol 262, TG 62, BUN/Creat 18,35 , basal insulin 3,7, TSH 1,46 , total testosterone 5,6.Sleeping very well, but used to have severe sleeping patterns.

Can you be more specific with your back pain? You say lumbosciatic pain but is it really there. Many people who mention sciatic pain actually have a few other issues going on. Obviously impossible to diagnose over the web but just to throw some bread crumbs your way:

**Physical:** Legit Chiropractors can be a godsend which I send many clients to (there are many bad ones so get a referral - anyone that doesn't try to fix you on the first 1-2 visits and tries to put you on a "plan" - RUN AWAY). It could just be a structural thing which can be fixed. I've had hundreds of clients get results with the 3 recommended chiros I send them to. It could be your shoes that you're wearing. Your walking gait could be suboptimal. Many people also don't know how to run properly. Lookup POSE running method. I was taught by Dr. Nicholas Romanov himself and it's a game changer for runners. Your body angle, the way you "pull,pull,pull" is vitally important in how you make impact with the ground. Etc.

You also may need to strengthen yourself through strength training since you re-injured it digging a hole. More spinal extension strength and just posterior chain strength in general + better digging technique

**Nutrient:** As [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) said, it could be Vit D or melatonin. Your Vit D is 27 which I think is considered pretty low being in Beunos Aires. From what I've read, it could be low for you but be optimal for someone else. You may also might want to look at your gut biome as the gut, after muscle testing, can be causing lower back issues. How is your digestion? Do you Rest + Digest?

Hopefully that helps and you can continue on your reading.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-17T21:45:30+0300

### JanSz — 2018-10-18T16:37:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/puting-the-blog-knowledge-to-work.22313/post-253586>

> [axel brandt said:](/goto/post?id=253584)
>
> Thanks for your suggestion janSz, I'll try. I'm taking all the sun that I can.

Sun comes first. always
Wild animal is exposed to sunlight energy all the time.
Vit D comes due to UVb (it is only part of the story)
"Civilized" person spends most time indoors.
One may be thinking only about vit D and get sunlight around noon time.
But that misses a big part of Circadian Rhythm.
The UVa may be even more important.
So seeing sunlight specially early by sunrise, in the morning is important (on clear days, but also any time, fog, rain, snow, clouds notwithstanding)

When [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) started educating us about that, he came up with the story of (healthy for very long time) restaurant crew in Chicago's restaurant).
They were exposed to black light there.
Constantly indoor, when outdoor, there is not much daylight in Chicago.
Black light=UVa
That crew was surprisingly healthy. Same people worked there for 20+ years, no sick days.
That was first story (about UVa).
Shortly after he give us UVb story, via dr Wunch video of coal miners exposed to mercury arc lamps.

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738)
\------------------------------------------------------------
At first I got
Red Sperti (because it emits UV a+b), (White Sperti have only UVb)
but that I use 16min/day, sometimes twice that, (enough UVb not enough UVa)
so I added
UV Light Black Light, HouLight High Power 50W (385-400nm, UVA)
12 hrs/day
This HouLight is the only light that dims my eye glasses (in similar way as sunlight does (even on cloudy day)

..............

Thanks, Jan. My tube black light (365nm) was off Amazon -- [https://smile.amazon.com/gp/product/B0006MQSR6/ref=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o07\_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://smile.amazon.com/gp/product/B0006MQSR6/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o07_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1) . I selected it from a tip by a forum member whose name I'm sorry I forgot. As I recall, he said he used it inside a sauna box which was my initial idea on ordering one, except I placed it where it was on more than just the time I might spend in a sauna, happily now only in winter.

Hope this satisfies your directive to post something? When I have blue blocking glasses on (UVEX cheapies) and look directly at the uv tube light, it is a dim green... I no longer wear prescription glasses since cataract surgeries, but the IOLs I have do block some UV. I think I'll like your HouLight LED unit (385-400nm) as well, which I ordered and would like to keep it on in the room where I am the most -- for 12 hours, like you mentioned. I'm unsure which is preferable, the 365nm or ~385nm... Am I good with both/either one? Always appreciate your posts. Cheers!

# Thread: Circadian optimizing down lights in new house construction?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-optimizing-down-lights-in-new-house-construction.22323/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Benjamin Stone — 2018-10-17T21:34:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-optimizing-down-lights-in-new-house-construction.22323/post-253508>

Hi all,

I know the ideal is darkness at night pierced only by firelight or candle, followed by perhaps moderate use of low flicker incandescents or black lights. But, pragmatically we have the need to install down lights, and I am looking for the best option. While Incandescents seem to be favoured, there are SORAA bulbs which are flicker and blue free, but not yet available for pots in the ceiling.

Enter:

[https://www.lsgc.com/collections/all/products/good-day-night-led-downlight](https://www.lsgc.com/collections/all/products/good-day-night-led-downlight)

Assuming this could be wired (would not want the wireless), and assuming it is verified to be relatively low flicker or flicker free - the automatic circadian adjustment seems like a pretty good compromise. We have a large household and most of the family does not adhere to light hygiene practices, so objective here is to minimize harm while maintaining mostly normal function for household.

Thanks for any feedback on this or other options for down lights!

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-17T21:40:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-optimizing-down-lights-in-new-house-construction.22323/post-253510>

You have options.......but your family context would probably determine my answer. Our answer at the Farm would not be everyone's answer. I used circadian light with a set color temp of 2700K = 9AM.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-17T21:40:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-optimizing-down-lights-in-new-house-construction.22323/post-253511>

YMMV. I have no young kids at home. That is a big deal in making the decision. It is not as easy as you think

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-17T21:40:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-optimizing-down-lights-in-new-house-construction.22323/post-253512>

Here is why:
It bothers me when I visit a medical school or residency program and see so much talent that is in the pursuit of myth. I think we have a duty to alter that trajectory. The best way to engage the mind is teaching it how to think and not what it should be thinking about. The same is true for the public and all those non-black swans out there. I wonder when they will choose to awaken?
[https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/why-...arning-mental-illness-jack-kruse/?published=t](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/why-infants-children-struggling-learning-mental-illness-jack-kruse/?published=t)

> YMMV. I have no young kids at home. That is a big deal in making the decision. It is not as easy as you think

Yes, kids make it so much harder, especially from a compliance perspective. I'm ok being the "weird" dad, but can't create a climate where they feel they need to sneak in bulbs if I'm too dogmatic, don't want to lose their trust.

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=253510)
>
> You have options.......but your family context would probably determine my answer. Our answer at the Farm would not be everyone's answer. I used circadian light with a set color temp of 2700K = 9AM.

That's the kind of system I'd like. We have HUEs installed now, which transfer to low-red at night and around that temp in the am. But now that I am drinking from your firehose I realize the HUE lights are all little EMF bombs, so we leave them off!

May I ask what you installed?

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-17T21:47:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-optimizing-down-lights-in-new-house-construction.22323/post-253521>

> Thanks. That's the tough part, I know kids will use them, even if they have SORAA bulbs or other amber light at desk for homework, so need to minimize harm in their rooms - other rooms we can better control!

Time to watch a couple submarine movies. Silent running mode with red light is the new cool in my house.

I really feel what you're laying down here:

> I'm ok being the "weird" dad, but can't create a climate where they feel they need to sneak in bulbs if I'm too dogmatic, don't want to lose their trust.

Have you shared much of the info here with you children? I think kids are usually more receptive than we think to coherent information even if they want to look cool and dismissive.

To my son it's a simple equation. He wants as much advantage as he can get. He's less disciplined, but he's also 13 and I'm fortunate to have a kid with strong redox.

### Benjamin Stone — 2018-10-18T01:06:20+0300

>

Thanks. I go out of my way to weave in stories to help the kids grok the rationale. I think they understand it, but they don't always feel they need to adhere by it. I pushed it too far to be candid in the past, so need to be careful. It's a slippery slope to zealotry. And what you said, most of our kids are resilient (we have some issues) but its frustrating knowing you could do more to help them. I wish I had this knowledge \*before\* they became little people, back when they wouldn't think twice to question anything we did, it would just be their "normal".

### drezy — 2018-10-18T03:15:12+0300

The boogie man loves the taste of kids that bathe in blue light and wifi and Santa hates them!

Too extreme? OK, maybe... Maybe Santa just dislikes them strongly.

I'm not sure about zealotry when I think the cost is asymmetric.

No doubt there are challenges trying to lead a household.

### Benjamin Stone — 2018-10-18T14:17:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-optimizing-down-lights-in-new-house-construction.22323/post-253576>

Just thought it was worth tagging this product here :

[https://www.soraa.com/news\_releases/90](https://www.soraa.com/news_releases/90)

This reads to me as though a power line option is available for this circadian lighting system, but require further verification.

"
**Connected and Secure**
SORAA Sky communicates over existing power lines in your home with proven, industry-standard HomePlug® GreenPHY technology, which means no wireless hub or internet connection is required to provide seamless integration with no wireless dead spots – just a plug–and-play connection to the Internet. The result is the most advanced security available, with end-to-end encryption of data and sensor events.

"

### Mhoej — 2018-10-18T21:56:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-optimizing-down-lights-in-new-house-construction.22323/post-253628>

It's a challenge going candles only, but it is worth it. Bought a new place a year ago and we never installed any lights, in fact I took down all existing lights (incl. the fridge, oven, freezer) and haven't had any lights in our place for a year now. There are no light bulbs to turn on. The place before that we always had the lights off. It's candles and some red light torches only. It makes it easier that wife is 100% on board and our kids are still little.

Because of the high latitude we're at it is easy in summer with the very long days, but more challenging in winter. In the winter-time I make "swedish torches/canadian candle" (google a pic) to put outside, which gives a break from the darkness. Most people think we're nuts, but I think they're nuts for accepting all the ailments that come with melanopsin dysfunction (which we've had/have our fair share of). All of it is made much easier by living in the country side with a forest as neighbour for sure, low of population density is your friend.

If you don't want to eat crappy food stop having it in your house. If you don't want kids/yourself to watch tv throw out the stupid box. If you don't want nnemf in your house, then don't settle for just remembering to turn wifi off at night, hardwire it. If you don't want your family exposed to artificial lights then remove it from the house.

### Benjamin Stone — 2018-10-18T22:24:44+0300

# Thread: NZ beef a deuterium mismatch for eating in Northern US?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nz-beef-a-deuterium-mismatch-for-eating-in-northern-us.22324/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Benjamin Stone — 2018-10-17T21:39:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nz-beef-a-deuterium-mismatch-for-eating-in-northern-us.22324/post-253509>

So, we can buy this incredibly marbled, grass finished beef imported from New Zealand.

[https://www.firstlight.farm/find-your-marble-score/](https://www.firstlight.farm/find-your-marble-score/)

I notice it has a distinct taste to the fat, different then local fat. Personally I do not like it as much, but others in household do.

I'm wondering if the fat content presents a bad deuterium mismatch to be eating in northern latitudes given the meat was harvested in New Zealand?

I know Dr. Kruse mentioned issues with water in that part of the world, which I love visiting, so wonder if that works its way up through the food supply.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-17T21:43:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nz-beef-a-deuterium-mismatch-for-eating-in-northern-us.22324/post-253515>

yes but not one that I think would matter a lot based upon what we know.

### Benjamin Stone — 2018-10-17T21:47:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nz-beef-a-deuterium-mismatch-for-eating-in-northern-us.22324/post-253520>

# Thread: Have I been working NightShift the past couple years?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-i-been-working-nightshift-the-past-couple-years.22326/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kris90 — 2018-10-18T17:03:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-i-been-working-nightshift-the-past-couple-years.22326/post-253591>

I just had a come-to-jesus moment this morning, because I've noticed a few unfavorable changes in myself over the last few weeks... Of course the days are becoming shorter rapidly, but I've been doing a lot of CT to offset this - works well.

BUT, I realised, I am driving to the office when its still pitch black (leave my house around 6:15 AM). My eyes are getting stabbed by car headlights (even though I wear blue blockers driving). I get to work around 7 AM (sun is not quite risen yet) and walk into my building full of fluorescent and LED lights. I've been doing this for probably the last 7 years.

In summer, this is a non-issue, because I get my morning sun prior to leaving my house (sun is up by 5:15 AM at summer solstice), but once September/October hits, I now have this mismatch. The biggest struggle I've dealt with over the years has been hypogonadism (secondary - meaning low LH/FSH). I've learned how critical a sunrise is for turning on the pituitary, and I'm betting now that blue light SANS red (from fake lights) before sunrise absolutely destroys the VitA/Melanopsin bond and screws up the diurnal release of the pituitary hormones from AM sunlight. EVERY year I've always noticed I go downhill in terms of seasonal affective disorder and adrenal fatigue by around mid-end October. The only times I haven't had these issues is when I was on hormone replacement (HCG). But because I'm off my meds, it seems to be affecting me now. The only thing that helps is doing deep CT prior to going to work. I know because I missed 1 day this week, and felt terrible.

I spoke with my wife about it this morning, and she has never faced the degree of seasonal affective disorder I have despite making less effort as a mitochondriac, but the one thing I realised is she always went into the office later (never earlier than sunrise). Most times, I'm in the office as early as 6:30-7:00 AM. I think this has been screwing me. The reason I like working early hours is so I can be home in time to make dinner before sunset (which is 4:30 PM at winter solstice) and I like working a compressed schedule so I can get more days off so I can focus on growing my business, but now I'm starting to see the detriment. I need to reconstruct my AM especially in winter.

Thoughts?

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-18T17:05:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-i-been-working-nightshift-the-past-couple-years.22326/post-253593>

Not sure why you're waiting to repair this environmental defect unless you have a death wish.

### kris90 — 2018-10-18T17:12:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-i-been-working-nightshift-the-past-couple-years.22326/post-253598>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=253593)
>
> Not sure why you're waiting to repair this environmental defect unless you have a death wish.

Everything just clicked for me last night Jack. I've taken immediate action (i.e. coming in later) but now I'm working on a long term plan. I drove in just as the sun was rising. It was -5 C with the windows down and sun in my eye the whole way while I was still warming up from my 45 F plunge in my pool. What an amazing way to commute to work.

That part was easy to change. Now I just have to figure out when I can work on my business! I guess weekends.

### kris90 — 2018-10-18T17:16:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-i-been-working-nightshift-the-past-couple-years.22326/post-253599>

I'd rather work 7 days a week with an optimal circadian rhythm than work 4.5 days a week and totally fuck up my AM. That has become my takehome.

It's time to REALLY thrive this winter. I think I did pretty good in the past all-things-considering, but I settled for the B. Time for an A+

### Jack Kruse — 2018-10-19T02:39:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-i-been-working-nightshift-the-past-couple-years.22326/post-253656>

I got something for you.

What is coming for you is a lot of hard choices and reality.

The only thing standing between you and your goal is the bullshit story you keep telling yourself as to why you can't achieve it. This is Jim Thorpe below.
