### Alyce Miles OD — 2019-11-23T18:09:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alyces-optimal-journal.22594/post-282679>

Reading an interesting book about technology influencing our brains (Nicholas Carr)

Some quotes

"For most people, the movements of the sun, the moon, and the stars provided all the clocks they needed. Life was ....."dominated by agrarian rhythms, free of haste, careless of exactitude, unconcerned with productivity"

"if we stop exercising our mental skills, we do not just forget them: the brain map space for those skills is just turned over to the skills we practice instead."

"The possibility of intellectual decay is inherent in the malleability of our brains." That doesn't mean that we can't, with concerted effort, once again redirect our neural signals and rebuild the skills we've lost. What it does mean is that the vital paths in our brains become..... the paths of least resistance.

### Alyce Miles OD — 2019-11-25T16:50:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alyces-optimal-journal.22594/post-282772>

Since the SCN pays attention to **cold** in the fall/winter and **_not blue light_** as it (blue light) changes from higher percent in summer and lower percent in winter could the exposure to cold every day help people like me with depression/and or bipolar family histories?

I am and have been since June of 2018 taking cold showers (minimal lukewarm to wash hair in winter) to stimulate the cold receptors and communicate to the SCN what is in the environment.

Does the timing matter? Early morning cold verses anytime cold for those of us with the mood disorders?

### Alyce Miles OD — 2019-12-06T19:39:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alyces-optimal-journal.22594/post-283190>

I have got to figure out this stress thing. I can sleep so well some nights and then BAM something sets me off and I am awake all night. It is such a switch.

I read something in Inger's journal how she thought if she could erase the past and erase the future what would be left? The present. She tried to live in those moments and that is such a profound idea for me. Erase past and present and live in the NOW. Thank you Inger I need to learn how to do this.

### Alyce Miles OD — 2019-12-06T23:17:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alyces-optimal-journal.22594/post-283210>

Couple of quotes from some reading

Grafman: "The more you multi-task, the less deliberate you become; the less able to think and reason out a problem. You become..... more likely to rely on conventional ideas and solutions rather than challenge them with original lines of thought."

Senca: "To be everywhere is to be nowhere--"

Carr: " New strengths in visual spatial abilities from the "internet" go hand in hand with our weakening of our capacity for the kind of "deep processing" that underpins knowledge acquisition, inductive analysis, critical thinking, imagination and reflection."

She tried to live in those moments and that is such a profound idea for me. Erase past and present and live in the NOW. Thank you Inger I need to learn how to do this.

Alyce, one other tool that is really important for me too (in addition to the "only candle rule after sunset"/blueblockers etc. and taking care that evenings are calm and relaxing, no stress, being in the present, silence, no reading or watching bad news etc.) is that I really keep to an animal diet from fall to spring. Like really strict.
I tried to eat some dark raw chocolate a week or two ago, and man it made me feel anxious. And no, I was not anxious about eating the chocolate...lol I wanted it and thought it was a good thing!
It is like my body knows exactly what it should eat!! Crazy! It affects me almost mostly mentally, when I eat what I am not meant to eat.
My body wants just animal food and fat in the dark months. Period.
Probably you do this already, but just thought to mention it in case... I can tell for me it is very important to get it right. For mood stability.
I am now getting 1/8 Galloway cow . everything included, bones and all.. from a coworker that are farmers
This is the right winter food for me. And seafood of course

### Alyce Miles OD — 2019-12-17T18:02:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alyces-optimal-journal.22594/post-283709>

So since it is dark been meditating for hours each day.... After kid is asleep..

I had something happen I don't know how to explain. I was listening to some 10 hertz Cello music and just flowing with the Cello. I had this image of my mother playing the Cello in our backyard of my childhood home just pop into my head. I had a rough time there with angry abusive older brother always hitting me and my mom not being able to stop it. So some painful memories there and then there is this beautiful Cello with my mother playing it like a professional. She had a Dissociative Disorder from sexual trauma as a child. Constant stress no smiling no sharing of dreams. I could say I knew her behaviors but I didn't know HER at all.

So she is playing this Cello in a black dress in the warm sunshine. I am outside sitting with her just listening to her play and watching her. She is about the age 42 before she came down with cancer, and she looks like she took care of herself and spent time outside and ate the right foods. She is smiling and she is entranced by the music she is creating. The weather is so perfect sunny and warm--you couldn't ask for a more perfect day. I am sitting there listening and thinking "where is this going?" I kept the vision going and then I imagined my mom saying positive things to me which she rarely did.
"Alyce, you are so smart, you are making good decisions, you are doing well here." I couldn't sleep at all for the last few days. Only 2-3 hours of sleep and then lying awake in the dark thinking about this after my mother has been dead since 1994. The last words she spoke before she went into a coma was to me, "Alyce, you have been so kind to me today." I hadn't done anything special after the Hospice Nurse brought her home from the hospital. I had to study for some tests and I left around 3-4pm that Wednesday. She died at 10:30pm shortly after when I got home from the Library.

This vision troubled and vexed me in many ways as I have very very few if any fond memories of my mother. I have fond memories of her mother my Grandmother. I loved going to see her in her tiny red house in Colorado. My mother was always strained financially and emotionally. I have no financial stress but emotional stress can go off the chart and I have so much trouble controlling it and reigning it in. But this vision is so vivid almost like it happened. My neck got hot my head got hot and I couldn't sleep at all and I felt some strange feeling radiating all over my spine and neck. When I got up I went into the cold shower and just put my head and neck under the shower to "cool it down." It helped so much and I started crying in the cold shower and I was thinking, "Is this the grieving I have **_never_** done for the loss of my mother?" I felt numb when she died; I was 18. I cried then but I don't think I ever grieved over the loss of her life and more importantly the loss of her and not knowing her.

After reflecting on this vision I know it didn't happen but _**my mind created it**_. I am going to CHOOSE TO BELIEVE it and use it when I have negative thoughts about my mom or my childhood or anything else that happened at that time. It was as if I was a little girl sitting with her mom and everything was good and positive instead of strife and stress. Such a unbelievably powerful image which has turned into a memory. Maybe this memory was something that meditation brought out??? I had no definite plans on meditation I read some works about not setting goals in meditation. Well, this vision was certainly not a goal for me. I want to keep going back to that place of peace, tranquility, hope and love with my mom. I want to understand myself better and accept and move to the present despite these painful past memories.

### Inger — 2019-12-17T20:38:07+0200

Alice, that is so wonderful!
It is trauma therapy you are doing right there. The darkness is so good for that, from my experience! It can be very painful but it is so good when it comes out, the grief we have not grieved..... it just have to be grieved for us to heal.
I guess one reason people often feel uncomfortable with darkness is that they sense all the very painful things they have hidden in the unconscious might come to the surface....

Anxiety, fear and relationship issues are the real issues here....I was in pain and I went downstairs and got my cloth bag for ice and filled it up with ice water and put it on my head and within an hour the pain went down the burning in hands and feet went down and I was like "this is coming from my brain......" Cooling down the CSF and brain cooled down the peripheral nerves which happens in sleep but was not happening because I haven't been sleeping..... I am trying to live in the "present" so hard to visualize I keep getting stuck in repeat "past" or repeat "future" fears......

I had been sleeping so good for the last year with the occasional moonlight night insomnia.. This insomnia for 5-7 days feels like before I ever did any of the principles here.....

### Alyce Miles OD — 2019-12-31T00:47:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alyces-optimal-journal.22594/post-284073>

Met a woman on my mini trip to the south last week. She worked 24 years 11pm-7am shift. She was really a gem to talk to. She biohacked her environment in a counterintuitive way.... She blacked out the entire house. She worked nights and came home to a dark house so she could sleep. She did sleep but did gain weight...... I would say that she lived the life of a mouse in a human body. She was as sweet as she could be but did smoke to generate the NAD NADPH pulse of energy she needed due to her lack of IR and UV light. She did have a gastric sleeve to remove some weight but I mentioned cold to her and Jack Kruse's website and explained why she gained weight and how she was able to sleep and possibly avoid the melatonin cliff that many night shift workers get lost in trying to sleep in a bright room. She even blacked out her kitchen and bathrooms so she could wander in her house during the day. I mentioned all the things she did probably saved her from cancer at an early age......

Interesting now how I can make sense of this stuff....

### Alyce Miles OD — 2020-01-11T20:35:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alyces-optimal-journal.22594/post-284445>

Another 2 Books read:
The Unbroken Field Dr. Michael Greenwood
A quote : page 226

Autobiobgraphy in 5 Short Chapters : Portia Nelson

Chapter 1-- I walk down the street. There is a deep hole in the sidewalk. I fall in. I am lost...I am helpless; it isn't my fault. It takes me forever to find a way out.

Chapter 2--I walk down the same street. There is a deep hole in the sidewalk. I pretend I don't see it. I fall in again. I can't believe I am in the same place; but it isn't my fault. It still takes a long time to get out.

Chapter 3-- I walk down the same street. There is a deep hole in the sidewalk. I see it there. I still fall in....it's a habit. My eyes are open. I know where I am. It is my fault. I get out immediately.

Chapter 4--I walk down the same street. There is a deep hole in the sidewalk. I walk around it.

Chapter 5--I walk down a different street.

I am between Chapters 4 and 5 some days.

The other book was Sounds of Healing Mitchell L. Gaynor MD

**I want you and them to learn better than you have been taught**. Your lab results for any test might be good or bad now........but are they good/bad enough to live a life of thriving to avoid doctors intervention in Nature's game inside your cells for the rest of your life?

The Functional medical profession loves to collect lab data because they really don't have cures for any disease. When the data fails to improve outcomes they are back to square one and you are usually broke because rarely is their lab data covered by insurance.

I teach the latest data so people can upgrade old outdated ideas. I do it to plant a seed in your mind with the hope one day something new and magnificent will emerge in your life based upon Nature's innate wisdom. I am never dismayed by ignorance, indifference, opposition, and ridicule from you or anyone else. In my opinion, no honest, unselfish effort is ever lost; I believe that in time, some of my wisdom seeds will fall onto the good ground in your mind and produce an abundant harvest.

Thus you will become an active factor in Nature's great evolutionary scheme and thereby work out your own salvation physically, mentally, morally and spiritually here and hereafter, ever and forever. That is the credo of the Black Swan. That is why I do what I do.

### Alyce Miles OD — 2020-02-11T17:49:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alyces-optimal-journal.22594/post-285885>

Raynaud's

I have been thinking about this condition so much because I am trying to understand it as it affects me badly. I have read articles where the glucose in the venous is different than the arteriole. I also know that insulin works differently depending upon the temperature. So I have been thinking Raynaud's is an altered insulin/glucose light problem at the microcirculatory level and the brain. When my feet and hands warm back up there is a numb sensation and redness. I have been experimenting all winter and I have noticed with out labs that sugar really affects this on me. When I eat sugar/carbs that should not be present in my environment my hands get colder and the rebound inflammation is bad. It also seems to be affected by the light cycles. So when the seasons change in late Oct/Nov and Feb/Mar the body is trying to make the adjustment to either burn fat or carbs depending upon the temperature and light signals from the outside.

I also have taken a few warm/hot showers to see the effect. Since insulin works differently when the temperature changes when the circulation increases after heat or rebound inflammation there is redness and numbness for me. It is almost like peripheral neuropathy without the diabetes. It is like Raynaud's is a from of diabetes but just in the peripheral microcirculation. This carbohydrate change explains why it was discovered before the advent of artificial light. It also correlates nicely with diabetic symptoms of circulation, neuropathy, and gangrene ( a form of auto amputation). It seems to me that when the hetroplasmy in the tissues of the hands and feet or corresponding brain areas.... increases the disease progresses in those areas. Even if you don't have diabetes..... the mitochondria still have to deal with carbs or fats in the diet and the light environment. The switch flipping is cycled so fast in our lit up world and imported food.........

So when the seasons change in late Oct/Nov and Feb/Mar the body is trying to make the adjustment to either burn fat or carbs depending upon the temperature and light signals from the outside.
>
> I also have taken a few warm/hot showers to see the effect. Since insulin works differently when the temperature changes when the circulation increases after heat or rebound inflammation there is redness and numbness for me. It is almost like peripheral neuropathy without the diabetes. It is like Raynaud's is a from of diabetes but just in the peripheral microcirculation. This carbohydrate change explains why it was discovered before the advent of artificial light. It also correlates nicely with diabetic symptoms of circulation, neuropathy, and gangrene ( a form of auto amputation). It seems to me that when the hetroplasmy in the tissues of the hands and feet or corresponding brain areas.... increases the disease progresses in those areas. Neurocardiac responses to esophageal stimulation have been reported since 1990s. Tougas et al[23](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1002/joa3.12125#joa312125-bib-0023) investigated the effects of distal esophageal stimulation on the human heart rate variability using power spectrum of frequency domain analyses. They found that esophageal stimuli, whether electrical or mechanical, increased high frequency component (HF) and decreased low frequency component (LF) and basic heart rate. These autonomic nervous conditions are prerequisite of vagally mediated AF development.

This explains a lot of what happens to me.....

### Martijn — 2018-12-02T14:50:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/travelling-in-shielded-fabric-inside-an-airplane.22597/post-257108>

Hi everyone,

I am planning to travel down south soon and I need some suggestions. Besides the Jet Lag Rx, blue blockers, and tons of seafood to mitigate the negative effects, I want to take it a step further because that's what crazy mitchondriacs do.

Think about it, 10+ hours on an airplane with 250 'obedient idiots' like Dr. Jack Kruse says that all use microwave devices, wifi + being in a faraday cage in the sky is bad news.

So, has anyone ever traveled with a shielding fabric like being in a canopy inside an airplane? I heard that Dr. Mercola does this or atleast in all of the hotels he is staying in. Most shielding fabrics can not be grounded, would this be a problem? I am also not sure if I can put my feet on the metal framework infront of me since the fabric won't hit the floor then to create a faraday effect, so I can't be grounded myself either. I found a fabric that can be grounded but it's more expensive and it's grey, which I don't like. I prefer white fabric. Would this be way better though? I'd love to hear some insight or ideas on this. This seems like the least harmful way to travel besides avoiding airplanes and airports all together.

# Thread: FIR?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fir.22599/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Theka — 2018-12-02T22:51:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fir.22599/post-257127>

I have been reading and thinking about FIR uses and therapies. I found this summary of research but even not understanding all of it, it raises more questions. They ask this, "Since the principle chromophore at FIR wavelengths is not CCO but rather water, we must ask ourselves how can the biological effects of red and NIR absorption be so similar to those seen with FIR ?" It seems to me that water must be the key. They also ask whether mito respond to non-heating FIR like they do to LLLT. What about heating FIR vs non-heating?

What about FIR panels for home heating or would the potential emf negate any benefits? A mirror in the bathroom that never fogs is pretty cool, in theory. The textile uses wouldn't have the emf, so they sound safe.
Are there any links someone could point me to?

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3699878/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3699878/)

### Jack Kruse — 2018-12-03T00:16:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fir.22599/post-257129>

FIR therapy utilizes longer wavelengths of the infrared spectrum than the established near infrared-radiation therapy (NIR) and regulatory mechanisms have been shown to involve the photoreactive complexes of the ETC.

Karu T. I., Pyatibrat L. V., Kolyakov S. F. & Afanasyeva N. I. Absorption measurements of cell monolayers relevant to mechanisms of laser phototherapy: reduction or oxidation of cytochrome c oxidase under laser radiation at 632.8 nm. Photomedicine and laser surgery 26, 593–599, doi: 10.1089/pho.2008.2246 (2008).

### Theka — 2018-12-03T22:40:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fir.22599/post-257198>

That sounds like benefits were transitory at best depending upon redox with specific wavelengths and a laser source. Would that necessarily also be the case in the non-thermal instances with ceramics or textiles? What about the 'waon' therapy in Japan that is sauna based? And what about using FIR as a heating source so that it is an ambient field in the environment? It sounds like it could have unintended consequences that haven't been considered.

# Thread: Introduction - Student-Athlete UCLA. Trying to Live My Best Life!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introduction-student-athlete-ucla-trying-to-live-my-best-life.22600/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Erik Holt — 2018-12-03T00:08:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introduction-student-athlete-ucla-trying-to-live-my-best-life.22600/post-257128>

Hello everyone!

My name is Erik Holt. I am a 22 year old student-athlete at UCLA and currently finishing up my last soccer season this fall. I was born and raised in San Diego, CA up until I moved to Arizona at 16 to pursue further development of my soccer career. From a young age, I have always been fascinated by health and fitness. I continue to look for new information and studies to both further my goals both on and off the field. I enjoy listening to The Ben Greenfield Fitness Podcast because of the relation it has with achieving high athletic performance as well as other health advise. However; about two years ago, my dad was on Youtube and found a video by Jack Kruse. He sent it over to me and I was blown away by all his information and context. Over the last two years, I have listened to just about every Youtube Video and Podcast that I could get my hands on to learn more and more about the topics Jack discusses. I have also read some of the books he has mentioned within the Vermont Conferences like EPI - PAELO RX and others. I have truly been blown away by the material, science, evidence and people who Jack has discussed and put fourth through all his content. I have finally decided to take the first step and join his tribe in order to further my development.
I thought I'd mention my routine below in order to gain advice about how I can improve as well as fix anything that may need to be adjusted. I live on the 9th floor of a Dorm here at UCLA where Wifi and other devices are prominent on campus. In addition, you could only imagine all of the negative environmental factors that Jack has mentioned within my living situation here in LA. Here below are the following things I do with my day.
\- 20-30 minutes of Sauna
\- Get outside in the morning barefoot with my shirt off; around 20 minutes looking near the sun.
\- Drink Mountain Spring Water as much as possible; or at least good alkaline water.
\- Cold Thermogenesis: since it is "Winter" in LA, I get to the ocean 3x a week and go in for about 15-20 minutes. Otherwise, I use an ice bath here at school that the student athletes have for recovery. In addition, I also take 3 minute cold showers at least once a day.
\- I use blue blocking glasses as much as possible when using computers, at class, and whenever the sun goes down. I also have a protecter shield to block some EMF from my computer.
\- I train from 11-2 everyday and live in LA so I am able to get Vitamin D year around.

Here are just a few things off the top of my head that I do. I try to be highly aware of my environment as well as the environment that I ideally want to be in. I want to learn as much as I can about everyone's routines and experiences in order to optimize my life and share this with others.

My main question as I finish college with an International Relations degree is how do I transition into a health related field. I want to go back to school if I end up not pursing professional soccer and pursue something that will allow me to become knowledgeable about the topics that Jack Kruse talks about. I'm just trying to find out where to start. I look forward to any responses and advice- thank you!

### Jack Kruse — 2018-12-03T00:20:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introduction-student-athlete-ucla-trying-to-live-my-best-life.22600/post-257130>

Welcome. UCLA on the 9th floor yikes.

Transition into healthcare is easier today than ever with the lack of degrees being pushed in functional medicine.

TODAY's DEEP CT LESSON: Use the albedo effect of the snow to connect to the electric and magnetic flux in your country high up in latitudes. That region on Earth is more connected than you think in this way. Here are some facts about the sun that are not disputed by anyone credible:

1\. The Sun has a magnetic field that must be generated by electric currents. This is attributed to the “Solar dynamo”.

2\. The Sun is part of the “Heliospheric Current Sheet”. The current that flows in the sheet is tiny, 10⁻¹⁰A/m², but the area of the sheet is large (though I believe the current density falls off with distance)

3\. The Themis satellites discovered “flux ropes” connecting the Sun with the Earth. Although the name refers to magnetic flux, they carry current too, and it is estimated that they pump 650,000 Amps into the Arctic region via the poles. This is where you win at high latitudes when you use CT, grounding, and AM sunrises and PM sunsets. Do I believe humans are capable of using these currents on Earth? I do.

Seeing the sun is seeing its magnetic flux in the AM where you live. The scientists of today think deeply about things that do not matter instead of clearly about the things that connect us to nature. Our brain is a biologic electromagnetic receiver. It turns out the part with the most fidelity as a receiver is a mitochondrial matrix. It links directly to the sun. It uses the blood as its transformer. This is why the blood is a non-Newtonian magnetohydrodynamic fluid. when you live at high latitude you need to have a circulatory system that is in tip-top shape to take advantage of the core magnetic flux in the sun's light. In this way, when you expand the idea to the Universe scale, you'll begin to understand that in the Universe there is a core library from which we obtain knowledge, strength, and inspiration. Our mitochondrial matrix has figured out how to tap this wisdom in the sun's magnetic footprint. Humans have not realized it yet, but I believe quantum biology will soon prove it. Human knowledge barely has touched this core. To date, few have penetrated into the secrets of this core knowledge of nature, but I believe that it exists because of all the things we've discovered so far about Nature. All that was great about nature's laws in the past has marginalized, ridiculed, condemned, combated, suppressed — (Flexner report/Rockefeller) Now in the 21st century, the world of quantum mechanics and all its weirdness is allowing all of that scientific suppression to re-emerge with a larger potential energy to leap understanding forward. In this way, the old science of nature will emerge and change consciousness more powerfully than ever before. I am quite confident in science, that one day, will become more triumphant for mankind and be able to elevate modern man from the struggle of his current paradigms. The problem with our species right now is that we’re stuck in an outdated technical paradigm that is running our economic engines. Our system is rotten to the core because we are relying on something harming us in non-linear ways. Science is being used to make the economy run today.......and that’s about all it does. The economy IS the problem because it relies on Big Tech revenue to operate to generate the cash flows we think we need. This all comes from the Rockefeller mindset we got. # Thread: Increased Transpiration in Plants

### Optimal Health is EZ — 2018-12-04T00:50:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/increased-transpiration-in-plants.22607/post-257204>

**Hello all -
**
Attached are two pictures from a scientific study of wheatgrass. The "control" sample of wheatgrass received normal filtered water and in the picture you can see there are fewer droplets on the grass. The "vitalized" sample has increased droplets; this sample received the same initial water as the control group, but then the water went through a magnetic treatment. All variables were held the same besides this treatment of the water. No chemicals added.

The vitalized water in other tests showed:

-   30% increase in biomass
-   20% increase in Brix (nutrient density)

**For this post I'm trying to specifically understand the droplet formation from a mitochondriac perspective.**

I know Dr. Kruse has mentioned water on the surface in two other areas that I recall:

-   Tears on the cornea act to cool the surface, like a semiconductor, which will increase photoelectric absorption, particularly of UV light, when cooled.
-   Isomerization of vitamin D3 on the skin surface needs water to function.

From what I can tell this droplet formation on wheatgrass is some type of guttation or transpiration. The vitalized water (magnetic treatment) seems to increase this process in multiple studies done. Can anyone speak to the process of water on the leaf surface in plants? Is guttation or droplet formation a way to increase yield from sunlight?

I apologize if this isn't the best place to post this topic - I'm not sure where it should go! I consider this a biohack of water, and biohacking water should be good for people if it's good for plants...perhaps.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-12-04T19:30:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-video-dr-martin-pall-and-much-more.22608/post-257248>

All nnEMF drops oxygen availability and screws with calcium at the same time. It is not a one or the other effect. For example, another natural form of infertility is called menopause.

**Menopause** is always found in a low O2 surface environment due to a rising heteroplasmy rate in a colony of mitochondria. Hormone crash is a low O2 surface environment, too. LR is a pseudohypoxic state. When someone is LR they usually have fertility issues. When oxygen drops cells have to use glycolysis and the PPP because they cannot use the TCA and urea cycle to create energy and move H+ properly.

Oxygen is the terminal electron acceptor for mitochondria.

**Oxygen has a very strong affinity for electrons, hence why it is able to provide a pull for electrons on our inner mitochondrial membrane to improve the current and magnetic flux in mitochondria. When the respiratory proteins are not properly aligned by melanopsin damage, not even the valence shell of oxygen can the desire for electrons by this atom can be met fast enough when the cytochromes defective. This is why redox shifted mitochondria exist. This is why pseudohypoxia exists in these states. Humans, birds, and bats are a species who has a very high aerobic capacity because they have a large mitochondria capacity. They all use oxygen as their terminal electron acceptor. This is why David Sinclair’s paper, from Dec 2013, on pseudohypoxia link to NAD+ and Calcium, is incredibly important for you to understand completely, on your road to optimal. All lead to infertility. When you are young and fertile and you live around nnEMF the young become the old with respect to their ability to make germ cells.

**When the respiratory proteins do a poor job of this, low NAD+ and SIRT 1 ratio’s are the results in cells. How does ketosis fit into this scenario? If you read my book, The Epi-Paleo Rx, you will see that ketosis is a “small critical part of reversing most diseases. If you don’t understand my blogs, you will think I have just contradicted myself in my book. I have not. Ketosis can change the flow of electrons, But it has zero effect on the electron spin or quantum spin state, of electrons or proton spin (deuterium level). Moreover, it has no effect on size or extent of damage of the ECT protein components in cytochromes. Ironically where ketosis works of us, is by narrowing the distance between the respiratory proteins in the mitochondrial electron transport that ultimately determines the speed of flow of electrons. Ketosis only augments the flow of electrons. If the cytochromes engines are damaged ketosis won't help either.........you have to repair the engine redox power to replace old damage cytochromes and this takes more DC electric power and the sun is the best source of this wireless power.**
**

### Scompy — 2018-12-05T04:01:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-video-dr-martin-pall-and-much-more.22608/post-257281>

5G will be using 4G frequencies as well... Telecoms are also buying up 600 MHz bands and they'll keep using everything up to the normal 940 MHz, **in addition** to adding onto all of the newer EM Spectrum mmWave auctions that have been over-publicized: 20 Ghz to the 70 Ghz part of the spectrum. Why? Because geographical barriers are going to be a huge hindrance for the newer mmWaves that don't penetrate as well. A person that connects to the IoT or 5G will need multiple antennas built into the phones, vehicles and devices to maintain a continual connection. Unless they solve the design problems, it may end up being more practical in most environments to continue to use the 600 MHz to 1000 MHz spectrum, increase the power density, and only use the mmWaves in areas where it's more open to beam-form from point to point without the potential of interference. If you have an automated vehicle traveling at 60 MPH, if they attempt to use mmWaves, they'll also need many more antennas in the MHz part of the spectrum to penetrate through materials.

For these reasons, I see 5G more of an excuse to increase the overall intensity as a political grab. They will crank up the power-output in the name of ensuring connectivity and also put in towers as a base infrastructure all over, just like the Smart Meter grid rollout forced upon us and still being implemented. Once the infrastructure is in-place, then it's going to be very hard to take down...

Specific cities and towns all over have been chosen to implement and 'experiment' upon citizens in those locations. Texas has been doing this for over 2 years. Some places, like in Cali, have been blocking Telecoms from putting in the infrastructure with local laws.

This was covered in my recent Vermont speech (2017) on the retina. The Vermont (2018) talk might be more important to the etiology of this disease. This information goes all the way back to Dr. Delgado experiments in the 1960's on bulls. Listen to this very ominous warning from the inventor of electromagnetic mind control. # Thread: Bees and nnEMF.....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bees-and-nnemf.22614/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2018-12-05T02:30:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bees-and-nnemf.22614/post-257273>

Increase in the usage of electronic gadgets has led to electropollution of the environment. Honeybee behavior and biology have been affected by electrosmog since these insects have magnetite in their bodies which helps them in magneto-navigation. There are reports of sudden disappearance of bee populations from honey- bee colonies. According to "industry insiders" and "industry scientists" the reason is still not clear. The literature is less murky. Researchers have compared the performance of honeybees in cellphone radiation exposed and unexposed colonies. A significant (p < 0.05) decline in colony strength and in the egg laying rate of the queen was observed. The behavior of exposed foragers was negatively influenced by the electromagnetic pollution exposure, there was neither honey nor pollen in the colony at the end of their experiment.

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-12-05T21:08:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bees-and-nnemf.22614/post-257322>

Bees are one of our canaries in coal mine for observing effects of ongoing electrosmog pollution.

Birds apparently are being similarly affected, but I haven't seen any hard studies on that.

I have heard that although beneficial insects like bees suffer under the RF-EMF onslaught, insects that are considered pests, like ticks, flies, and mosquitos seem to get along fine with RF radiation. But I don't have any hard science to back that story up definitely. Not yet...

### Jack Kruse — 2018-12-06T20:24:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/who-packs-your-parachute.22617/post-257411>

Why do exogenous things uncouple endogenous biology cycles in cells?

Life is a kind of prison for us when we believe the wrong things. We are connected to the universe, but we experience this world disconnected from the whole. It is an optical delusion of our conscious perception allowing us to separate space from time. This prison is unique. We can constrict our life or we can broaden its walls via our perspective. Our perspective is our conscious optical delusion of how we control the flow of light in our brain and emit those frequencies back to others to sense.

What is the relationship between order and chaos? I believe a living cell embodies both processes. You must have chaos within to become vital. Living and learning manifest from chaos, migrating from the safety of comfort to the foolishness and stupidity of risk is how an order is slowly created. Behind the veil of apparent randomness, many processes are highly ordered, following simple rules. These rules came from Turing and Shannon in computing and information theory. Deep in our unconscious is a pervasive need for a "logical" universe that makes sense. This is why you believe there is no way a cup of coffee can hurt me today but it was fine in our past. It turns out, nature is always one step beyond logic. Black Swans are taught that the chaos in science is merely a natural order waiting to be deciphered. Information is how the truth is liberated from disorder and chaos.

Ignoring light is akin to inviting disease to your doorstep. Do not die while you’re still alive. Response and control mechanisms are linked but not always coupled properly in nature. Conventional medicine and physiology often tend to ignore the biophysical part of organic existence in cells despite the fact that biochemical reactions without regulative and controlling information would end up in metabolical chaos. Each cell orchestrates 100,000 biochemical reactions per second. Light frequency is the “steering wheel” of these reactions. Coffee, screens, phones, and supplements and often time Rx's uncouple all these biologic systems control panels below the cell level and these HINDER our recovery.

Light in the living organism propagates on biochemical pathways thereby controlling and steering the reaction chains and their outcome. Thermal management in the mitochondria of mammals is anything but trivial since infrared radiation is not only the product of quantum energy degradation from photochemical processes down to infrared light. Messages from nature to our cells must be delivered optimally. This is why our genome is designed to respond to environmental signals, not control them. In nature, atoms are coupled to light to control responses. To believe in omnipotent power of genes supports lazy comfort and ignorance in people of science today. When the message from outside it is jumbled, inflammation and poor redox results and diseases manifest.

# Thread: Deuterium Depleted Water Questions/Research/Production Ideas
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-depleted-water-questions-research-production-ideas.22618/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet

### TabulaRasa — 2018-12-05T22:18:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-depleted-water-questions-research-production-ideas.22618/post-257331>

Hello! I am medical resident who studies subjects like this in my free time.
DDW looks like something to me that could potentially be very important. Because of that I have looked into the subject quite a bit (ie. I spent about a week going through the global isotopic distribution of it) and I had a few questions or ideas I wanted to put towards the community.

Testing questions:
I have spent a while looking over most of the information online and there were 4 pieces of data I could not find that would be **immensely** appreciated.
Has anyone tested or found the testing results for these 3 items:
1) DDW produced with the fractional freezing method (specially what the PPM was before, after 1 cycle, and after multiple cycles).
2) Water produced from the John Ellis machine?
3) Water from the Iceland Glacial Spring water brand.
4) (less important) does anyone know where you can get a list of the deuterium content of a wide range of foods? I found bits and pieces but no comprehensive ones.

Procuring questions:
From what I've looked at so far, Naya Water (assuming it comes from the correct spring and now Naya uses multiple) seems to be the best spring water brand to go with for low DDW content that can be mixed with Qualivaria or anything. Does anyone know where it can be bought in the USA for a reasonable price?

Production ideas:
I identified the following options on how to get DDW water.
1) A home device that produces it with a high energy cost. The John Ellis Machine seems to be the only example of that. My primary concern with it is that there was no way for me to verify if it did. I contacted the manufacturer directly and never heard back.
2) Get it from a low deuterium concentration spring source. That would either be somewhere in the rocky mountains or from the correct spring bottled water brand (ie. Naya or possibly iceland glacial water).
3) Hope new technology gets made that can do the separation (I have faith this will emerge within 10 years since a market demand is starting to be created, but 10 years is a while to wait).
4) Purchase it from the nuclear power industry in the process of them refining it for either future nuclear fusion or just heavy water for reactors. The main issue with this approach is that most of the separation approaches I have seen do not so much concentrate a light and heavy water as they concentrate a heavy water and make a lot of slightly light water waste.
5) Engineer your own home production device. As far as I could tell this is not feasible.
6) Hope that better RO membranes for this purpose can be made. I read that some have been developed that can produce a bit of a difference, but it is not enough to be viable.
7) Test if the home freezing method works, and what the relative time comittment is to the water produced.
8) Buy qualivaria and dilute it with the most DDW spring water you can get.
9) Manufacture a co-op distillation tower.
10) Produce it via electrolysis which is then combusted in a fuel cell. I am drawn to #10 since light water has an 8-1 affinity for electrolyzing and that will essentially get a better ration than tons of distillation cycles.

I spoke to a chemical phd who has done a lot of work in the fuel cell industry. She told me that the electrolysis idea is most likely to unstable to be safe (hydrogen gas is difficult to work with and will frequently explode on you), and she thought that the best option would be to go with a co-op distillation tower under vaccum. In either case, the correct course of action would be to consult a chemical engineer (or a firm with a team of them) to get an estimate of what it would cost (high 5 figures low 6 figures) and then just produce and sell the stuff. Assuming that was the case, doing it in the vicinity of Denver CO (maybe a bit west) would seem like a good spot/location to do it in since there is lower DDW to start with and its a central distribution hub for the USA. If the interest was there for a group of people to do that, I could see that being a great way to do it.

Anyways...
If you have any thoughts or ideas on any of what I wrote, particularly the data I am trying to find, please comment!

Thank you

### JackieNewZealand — 2018-12-06T00:52:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-depleted-water-questions-research-production-ideas.22618/post-257345>

Hello. I too was wondering about the John Ellis machine. Don't think it's legit, but unsure. After listening to a dozen or so of Gerald Pollack videos, have concluded that DDW water may not be necessary. Pollack is often asked what water he drinks and always answers that more research needs to be done and he tries all kinds and really doesn't know which is best. There is one interview where he reluctantly mentions a man called Jef (one "f") Harvey who markets H3O2 water and he said that there was ample evidence that it cured several people of serious conditions including a man on kidney dialysis. He also said they were way behind in production ... but I don't know what the current status is for that company. Frankly I feel really great just drinking a lot MORE water (as per the book "you're not sick, you're thirsty") and maybe the quantity of water is what really matters and perhaps no special water is needed (as long as it's a clean source). Don't know if that Harvey water is deuterium depleted, as Pollack did not mention this, but it might be worth looking into if you think it's important. I trust Gerald Pollack and if you ever hear him speaking about DDW and / or how to make it, then please let me know. Good luck.

> it is not because he/she have not drank water.

I think Dr Boros misses a key point with camels. Yes a camel has the specific adaptations to cross the desert without water, but will drink around 200 litres when they eventually reach a water source. Camels in cold environments classed as deserts will take in around 10 litres a day from snow, the minimum they need to survive. Even if marathoners can make 20 litres of matrix water during a 2 hour marathon, it's an acute stress response to the demands placed on the mitochondria, but it doesn't make a chronic lower grade stress response healthy to mitochondria forced to contribute to the organism as a whole indefinitely. With all their special water conserving adaptations, camels still need a source of water.

### TabulaRasa — 2018-12-08T22:56:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-depleted-water-questions-research-production-ideas.22618/post-257613>

### oliverg — 2018-12-19T20:28:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-depleted-water-questions-research-production-ideas.22618/post-258458>

TabulaRasa, home freezing and/or distillation reduces deuterium, but by extremely small amounts. You would have to do it hundreds of times to get anywhere worthwhile (maybe .5-2ppm difference per go around).

For those wondering about the John Ellis water, it definitely isn’t deuterium depleted, but something people seem to miss, is that on the website the claims have nothing to do with deuterium depletion. They say the real difference is the hydrogen bond angle, which has been changed from the usual 104.5 degrees to 114 degrees, making it better to help the body deplete deuterium, but mainly produce more hydrogen with less energy cost for the body than with usual water. I have no idea if any of it is legit in any way. Talking to a bunch of people who swear by it, some who are going to get their body’s deuterium levels tested before and after a few months of drinking JE water. We’ll see.

I really don't think even a lean person requires much food/fat. If the environment is optimized, then so will autophagy. That's how IF works it's magic: increased autophagy which allows us to recycle protons in the matrix.

If you go back and re-read Jack's CT blogs, you will see that cold/sleep/fasting was primordial. They all enhance autophagy. Life began at the bottom of the ocean where hydrothermal vents split water to produce energy. That energy was never lost. It was recaptured in water. That was Jack's message I believe in the CT blogs, and the fact that cold water = more electron dense was a big deal (photoelectric effect). Water is the key to life and without water we lose light. When life evolved to land, it had to bring water with it. Plants via photosynthesis tapped into the earth's supply of water, and animals made their water via cellular respiration (reversing that process). Light is what drives the process.

### kris90 — 2018-12-20T16:23:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-depleted-water-questions-research-production-ideas.22618/post-258538>

We need to stop thinking carbs vs fats or drinking water vs making water. Remember it's all about electrons and protons.

Carbs = less electrons+protons and more deuterium
Fats = more electrons+protons and less deuterium

Light interacts with electrons in matter via the photoelectric effect. Light (red) can move things with mass (protons). Light does not care about carbs vs fats. It cares about electrons and protons and nothing else.

### shah78 — 2019-02-25T02:41:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-depleted-water-questions-research-production-ideas.22618/post-263912>

Hi, I need to brainstorm my Manufacturing of my personal supply of ddw. In May of 20 18 I was drinking 5 quarts of water a day for the past year or so. In June I stepped up my in chicxulub crater water time from 6 hours a day to 9 and miraculously needed only 3 quarts of water a day. Obviously I was making my own water like Jerry Pollock says. That amount stayed the same until October when I watched the three and a half hour ddw orgy Marathon. By Laszlo and Chee. I then started lowering my water consumption with the new vasopressin information. Over the few months I got my water down to a quart and a half a day. My goal is to duplicate Laszl's one cup of day. Anybody have any good ideas how to do thi?. I went into ketosis to experiment on December 22nd and I'm still in ketosis. Here is my day. I wake up with no thirst I pretty much don't drink much after 12 noon, the previous day. More specifically I drink after my one meal a day at about 11am. It seems that eating is what makes me thirsty. I pretty much eat the same thing everyday lots of avocados, soft boiled eggs grass-fed beef, lots of fish and conch. I also eat fresh turmeric, ginger, a little bit of onion and garlic. Also lots of lime and cocoa nibs, pepper and100gms butter a day....so let's resume the day. I wake up with very little thirst I go to the beach and eat from 7 a.m. till 11 am. I get thirsty during this time. And polish off about 20 ounces of lime water. I remain thirsty for about 3 hours. Then the magic starts.  Around 2 p.m. I start getting less thirsty. My mouth /throat starts to get moist. I pretty much remain non thirsty throughout the evening and night-time. I do sip some water most evenings maybe a half a cup. My only concern is that the urine is not clear anymore. It was clear at 3 quarts a day I think it was clear at 2 quarts a day. But it is not clear anymore when I go below 1 quart a day. Is this dispositive that I'm going too quickly going too low? Any and all crazy theories welcome.

The idea is that when all works as it should
**body makes metabolic water, 3000 gallons/day**(time 29:00)
or maybe 3000liters not gallons
That is circulation.
There should be spare 1 or 1.5 liters within that volume for discarding it as urine.
\------
So I would expect
that no matter how much you drink (if you eat lots of fat or if you do have body fat available)
on the low side
if you drink less (than your minimum drinking volume as required by your body)
that should not affect your urine volume
(you will pee more if you drink over your minimum required drinking water)
\-------------------

"Normal" amounts that people are expected to drink is 1.5 to 2 liters = 1.6 to 2.1 US-quarts
You report drinking 5 US-quarts = 4.7 liter
That is a lot
It indicates some problems.

Yeh I know you're not a beginner, but I didn't know if you'd read it.

From it, apart from the general risks of dehydration, I'm concerned that if I under drink and keep vasopressin chronically raised, or yo yoing, I risk losing the circadian control of water flow. Its a bit scary that I can screw up my circadian rhythm by a poor decision about water intake …  Not just feed back loops but feed forward is interesting ..... Something that jumped out for me was that during autophagy we cannot make any water at CCO. Tie that in with the mouse research that mice increased water intake just before sleep, based on circadian rhythm, not physiological need, and I would be concerned that, without enough water in the system, it would interfere with sleep itself, or my autophagy during sleep. With dementia in my maternal mitochondrial line, and the circadian rhythm of CSF production, that could be an epic disaster, even in a "good" in terms of nnemf environment. In a "bad" environment the shit is just likely to hit the fan sooner. Without that in my mitochondrial heritage, it might take longer, but whichever way I look at it, I see risks. Jack didn't mention that the mice were being deuterium depleted by diet/water, so I assume not, but then from my own journal (and I still don't know the answer ):

"If REM sleep depletes deuterium …. Do hibernating bears need to load up with deuterium to ensure they can access REM sleep …I was looking up deuterium and REM sleep and _"Finally, in naïve mice, deuterium-depleted water treatment increased EEG indices of wakefulness, and decreased duration of REM sleep"_ jumped out at me from this [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/25092571](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/25092571). So could too low blood levels of deuterium prevent someone from entering the REM sleep phase as a protective mechanism?"

Then there's the harvesting H+ from water ….

Camel analogy  aside, I think the Boros hack is missing so much biophysics that I won't be giving JanSz my drinking/urine stats any time soon.

### Sue-UK — 2019-02-28T15:39:00+0200

If its the only source of external water, one issue is that if my health gets worse, there's nowhere lower to go DDW wise. In the Somylai book they stopped DDW in advance of surgery, because some people's wounds didn't heal as well or as quickly. This implies deuterium is needed for repair/regeneration, not just after surgery, but any accident, or general day to day damage. It also kinda links back to the deuterium depleted mice getting less REM sleep...……….? There's also a risk of infection, from its role with UV in the blood, and in the immune cells? In the wrong place, or in the wrong amount, deuterium can be toxic, but I think there's a goldilocks zone, at the right time, in the right place and in the right amount, I believe its essential. It seems to me that its losing control of deuterium that is the problem, not deuterium itself. What I haven't found out is how low deuterium levels in the blood can go without risking what I call deuterium starvation,  but I suspect that forcing the mitochondria to supply all my body's water needs and a very low deuterium diet increases the risk.

I don't know enough about the safety of drinking DDW-25 straight for my own context, let alone anyone else.

Part 1  musings from a rabbit hole I'm still delving into

JK "Water is just stored light energy and information used for organization." This implies to me that water intake brings in new energy, light and information from the environment.
[https://www.jstage.jst.go.jp/article/jpa/19/5/19\_5\_207/\_pdf](https://www.jstage.jst.go.jp/article/jpa/19/5/19_5_207/_pdf) The Turnover of Body Water as an indicator of health seems to point to daily water turnover decreasing with age (higher heteroplasmy?). I would suspect that good vasopressin circadian rhythm is critical to maintaining good body water turnover? If so, deliberately under drinking doesn't seem logical ….

As seasonal omnivores, whereas there may be advantages in some conditions/contexts to eat a keto diet until we get to the point where its no longer needed, if its not necessary then eating seasonally and the carb intake matching the environmental cues of the latitude we are in makes more evolutionary sense, and more informational sense to the mitochondria. As 100g of carbs only makes 55g of water, and in the summer our losses through sweat etc increase, it makes sense that we would need to take in more water than if we were in seasonally directed keto during the winter. Good sunlight = better turnover of body water? The alternative in winter to increase body water turnover might be drinking water, and doing CT to prompt getting rid of unstructured water? i.e water that has "spent" its hydrogen information?

Also, the mineral content of water could be classed as nutritional, certainly the 14 week silicon enriched water hack I'm doing is proving very interesting. I would suspect that it would be possible to use specific waters, - even a water cocktail \- other than commercial DDW, to improve/protect the deuterium depleting pathways, rather than simply deplete deuterium. I also think the right water has the potential to clean up the pineal, so that I can sense the environmental signals better, for better circadian rhythm.

### Greyslate — 2019-03-20T14:10:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-depleted-water-questions-research-production-ideas.22618/post-265990>

> [TabulaRasa said:](/goto/post?id=257331)
>
> Hello! I am medical resident who studies subjects like this in my free time.
> DDW looks like something to me that could potentially be very important. Because of that I have looked into the subject quite a bit (ie. I spent about a week going through the global isotopic distribution of it) and I had a few questions or ideas I wanted to put towards the community.
>
> Testing questions:
> I have spent a while looking over most of the information online and there were 4 pieces of data I could not find that would be **immensely** appreciated.
> Has anyone tested or found the testing results for these 3 items:
> 1) DDW produced with the fractional freezing method (specially what the PPM was before, after 1 cycle, and after multiple cycles).
> 2) Water produced from the John Ellis machine?
> 3) Water from the Iceland Glacial Spring water brand.
> 4) (less important) does anyone know where you can get a list of the deuterium content of a wide range of foods? I found bits and pieces but no comprehensive ones.
>
> Procuring questions:
> From what I've looked at so far, Naya Water (assuming it comes from the correct spring and now Naya uses multiple) seems to be the best spring water brand to go with for low DDW content that can be mixed with Qualivaria or anything. Does anyone know where it can be bought in the USA for a reasonable price?
>
> Production ideas:
> I identified the following options on how to get DDW water.
> 1) A home device that produces it with a high energy cost. The John Ellis Machine seems to be the only example of that. My primary concern with it is that there was no way for me to verify if it did. I contacted the manufacturer directly and never heard back.
> 2) Get it from a low deuterium concentration spring source. That would either be somewhere in the rocky mountains or from the correct spring bottled water brand (ie. Naya or possibly iceland glacial water).
> 3) Hope new technology gets made that can do the separation (I have faith this will emerge within 10 years since a market demand is starting to be created, but 10 years is a while to wait).
> 4) Purchase it from the nuclear power industry in the process of them refining it for either future nuclear fusion or just heavy water for reactors. The main issue with this approach is that most of the separation approaches I have seen do not so much concentrate a light and heavy water as they concentrate a heavy water and make a lot of slightly light water waste.
> 5) Engineer your own home production device. As far as I could tell this is not feasible.
> 6) Hope that better RO membranes for this purpose can be made. I read that some have been developed that can produce a bit of a difference, but it is not enough to be viable.
> 7) Test if the home freezing method works, and what the relative time comittment is to the water produced.
> 8) Buy qualivaria and dilute it with the most DDW spring water you can get.
> 9) Manufacture a co-op distillation tower.
> 10) Produce it via electrolysis which is then combusted in a fuel cell. I am drawn to #10 since light water has an 8-1 affinity for electrolyzing and that will essentially get a better ration than tons of distillation cycles.
>
> I spoke to a chemical phd who has done a lot of work in the fuel cell industry. She told me that the electrolysis idea is most likely to unstable to be safe (hydrogen gas is difficult to work with and will frequently explode on you), and she thought that the best option would be to go with a co-op distillation tower under vaccum. In either case, the correct course of action would be to consult a chemical engineer (or a firm with a team of them) to get an estimate of what it would cost (high 5 figures low 6 figures) and then just produce and sell the stuff. Assuming that was the case, doing it in the vicinity of Denver CO (maybe a bit west) would seem like a good spot/location to do it in since there is lower DDW to start with and its a central distribution hub for the USA. If the interest was there for a group of people to do that, I could see that being a great way to do it.
>
> Anyways...
> If you have any thoughts or ideas on any of what I wrote, particularly the data I am trying to find, please comment!
>
> Thank you

I learned about DD on YouTube and how to make the water.Already on a DD diet i felt the difference between arrowhead vs DD water made with RO water instantly. No doubt this process works for some.The greatest part is i can feel myself healing from various things just like a meat diet,same concept.
Mike,

### Inger — 2019-03-20T17:00:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-depleted-water-questions-research-production-ideas.22618/post-266013>

I am now starting to think that the freezing method really makes a difference.

I used to freeze almost all my water, I use Volvic spring water. I freeze it only once, losing about 5% maybe. or less, depending when I catch the freezing process. 1,5-2 hours.

So I got a little lazy and stopped freezing it for a few weeks. And after a while I had to pee in the early morning again! Drinking DDW for a while stopped it completely, I never had to pee in the early mornings anymore.
So I started freezing it again, and just after a few days I realized I have not had to pee in the morning anymore. At all.

Could be coincidence for sure, But I am going to continue freezing my water now
Even if it drops only a few%, it might still be worth it for me. I feel it is.

### JanSz — 2019-03-20T17:35:42+0200

Inger
get one of this thermometers or something real close
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000E8DM5S/ref=oh\_aui\_search\_asin\_title?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000E8DM5S/ref=oh_aui_search_asin_title?ie=UTF8&psc=1)

The idea is to cool water real close to freezing point
but
still to have liquid only water.

after you take a water out of freezer and shake it
you will get tiny amount of ice (may be hard to see it)
so you will not loose (practically) any water volume.

We are talking of taking out about 10 heavy atoms out of a mix of million.
anything within
38.84°F=3.8°C
and
32°F=0°C
will work, but closer to zero C will work better.
OTOH
if you freeze your water solid, you are messing up the process.

Here is my plan everyday to get the right type of water in the right place made by your body.

Wake up naturally at sunrise and get outside grounding doing burpies as nude as possible. Kiniki shorts or nude. Then lay on the ground looking at the sunrise. Rub down with aged Orin. Then cold water therapy. Wash really well in outside cold water shower before drying off and getting dressed.
Fast for 23hrs per day, loop all your Orin for about 12hrs. From sunrise to sunset.
While at work Look at the sun as much as possible through the day, right through to sunset. If possible grounding walking barefoot whenever you can.
Get home for 5pm 1hr eating window. One plate meal. High in good fat, seafood and simple salads.
Dry fast from 6pm to 6am. Store any matrix water you pee out from now on for body rubs the next morning.
After sundown wear blueblockers, and a hoodie to protect skin from artificial lights and EMFs.
Get to sleep around 10pm, because an hour before midnight is worth 2 after.

# Thread: Stephen’s Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stephen%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.22620/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 45
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Stephen W — 2018-12-06T01:13:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stephen%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.22620/post-257346>

Thanks in advance for taking the time to read my journal. I appreciate any help.

My Quest? To see if I can reverse my dust mite allergy and nose breathe with ease.

I’m 35, male, I would say I look fit and healthy but something is off, with my main symptom I’ve delt with all my life is a blocked nose. Not really a runny nose, just inflamed (swollen turbinates) but for sure always some crusty mucus, and yellow in colour usually with clear.

I live on the Sunshine Coast Queensland Australia this past 5 years. Was born and grew up a couple hours west of here.

Always a mouth breather, However this last year I forced myself to breathe through my nose nearly 24/7 because of the research I had done on the dangers of chronic mouth breathing. This was one of the hardest things I’ve done in my life. The suffocating feeling is not fun, but I know it’s more of an intolerance to Co2 build up (mouth breathing all my life through out my breathing centre) than true suffocating, and my research on Buteyko breathing convinced me to stick it out, and the suffocating feeling would go away once I got used to tolerating more carbon dioxide in my body.
And Buteyko was right, I slowly got used to it.

However, my nose is far from clear breathing. The turbinates constantly swell and are swollen with the slightest sensations, of smell, wind in the face, eating or drinking. But one of the worst times the insides of my nose swells is when I lay down. My body seems like it can’t constrict the blood flow fast enough as I lay down. For it to unblock, I need to keep my mouth shut, start suffocating, and the adrenaline all of a sudden shrinks the internals in my nose and I can nose breathe again, ok.

The reason I have this problem? I had a recent allergy skin prick test done and I have a very significant allergy to dust mites. I believe I’ve had this allergy all my life because I remember getting the test done as a boy and although I can’t recall much, my mum said she thinks it was dust mites, and I went on to have allergy shots for a year or so later (obviously with no luck).

So, having this allergy all my life, constant exposure, I see why now I’m having other troubles now. My adrenals must have taken a serious hit over these years right?

I don’t really understand it all, but I have problems also such as slight autonomic dysfunction. I think I have a slow sympathetic response namely because 1. My nose swells when I lay (sympathetic vasoconstriction doesn’t kick in) 2. I get a 30bpm heart rate jump upon standing from lying (another vasoconstriction sympathetic problem, like POTS?) 3. Morning stools very soft to runny. Needing to go to the toilet for a number 2 is normally what wakes me (again, this is probably a parasympathetic nervous system response due to lack of sympathetic response) 4. If I drink Alcohol, it will swell the turbinates in my nose (I would say because alcohol relaxes you and more parasympathetic response and my sympathetic response can’t come close to keeping up). 4. The turbinates in my nose do shrink and I can breathe a lot better if I get a hit of adrenaline for example from a cold shower, holding my breath, getting a fright or getting mad, lifting heavy weights, but this only lasts a few minutes ( This is all sympathetic nervous system related).
So that’s why I feel I have an imbalance of my autonomic nervous system, and I would think this is due to my life long exposure to my dust mite allergy. And yes, I’ve tried to eliminate dust mite exposure as much as possible, but it’s impossible.
If I can get rid of this allergy, I don’t think I would have any problems. And I could finally reach my ultimate health goal, and that is to easily nose breathe.

Here is some of my history...best I can remember.

Age 5: Asthma attack

Age 13: Allergy skin prick test shows positive for dust mites and I go on allergy shots for a year or so. No changes.

Age 16: Polyps surgically removed from my nose

Age: Early 20s: Professional level motocross racing. Lots of physical training. I grew out of my asthma trouble, no wheezing at all, no puffers anymore.
However, started getting tonsillitis a lot. I’d recover, but training would always bring tonsillitis back on. It got so bad once, I was so sick, from what I remember it was a secondary infection from tonsillitis (big welt like bruises on my shins). TONSILS REMOVED at 22 years old.

Age Up to 30: Kept doing a lot of motocross racing and training, very physical. I was not getting sick as much like I used to. By the way I was still mouth breathing all along (it was normal for me).
Had a lot of injuries and broken bones at this time. Most major one was a crushed heel and broken ankle (till this day still full of metal and screws) and crushed vertebrae in my back. 3 weeks laying in hospital deconditioned me very bad. To the point of passing out trying to stand. Heart rate was hitting 200 when standing. Docs thought I may have a clot in my lung, but after the scans, all was clear. Just deconditioned bad in 3 weeks! Fit as a fiddle to nothing in 3 weeks.

Ok so here I am now 35 years old, annoying nose that I can barely breathe through. I recently had another skin pick test done as I said earlier to show significant dust mite allergy.

I found Jack Kruse through Wim Hofs Facebook group. I wanted to try the Wim Hof method to try and reverse my dust mite allergy. Jack’s views (or nature’s view) makes more sense to follow than Wims explanations, so here I am.

I’ve been trying to cold adapt for a few months Wim Hofs way, but I’m getting no progress really. At this stage I am doing just cold baths at 18°C for 20min a day, but I still shiver like hell. Not good with the cold. I was doing 5°C for shorter time, but still wasn’t adapting.

I’ve never eaten seafood my whole life until the last 2 weeks after finding Jack’s work. I think this seafood thing has been my missing link. Hopefully this will allow me to cold adapt when my cells become stronger? If they are not already screwed beyond repair?

The big question I’m really wanting answered is....
Will it be possible to reverse my dust mite allergy?

If know one knows, I’m going to find out...

I’m following Jack’s protocol, let’s hope something happens. I will keep you all posted.

EDIT: Mother has Hashimotos

Maternal haplotype H1b
(Other info in comments)

Thanks
Stephen

### Stephen W — 2019-01-09T12:42:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stephen%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.22620/post-259833>

Been doing cold baths last couple weeks. However my cold intolerance still sucks and I’ve only been doing approximately 25-26°C (78f). Quite a Few shivers still going on though in the first 10min, then kind of less shivering for the next 20min. Sometimes nice feeling with no shivers for 10min or so.

Doing this twice per day for a total of 1 hour.
Temp under tongue is around 35.5 to 36 degrees C (approximately 96f) during the bath. However I have had it at 36.6 c one time after I was at the beach. I handled the cold bath easy that day. Don’t know what my body temp should be?

Today I grounded my bed (earthing undersheet to grounding rod out the window) and an earthing mat on my balcony! Our living area is upstairs 2nd floor. Multimeter test (putting on my body) shows my voltage drops when I get on my bed now and when I stand on the mat! Cool!

Been getting tons of sun. From early 5:30am onwards all times of the day. Bare feet walking the dog in the park. Getting quite tanned up!

40% of food is seafood I reckon now.

Dropped coffee for 5 days now, replaced with chicory.

Drinking lots of Icelandic water, costing a lot but who gives a fuck, we’re talking about health here

What really sucks though, is my nose is still an issue. Nothing has changed. The turbinates just swell all the damn time blocking my nose up.
Especially when I lay down being the most annoying. It does clear after suffocating for a few minutes. What’s the go??!!

I absolutely know this is a neuro or nervous system issue. For example, as soon as I have a couple alcohol drinks (which I haven’t been doing much lately) and I feel that relaxed feeling, my nose swells shut. When I shine a light in my nose I can see the turbinates are completely swollen shutting off my nasal passage.

I can temporarily shrink them by holding my breath until adrenaline kicks in from suffocating and it makes the suckers shrink. And I can nose breathe quite easy... But only for a few minutes. And they swell again. And this is very uncomfortable to keep doing. This is what I have to do most of the day. My nose blocks up not just from alcohol, but from other things too like food and cold water. My nose is just about closed unless I’m moving and doing things, it’s much better.

The other way I make them shrink is to produce adrenaline with heavy weights in the gym. Get the sympathetic nervous system going. Or even get in the ice bath! But it only last temporarily, and the turbinates start puffing up again.

So it’s definitely a sympathetic/parasympathetic problem. I just don’t know if it’s something I’ve been born with and may never change it. I’ve had this issue as long as I can remember. It’s so frustrating. And again, I do have a dust mite allergy but this seems to maybe have escalated since my early teens to something such as maybe now vasomotor rhinitis..

Also, if there is a low pressure system around, my turbinates swell badly as well. It’s like they are flogged out and can’t keep constricted...

If anyone knows if there are articles that Jack covered involving nasal turbinates issue and the sympathetic/parasympathetic/adrenaline or similar please let me know.

Oh and I got blood test done yesterday to check some basic stuff. Should have results in a week.
Thanks

### Stephen W — 2019-01-17T05:01:58+0200

Some Labs are in! If anyone is kind enough to help me decipher this that would be much appreciated. Local doc says keep living a healthy life haha that doesn’t explain my dust mite allergy and my cold intolerance!

### Stephen W — 2019-01-19T23:33:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stephen%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.22620/post-260834>

I grounded my bed with an earthing sheet to an outside grounding rod about 2 weeks ago and my neck and lower back pain has become off the charts! So sore for no particular reason. I mean I’ve injured my back pretty bad when I was racing motocross back in the day (2 crushed vertebrae on 2 separate occasions) but I kind of recovered some what, but now it’s really flared up. Stiff as a board and pinching.
I’m assuming I’ve created an antenna for dirty electricity from the second floor?
I’ll disconnect and see how I go. I live in town on the Sunshine Coast Queensland Australia (Coolum)

### Stephen W — 2019-01-22T08:50:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stephen%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.22620/post-260990>

Some big inroads for me lately regarding CT! After going back and doing 80-90°C water because of all the shivering that I get, the last couple days I’ve gone back to 10-13°c water and can so far do 9min without shivering, and without shivering after! Might sound like nothing to some, but it’s massive for me. I’ve had serious cold intolerance since trying to do Wim Hof stuff 6 months ago.

What do I put it down to? Well I’ve religiously seen every sun rise every morning gounded with minimal clothing. I get LOTS of sun now and am surprised at how much I can handle now and not get burnt. Nicely tanned. I eat seafood 3 times a day. Still using blue blockers at night...even though everyone thinks I’m weird and only going through a fad. It’s here for life!
But one thing I noticed is still my low body temp, so I started upping my Celtic salt intake a few days ago in hopes of getting in more iodine and it seems to be working. I seem to be up .5°c more at times now. So actually today I’ve cautiously started supplementing with a potassium iodine supplement I have and I’ll monitor carefully and see how I go. Only small amounts.

### Stephen W — 2019-03-30T10:43:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stephen%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.22620/post-266827>

I took about 1 month off doing CT as I didn’t feel I was getting anywhere. I was struggling with the cold shivering a lot after minimal exposure. During the month off I pumped up my sun exposure to 15000IU per day (using Dminder app).
I’ve just gone back to CT now and am surprised how much better my cold tolerance is!
5min at 4.5°C with nearly no shivering afterwards. Previously that would have had me shivering for 1.5-2 hours later! Massive change. Get in the god damn sun!
However, I still haven’t fixed my main issue, but I’ll keep at it.

then check it for major hormones,

cortisol fraction is 4.26% by weight
The rest of the hormones are tiny fractions.

That picture makes me think of DHEAs as a (Water Tower) in the city.
And it is going to be hard to flush toilet if Water Tower is empty or even just low.

Indeed cortisol levels are super important to continue on living
and the body will rob anything to get it.
But **you will get nowhere if you forget about DHEAs.**

I'm adding some black lights based on [@Jim Laird](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20597/) suggestion.
Was going to get these ones: [https://www.1000bulbs.com/product/86203/F-40T12BL.html](https://www.1000bulbs.com/product/86203/F-40T12BL.html)

With the lights I have 3 sections of switches. Front, middle and back. The middle section I usually leave off all the time except when playing dodgeball with my youth clients.

I was thinking of either alternating the black lights with the regular in the front and back, or just putting them in the middle section and taking some of the regular lights out in the front or back sections.

Thoughts? Would black lights be the best for this atmosphere?
