Hi Christine!

I wanted to write to say thank you for sharing your story and journey with us. Your story and energy is just really incredible, I’m so happy you have been finding relief. I’m a newbie here and was addicted to [@Sean Waters](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19464/) Journal and was always really drawn to your responses and energy on his page. Your perspective is on such a spiritual level that I can really appreciate. So I came over to check yours out too! And wow, I can’t even imagine all you and your family have gone through, but I’m glad you found your way here and getting help. It seems you also have an amazing supportive partner who loves you and that helps tremendously!

I see the last post here was in July of this year, so how’ve you been? Any updates? I hope you’ve been well. ❤️

### 5G Canary — 2020-10-31T21:35:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christines-journal-ehs-mctd-5gcanary.22624/post-293170>

Thanks for your kind words Jackie! I was drawn to Sean and his Journal too... he is a very gifted writer with a very beautiful and rare soul. Glad to hear you got to meet with him and attend the member event. I would imagine it was magical! Hopefully I will be able to make the next event.

Yes...I have been blessed with an amazing partner.... and husband of over 30 years. The secret to any relationship is always in the mirror. Everything we do mirrors back..... Love obviously is the most important. But I don’t think any relationship can last if you stop believing in one another. My first consult with Jack was when we had stopped believing in one another. What I was experiencing seemed so crazy and far fetched... my husband was having a hard time believing. He could not step outside of his engineer brain. Which mirrored and I stopped believing in myself and in his ability to help me. Jack was the first and only Doctor that could understand what I was experiencing and what I was trying to communicate. He was instrumental in opening both our minds and eyes. Once we got the environment right it was much easier for me to pinpoint what was or is affecting me.

As far as an update.... We found out that our old neighborhood not only was a 5G test area but the water and land that our houses were built on was extremely toxic too. Erin Brockovich and a team of lawyers have swarmed into the area.... [https://www.wcnc.com/article/news/investigations/erin-brockovich-huntersville-mooresville-cancer-cluster-investigation/275-5cc18dd7-cc94-435e-9fd5-37c61ac2bfd1](https://www.wcnc.com/article/news/investigations/erin-brockovich-huntersville-mooresville-cancer-cluster-investigation/275-5cc18dd7-cc94-435e-9fd5-37c61ac2bfd1)
Hopefully they will be able to help fix the environment. There have been a lot of teens getting very rare and serious conditions... so sad. If I was to guess my crappy DNA, 5G, toxic water, bad EMF’s, and childhood revaccination along with the flu shot that the school I worked at required, all pushed me over the edge.

I am doing remarkably well now... thanks to Jack! I take no medications, no vitamins and no doctors. I use Matt’s blue blockers.... [https://raoptics.com/collections/glasses](https://raoptics.com/collections/glasses) . I use the magnetico.... [https://magneticosleep.com/](https://magneticosleep.com/). I don’t use ANY purchased bottle water...After lots of research I don’t trust any of them. I have a RO unit and only drink and shower in water that I filter myself. I use a magnetic stirrer to create a vortex before drinking..... [https://www.amazon.com/gp/aw/d/B088SF2CZP?psc=1&ref=ppx\_pop\_mob\_b\_asin\_image](https://www.amazon.com/gp/aw/d/B088SF2CZP?psc=1&ref=ppx_pop_mob_b_asin_image) . I don’t do well with the red light but my family uses it. I like fire, hot tub and cold tub. I like to listen to my body and eat what I crave... I follow my intuition as much as possible. I try to follow Mother Nature’s rules. I still can’t use anything plugged into the grid. I can use stuff that is plugged in but only if I am not holding it, or not holding it for very long.... weird I know! So I use battery operated stuff... vacuum, blower, etc. I still have good days and bad days. I also still struggle in certain stores with certain lighting but the reaction is much less.

I recently saw a Bob Marley quote.... “_You say you love Rain, but you use an umbrella to walk under it. You say you love Sun, but you seek shelter when it is shining. You say you love Wind, but when it comes you close your windows. So that's why I'm scared when you say you love me._” .... No wonder Mother Nature didn’t think I loved her.... kind of goes back to that mirror. I wasn’t loving or believing in her.

I read your journal.... it sounds like you are on the right path. I noticed you didn’t mention the smart meter? Definitely want that removed and changed to analog. Also, since you work with animals... I would be curious if any of the animals you are close with show similar ailments? The dog I am the closest too... completely mirrors me. When I was the sickest everyone told me to put her down... but I believed in her! She recently turned 15 and is in better shape now then when we got her. She used to live at the vet.... I barely take her anymore!

I barely take her anymore!

Thanks for the reply and update!
Really interesting and awful about your old neighborhood and area...I’m sure you are immensely grateful for getting out when you did. I’m hopeful that if Erin B is on it, things will be settled, but i don’t think any money could ever suffice for losing your health. It makes me wonder if the 5G is what accelerated the cancers and issues for more of the younger people who were getting sick. Usually toxic water and such will take years of build up, but if they are all getting zapped too....

loved the Bob Marley quote, I’m a big fan of his music and pearls of wisdom on love and living life. “Don’t worry, be happy” whenever I need a ray of sunshine, his songs are my go to. ☀️ that quote you posted and what you shared about your relationship I appreciate. It’s resonating with what I’m currently going through in my own at the moment (this I have not shared on my journal)...the believing in each other is what struck home for me today, so thank you; it’ll be something for me to think on.

I hope we are both able to make it to the next members event, would love to meet! ✨

glad you are following your instincts with nature and doing well in your new environment. I’m sure it can be frustrating sometimes to still not handle items plugged into the grid, but hey who needs to vacuum all the time anyways right? Lol
Would love to know more on your water system and the stirrer. That’s still an area in this transformation I haven’t made a deduction on yet. I use my well water and bottled spring water, but like you I really don’t like anything in a plastic bottle...unless I was bottling it myself from a fresh spring.
I don’t think we are staying in this house so not sure if I want to install a big system. I may just have the mountain valley glass jugs of spring water get delivered and just suck up the cost for that at the moment as a temporary fix.

Our house still has an analog meter so we are good there, but it’s on the outside of our bedroom wall so I have a Cornet RF meter coming in to test the house.

it’s an interesting question about the animals. The horses I work on are all at their owners barns or training facilities so I can’t really say what effects I see on them. Most barns don’t have WiFi and such, but I already see and work with the issues that we do put on our horses that aren’t natural (staying in a stall for a long time, bad food, bad tack, etc)

but you made me think about my house pets. The dog I have now is only 2.5yr and he has seemed very whiney/needy lately and I always thought it was just him being young (and a bit spoiled, lol) but I also know that we were leaving the WiFi on all day while we were gone and he was home and it makes me wonder....other than that he is healthy.

my last dog who made it to 15 years of age died from an enlarged heart. I always thought it was weird that she came down with that bc she was so healthy. But now knowing what we are learning, she would have always had WiFi on at home too and that house was a brand new house so it may have had a smart meter, I don’t know as we don’t live there anymore.
Hindsight certainly is a double edge sword...but we learn, change, embrace, adapt. ❤️

### 5G Canary — 2020-11-03T00:22:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christines-journal-ehs-mctd-5gcanary.22624/post-293204>

[@Jackie Jolie](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/25098/)
Your welcome! Yes... believing is a big deal. Doesn’t matter if it’s a spouse, child, friend, doctor etc. Our beliefs create our thoughts which shape our reality... when someone believes in you it is magnified in that mirror. I can still remember years ago when I was having infertility issues the doctor that I saw said...”you will get pregnant!” He didn’t say we can try or maybe.... it was “you will!” His strong belief....created the thought in us to believe....that we could get pregnant.... it created our reality. We ended up getting pregnant not long after without even having to do much.

Here is the company I use to filter our water.... [https://www.amazon.com/stores/iSpring/page/1D54E265-E27C-47ED-8271-57FA4531484F?ref\_=ast\_bln](https://www.amazon.com/stores/iSpring/page/1D54E265-E27C-47ED-8271-57FA4531484F?ref_=ast_bln)

The owner is extremely nice and helpful if you want to call and see what he would recommend for your situation. We have the whole house treated but my daughters just do their kitchen sink and shower. If you are doing cold therapy with an outside hose... you can purchase a filter for the hose too. Remember your skin is your largest organ so you don’t want to soak in toxic water.

I saw this a couple years ago and thought you might like it.... she is a Vet and has some great info and is very perceptive on how the horses all affect one another too.

### Marko Pollo — 2020-11-11T17:24:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christines-journal-ehs-mctd-5gcanary.22624/post-293296>

I read n a book on dowsing that places that attract cats can be less optimal for diurnal creatures. The presence of underground streams and stray currents can have an effect. The book above more talks about how to reduce and isolate from stray currents from transformers, pumps, power lines, buildins and electrical equipment. Location of electrical panels and proper grounding are key. A barn or pen between the transformer and electrical equipment can be trouble, depending on conductivity of the soil. Putting the electrical service panel on the side of the barn closer to the transformer allows currents to follow path of least resistance. Small stray currents on throughs and feeders can have Nonlinear effects on animals. The same principals should be used when designing our living spaces.
A thought I had was, if a location had a problem with stray currents, they could mitigated by putting a drip line around the perimeter. The moist soil would provide a low resistance path around and away from property. Planting a mix of annual grasses and perinial trees along moist perimeter will build water cycle to keep ground moist, provide habitat, block noise,light pollution and emf and give abundant privacy. I have had great luck using Cypress trees, but haven't tested anything to prove my theory.
If your environment sucks, fix it!

I dont see many chemtrails nowadays. i used to see a lot as a kid, but now I think the skies are too grey here to notice. Really? They are spraying black stuff? During the first lock down last May, all the roads in my area had a fine white dust on them - it was really peculiar. Trees are dying out here too. Ive spent a lot of time in woodlands in the last 12 months, and there are ALOT of dead/rotten trees. So much so that I decided to google it. Turns out 80% of our native Ash trees are dying out because of a fungus brought over from asia 30 years ago. Theyre calling the disease "Ash Die Off". Im wondering if its EMF related... Its very sad because the Ash is our third most common tree over here in the UK, and it was used throughout history for many different things. Also considered sacred by the pre-christian people of these lands.

### Matt Fowler — 2021-02-03T20:47:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christines-journal-ehs-mctd-5gcanary.22624/post-295204>

>
> I think the ash grew back in the void as the forests were cut for ship building and colonial expansion. Not saying fungus isn't killing trees, but I believe the effects of man are way worse and the landscape we see isn't natural.

Yes, thats very true - the landscape is totally bastardised. And the fungus killing Ash trees is also a result of man - the narrative is that it was transported to the UK on contaminated Asian Ash Sapling imports. They're say it has been present here since the late 80's. And if i am not mistaken, that is when wireless technologies started to increase in strength and prevalence - I am wondering if the fungus proliferation is due to the microwave/rf radiation. The Ash tree is native to these parts and was used for pretty much every job that required strong flexible wood - from the mighty longbow all the way to windsor chairs and Morris Traveller parts. Its also purported to be the tree upon which Odin sacrificed himself in Norse mythology (as written about in the Poetic Edda). Personally, it is a tree thats close to my heart, and it will be a huge loss when it has gone.

[https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/tr...ases/key-tree-pests-and-diseases/ash-dieback/](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/trees-woods-and-wildlife/tree-pests-and-diseases/key-tree-pests-and-diseases/ash-dieback/)

### 5G Canary — 2021-06-28T21:12:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christines-journal-ehs-mctd-5gcanary.22624/post-300413>

Rainbows require sunlight and moisture in the air but it’s the magic angle of 42 degrees that causes rainbows to exist.

As the sun rises in a morning sky, a rainbow continually descends. Eventually, only the top arc is visible before it sets below the horizon with a rising sun. Rainbows cannot appear when the sun is high in the sky because the anti-solar point has descended well below the horizon.

[https://inlightofnature.com/how-rainbows-form/](https://inlightofnature.com/how-rainbows-form/)

### 5G Canary — 2021-07-14T02:33:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christines-journal-ehs-mctd-5gcanary.22624/post-301104>

It is wise to remember your skin is your biggest organ and drinks in the water it is bathed in.... hot water is even worse because it opens the pores and you also breathe in the toxins.

I’ve always felt you get the best information about your zip code from your local vet and your hairdresser. The last visit I had with my hairdresser completely shocked me. She still works in my old neighborhood... I like to go down a few times a year to visit with her. I can still remember when I first got sick and she shared how a large number of her clients were coming down with cancer and autoimmune conditions.... we both noted the connection.

This last visit she shared that three different clients have children going through puberty... they have found out from their doctor that they have female parts on the outside but male parts on the inside. I immediately told her NOT to wash my hair... the lightbulb moment and look on her face said it all.

### 5G Canary — 2021-07-14T03:55:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christines-journal-ehs-mctd-5gcanary.22624/post-301114>

[https://www.wcnc.com/amp/article/ne...tion/275-5cc18dd7-cc94-435e-9fd5-37c61ac2bfd1](https://www.wcnc.com/amp/article/news/investigations/erin-brockovich-huntersville-mooresville-cancer-cluster-investigation/275-5cc18dd7-cc94-435e-9fd5-37c61ac2bfd1)

They are claiming it is coal ash. I am guessing it is Atrazine but there is also so much in the water now it would be hard to test... how do you test for things you don’t know even exist?

We lived there 17 years but unfortunately it wasn’t blatantly obvious until the last few years that it was definitely the environment. When I became very ill... I kept tasting like a sweet metal taste in the water. I kept making my husband test it but it always came back fine. I instinctively stopped wanting to shower because of how the water felt touching my skin and I hated how it tasted like metal if it touched your lips. I remember always getting upset with him because he loved taking scalding hot showers and steaming up the bathroom and I hated how it smelled and tasted. I also learned the hard way that if lead is in the water and you boil it... it actually can increase the concentration.

Clean water is a must... especially if you have small children or pets and you are bathing them in it.

### 5G Canary — 2021-10-15T00:15:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christines-journal-ehs-mctd-5gcanary.22624/post-304878>

> [Ravi Sandhu said:](/goto/post?id=304875)
>
> I have seen this video before and I can't believe this unfolded spontaneously, it's incredible.

It is pretty crazy... but very cool!

I read your journal... congratulations on your accomplishments. I also read that you took a short break from working out. I went through a period of extreme fatigue and exercise intolerance when I was my sickest. I was that person that absolutely loved working out... so it really bothered me that I wasn’t able too. I also really missed that runners high. I found I was drawn to playing games and the more challenging the better. I drove my family crazy because they knew I always wanted to play a game... chess, poker, checkers, scrabble etc. I read an article about chess players and was shocked to read you can burn up to 6,000 calories per day while playing chess. That was all it took for my family to embrace it... Lol! Obviously we aren’t playing at that level... but maybe someday! But the best part is both my daughters thinking improved exponentially... they both are smart but I’ve seen their thinking evolve as they play. They are very logical thinkers now... which at this point in time is critical. I also think it brings people closer together since you aren’t using a phone.

I am much healthier now so I am able to workout but I like to alternate working out with games. I find it’s a fun balance and it’s definitely is a big dopamine hit when you win a strategic game. We try to play outside since everything is better outside.

### Sean Waters — 2021-10-15T17:21:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christines-journal-ehs-mctd-5gcanary.22624/post-304903>

Ah god I hear that one - Mixed Martial Arts, Boxing and Football... definitely some of the biggest sacrifices to get well. It's hard when those activities are part of your identity and who you are.

I'm looking to take up Golf....

We also forget that with just 3 lbs of mass our brain takes up 20% of the body's energy intake. When we heal, our most energy demanding tissues (that have the highest Magnetic Field) the Brain and the Heart of course, will pull the Lion's share of the O2 and DHA to it and get the benefits of the healing first.

Unfortunately, if we have tissues we want to heal faster, like the Gut or the Thyroid or the Bones - they have to wait for seconds. It can seem like slow progress is being made, but as I look back over the last 2 years in the tropics, the healing has been totally non-linear, the transformation of my Brain and Sleep has been incredible. Only recently has my Redox in my Gut started to come along... Jack said it would take 3 years.

Thank you for your wonderful post, and your kind words, I really, really appreciate it! I am feeling so good these days and sometimes I feel a bit overwhelmed that I am getting functions that I SORELY MISSED back. ### 5G Canary — 2021-11-15T19:43:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christines-journal-ehs-mctd-5gcanary.22624/post-306241>

I love games and it’s football season so I thought we could play a game.... the “Game of Life!”

First rule.. you can’t make a mistake! That’s not my rule but Mother Natures. What does “you can’t make a mistake” mean? Does it mean you can’t afford to make a mistake in this game of Life? No... it means Mother Nature says there literally are no mistakes. Does a tree make a mistake when it grows? No. For example, three trees... one in the sun, one in full shade and one next to a smart meter. The one in the sun will flourish, the one in the shade will live, but the one by the smart meter will rapidly decline. None of these trees made a mistake... they all just received very different information from the light environment in which they grew. Unfortunately for the smart meter tree he got forced into man’s game. But unlike trees, we have the ability to very easily change our light environment. When you learn this rule the game becomes much easier to understand because you can’t make a mistake... if you are playing by Mother Natures rules. Playing by mans rules... “you can’t make a mistake” takes on a completely different meaning.

So, if life is a game... what ball do you use? The electric ball of Light made of toxic chemicals provided by man to trick, deceive and take the Soul? Or the natural ball of Light that Mother Nature provides showering you with energy and light to fill the Soul? What field do you play on? The artificial chemical field created by man to block and deceive the information you should naturally be receiving? Or the Magnetic field of dreams created by the Universe to provide information and manifest what you need and when you need it?

Are you playing Man’s manmade game or Mother Nature’s natural game? One is a faster version of the game and one is a more random natural play. When you choose to play fast...is it winning if you didn’t really comprehend the rules? What are the rules? In my opinion the biggest rule is to remember we are nature made and not man made. Play by Mother Nature’s rules and you can’t make a mistake! Mother Nature created you to multiply naturally from Love.... to create a winning team!

### 5G Canary — 2022-04-12T02:54:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christines-journal-ehs-mctd-5gcanary.22624/post-311986>

[
](https://youtu.be/YbuFLUJ89Kw)

I love this mashup... the timing is perfect! It’s a mashup of Zeppelin and the 1941 movie called “You’ll Never Get Rich.” The title stems from an old Army song... there are at least two versions of the lyrics with "You'll never get rich, you son of a bitch" often replaced with "You'll never get rich by digging a ditch.” They are always pumping us with subliminal messaging. I thought it was very cleverly done- Fred Astaire’s character complains that she isn’t following what everyone else is doing. She needs to be a trained silly dancing monkey because they don’t want any monkey’s not following orders. She reminds me of Einstein’s quote “The woman who follows the crowd will usually go no further than the crowd. The woman who walks alone is likely to find herself in places no one has ever been before.”

My kind of bird...

### 5G Canary — 2022-04-13T01:49:08+0300

That’s funny my daughter played soccer for 11 years and her song was AC/DC’s Thunderstruck. Hope your daughter rocks it out of the park tonight!

Time goes by so fast at times... that same soccer daughter of mine just announced she is getting married this next year.. 3/23/2023 she picked her lucky soccer numbers as her date. I should share this Einstein advice with her fiancé.. Albert Einstein was asked on his 50 wedding anniversary, “To what do you attribute the success of your marriage?” Professor Einstein offered this profound response. “When we first got married, we made a pact. It was this: In our life together, it was decided I would make all of the big decisions, and my wife would make all of the little decisions. For fifty years we held true to that agreement. I believe that is the reason for the success in our marriage. However, the strange thing is, that in fifty years, there hasn’t been one big decision. Lol!

Anatomy Lab... and the smell of formaldehyde!! I hated that smell and I remember having the only cat that was shot in the chest... kinda creepy. Guessing you not being able to smell would definitely be an advantage in that lab. That was a fun class though and I imagine your daughter will do great!

### 5G Canary — 2022-09-21T00:40:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christines-journal-ehs-mctd-5gcanary.22624/post-315900>

This summer in particular we got to enjoy a yard filled with an obscene amount of hummingbirds. Mama’s and babies and the always entertaining territorial male beak fights. Not sure why we had so many this year... maybe the ridiculous amount of plants and flowers I indulged in planting this summer or the many feeders my husband continued to install all summer long. But either way I am very grateful to have had them here. Still have quite a few that haven’t left yet. We even got them to feed from us this year... that was very cool!

But, as I say goodbye to summer and sense the seasons change I’m always saddened to see the hummingbirds leave... but they are a _**pivotal**_ reminder that it’s time again for me to **_pivot_**. Spend a day observing these magical little powerhouses and you’ll learn the 3 P’s!

**_Point_** the way... **_Pilot_** the way... **_Pivot_** away... _repeat_.

When you point the way, some may follow but most will not... that’s okay. You cannot force people to be courageous. You can only show courage by leading with it. Which is why you must also be willing to pilot the way. Show your courage by flying out of tradition and into the unknown...a leap of courage. _Open the cage_!

**_And then, the most crucial step of all....pivot._**

You pivot... our you become stuck. You pivot to prevent suffocating within that comfort zone. You pivot to prevent close-mindedness and stubbornness. You pivot so that you may remain flexible, adaptable, and antifragile.

Otherwise, your destiny stagnates. The “answers” you’ve found become prison bars. The cage slowly closes back around you. You become stuck in believing you’ve “made it” or that you’re “enlightened” or that your “way” is the only way. In short... you become dogmatic.

**_See the way, guide the way, then change the way. Do this again and again, with curiosity, humor, imagination.... and the magic and miracles of the universe become endless.

### 5G Canary — 2022-09-21T00:46:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christines-journal-ehs-mctd-5gcanary.22624/post-315902>

Most people believe the most frequently washed part of the body the last two years was the hands. I believe it was the brain! Everyone is running around saying they are “woke” or “enlightened” but by who? Mother Nature should be the answer but most “wake” from technology. I recently read this and thought I’d share...

“We should go and proclaim without cease and remind people at every step of what we are: that our capacity for self-delusion has no limits and that anybody who believes anything is mistaken.”

No matter how much we think we know, there is nearly an infinite amount of information that we do not. And yet we still cling to what we think we know despite this fact. We want our beliefs to be true so badly that we believe in vain. We cling. We obsess. We attach. We unreasonably and illogically lash out when our beliefs are reasoned with, perceiving an attack that doesn’t exist; all while raising nearly insurmountable walls of cognitive dissonance.

It gets to the point that the only way around the walls is to transcend them. That’s the detached state. The more we cling to a belief, the more closeminded and inflexible we become and the more difficult it is to change our mind. Better not to cling in the first place. Better not to become attached. Best, to practice healthy, mindful detachment from the jump.

The more we practice mindful detachment, the less our mindless attachment has any sway. The more we practice mindful attachment, the looser the clamps of cognitive dissonance become. Over time the walls crumble. The view clears up. Clarity manifests. We see with new eyes. We see how our beliefs were a hinderance to self-improvement.

There is a quote... “belief is a wound that knowledge heals.” Indeed. The knowledge gained in the detached state transforms the wound of belief into the sacred wound of flexible mindfulness. We are then capable of entertaining a thought without accepting it.

### Sean Waters — 2022-09-21T07:54:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christines-journal-ehs-mctd-5gcanary.22624/post-315916>

> [5G Canary said:](/goto/post?id=315900)
>
> This summer in particular we got to enjoy a yard filled with an obscene amount of hummingbirds. Mama’s and babies and the always entertaining territorial male beak fights. Not sure why we had so many this year... maybe the ridiculous amount of plants and flowers I indulged in planting this summer or the many feeders my husband continued to install all summer long. But either way I am very grateful to have had them here. Still have quite a few that haven’t left yet. We even got them to feed from us this year... that was very cool!
>
> But, as I say goodbye to summer and sense the seasons change I’m always saddened to see the hummingbirds leave... but they are a _**pivotal**_ reminder that it’s time again for me to **_pivot_**. Spend a day observing these magical little powerhouses and you’ll learn the 3 P’s!
>
> **_Point_** the way... **_Pilot_** the way... **_Pivot_** away... _repeat_.
>
> When you point the way, some may follow but most will not... that’s okay. You cannot force people to be courageous. You can only show courage by leading with it. Which is why you must also be willing to pilot the way. Show your courage by flying out of tradition and into the unknown...a leap of courage. _Open the cage_!
>
> **_And then, the most crucial step of all....pivot._**
>
> You pivot... our you become stuck. You pivot to prevent suffocating within that comfort zone. You pivot to prevent close-mindedness and stubbornness. You pivot so that you may remain flexible, adaptable, and antifragile.
>
> Otherwise, your destiny stagnates. The “answers” you’ve found become prison bars. The cage slowly closes back around you. You become stuck in believing you’ve “made it” or that you’re “enlightened” or that your “way” is the only way. In short... you become dogmatic.
>
> **_See the way, guide the way, then change the way. Do this again and again, with curiosity, humor, imagination.... and the magic and miracles of the universe become endless.
>
> [View attachment 22546](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/22546/)_**

_"You pivot... our you become stuck. You pivot to prevent suffocating within that comfort zone. You pivot to prevent close-mindedness and stubbornness. You pivot so that you may remain flexible, adaptable, and anti-fragile.

Otherwise, your destiny stagnates. The “answers” you’ve found become prison bars. The cage slowly closes back around you. You become stuck in believing you’ve “made it” or that you’re “enlightened” or that your “way” is the only way. In short... you become dogmatic.

See the way, guide the way, then change the way. Do this again and again, with curiosity, humor, imagination.... and the magic and miracles of the universe become endless."_

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) ....... **she said it a lot more poetically than I did.

What I wrote on my journal last night was sheer frustration. Lock it, censor any further posts... It's time is done anyway.
**
It was purely coincidental that I finished it yesterday after 2 years of trying to build it and we had our argument. I decided to post it to try and make a good day out of a bad day, but I couldn't help write those words because I was angry with you, not just yesterday, but for a long time...... you already know this.
**
I'd rather I hadn't have done it. But, what is done is done.

There was absolutely zero intention to release that off the back of our argument and use it as some kind of "boost"... _**Believe me, the amount of people I've pissed off on this site, I won't be building any following here I can tell you that.**_

The post made it seem otherwise, calculated... and that is not what I am trying to be, but maybe it is currently who I am - reactionary and unhinged.

Jack, I'm a scumbag from Birmingham. A Football Hooligan. I grew up fighting... fighting my stepdad, fighting my friends, enemies. This is part of my past that still interferes with my current life.... "not everyone is your opponent, Sean", a wise woman once told me.

I'm doing my best to change. I've failed that this week.

But, I see the same things in you. Your past, still interferes with your current life, you're still fighting.... this is why I believe we're entangled.

We make aberrated decisions based on our past that ripple across our surfaces unless those inner vibrations are changed.

We have to dive deep, over and over, to find the pearls in the ocean.. just because you haven't found them yet, doesn't mean they aren't there.
**
**You need people to challenge you, you've always said it. That is why I reached out to you in January to meet 1 to 1.

I don't know if someone around you deleted it before you could see it... but I never got a reply from you. I wanted to do this in person, not online.

You're very best friend told me in October 2020 that he thinks I am the one that needs to teach you how to be a good father. What I said that day in front of everybody is True.

But, the way that I was treated that weekend and other occasions, by yourself and others was not ok. No Friend, or "Son", needs to be treated like that. Not only that time, I witness some poor behaviour, that I knew was not serving you. Until it got to the point where I had to speak up. Speaking up wasn't allowed, neither by you or your circle. This went against everything that you taught me....... but still I defended you.

You've continued to carry out this behaviour, the aberrations, and maybe I'm a fool for believing otherwise..... perhaps that is why Christine and my girlfriend are insinuating strongly, that I walk away right now, that I leave you be......... but that's what everyone else does. I'm not everyone else.

Look at what I wrote to EWO... "he's gotten better", defending you right before I just said fuck it and wrote what I wrote. If I continue to defend you when I know you are fucking up, _then I am lying to you... and we agreed in Poland.... No Lies._

I had other members tell me, what they witnessed that weekend in Destin, and they personally approached me and were in utter shock that I didn't react aggressively. Trust me, if you were anyone else - you'd have gotten an introduction to how I used to do things in Birmingham.**

**But, I saw you were actually protecting the person you love even though you knew I was right. People are highly aware of the toxicity within your close circles, it's well known by all including you, but you choose to ignore it.

Y**ou're in need _of a BIG frequency shift._** You needn't feel alone or afraid. I'm going through it too. You're loved by me and loved by many.

**Anyway, regardless of the what you think, I am forever grateful and remain dedicated to being your student and your friend.

As I said, I'm not going anywhere. The divine timing will come.

****

### 5G Canary — 2023-03-27T19:57:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christines-journal-ehs-mctd-5gcanary.22624/post-320271>

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) Thanks for having us on the Q&A and for being you!

Best investment we ever made was joining your forum. I know both myself and my husband would not be this healthy if it weren’t for you and the forum. You facilitated in rekindling our relationship with Mother Nature. There is a wealth of information available to us through the quilt, blogs, series and combined with the Q&A’s it pushes us daily to dive deeper. I have to say there isn’t a day that goes by that I haven’t learned something new or have been inspired by either yourself and/or someone on this forum.

We are born with our mind, body and soul. Unfortunately, for those of us that go through the programming we tend to slowly lose the mind. We lose control of our thinking because we become so disconnected from nature’s web. When we no longer control our mind we tend to become just body focusing only on diet, exercise, illness, pills, what we have, drive etc.... this also makes it very easy to lose the soul. It also keeps us the perfect dancing monkey because we don’t even realize we lost our ability to think. We slowly become more shallow and learn to fear the deep because we except what we’ve been taught as the truth. What exactly is the deep? **_The deep is where we face our fears and search for the truth. It’s where we recapture the control of our mind and find our soul.

_**
You’ve taught me that we aren’t meant to do just what we love or what is easy for us. We all have things that we are naturally good at... it’s learning and doing the things we think we aren’t capable of and/or are afraid to do/experience. With free thinking our imaginations have no limits.

On a side note...
I enjoyed your epilogue to your new book. Our bodies are like an ancient text, an ancient map, a treasure chest filled with stories of earth and sky. The story is in our bones, our blood and our cells. I don’t know why but for some reason your epilogue reminded me of this painting called History of the House.

We are all connected and just like this painting depicts we are all stories... and just like an apartment building each story is a story within a story connecting together to create one large house.

The House...

From the artist...

-   We live in the present, but memories retain a world from the past.
-   There is always a sense of wanting to keep the elusive moment from fading away.
-   Using a single image, we set a trap to retain the invisible, the no longer tangible.

A giant book opens onto a stately presentable New York house. Built perhaps during the early 1900s the building’s solid façade stands out in sharp contrast to the soft facing of the book. The juxtaposition of well-worn paper with hard granite could draw an analogy to the elasticity of brain tissue and the toughness of the cranium-that human strongbox that holds our data of the past.

Human memory keeps the image of Venus, who was born from the font of the sea-a well-known metaphor of purity, beauty, infinity, and inexhaustible depth. The Birth of Venus was created by Sandro Botticelli more than 500 years ago, in 1485

The house takes up only so much space in the city, but human imagination has no limits and translates the images of the past into the framework of the present. It is worth noting how the symbolism of a book is similar to the symbolism of a residential building: as a page consists of parallel lines, an apartment block consists of parallel floors. Individual letters along those lines could be linked to individual faces looking out of the building’s windows.

Also this song... in the video the books on the floor represent us... we are the stories, the trees.

### Jack Kruse — 2023-03-29T13:52:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christines-journal-ehs-mctd-5gcanary.22624/post-320304>

I am only loyal to nature these days. It was a tough lesson. Loyalty is often lauded in most circles but few people realize how it is a double-edged sword. While ostensibly loyalty is thought of as a positive trait, we are much more apt these days to hear about loyalty in the context of a "sticky situation"—loyalty to a country, dogma, or religion leading to fanatical acts of chauvinism or violence, loyalty to family or friends leading to nepotism and cronyism in government, or loyalty to co-workers or a profession leading to cover-ups of financial chicanery or unethical dealings.

The commitment one shows using loyalty, as a tool, is often rewarded with a good reputation, success, and trust. Loyalty is the fundamental element to trust and opportunities. The interesting thing is loyalty is like a coin. It has two sides. When a person commits to someone else with extreme loyalty selflessly, they often will experience a loss of personal judgment, and insight and can become a slave to poor thought and reason rather than remaining an independent thinker.

In order to achieve success using loyalty as an asset, one must learn to stop when the negative consequences outweigh the positive consequences at the present moment. Loyalty cannot be bought. It needs to be earned. This is a two-way street. If the student knows a little and thinks they know a lot more without really doing the due diligence of research have they been loyal to the teacher or themselves? **Haven’t they become a prisoner of their OWN CAGE?** Isn't this how the Dunning-Kruger effect is born? The world needs people who think differently because that kind of thinking can eventually become the actions of a sniper. One shot, One Kill, minus their own biases to combat the collateral damage of being too confident when you are very arrogant about what you're missing. _The teacher has to subtract their own biases so the student can grow once they understand how to research and think well._

### 5G Canary — 2023-06-11T15:33:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christines-journal-ehs-mctd-5gcanary.22624/post-322284>

Was musing this morning about my hummers and thought I’d share... I’ve shared before that we have a ridiculous amount of hummingbirds in the yard each year and this year is no exception. Have you ever wondered how many times a hummingbird's wings beat per second? Those little magical wonders flutter their wings at a remarkable 80 times per second. They do NOT flap their wings, they rotate them in a figure 8, which makes it even more incredible. That also enables them to go backwards in the air and to hover in one spot. **_Their movement effortlessly allowing them to go back and forth in time but only really living in the present moment at that one spot in time. In order for them to survive they have to make the most of that one moment with critical thinking._** Hummingbirds are very intelligent animals in general, with a memory span they say that rivals that of an elephant. It appears to me they remember us each year and know who keeps stocking the feeder and who plants the flowers to grow for them. But they also have their ways of trying to communicate with us. They fly about our heads to alert us to empty feeders or water that has gone bad or just to say “Good Morning!”

But for myself it’s always been about the different sounds they make. Hummingbirds can create sounds that are both vocal and non-vocal. Vocal sounds are made with the voice box but the non-vocal aerodynamic sounds are made with their wing and tail feathers. Sometimes male hummers fight for territory by dueling with their beaks and creating the clicking sound you may have heard. Have you ever noticed not only are the sounds similar to a dolphin but their look is a little similar too with the long sword like beak. They also will circle you like a dolphin with their energy, vibration and frequencies... sometimes it almost feels like they are literally healing us.

Thought I’d share this poem... the poet’s death was a little suspicious on 9/23/73

"Ode to the Hummingbird"

The famous poet Pablo Neruda wrote this beautiful poem..........
Ode to the Hummingbird
The hummingbird
in flight
is a water-spark,
an incandescent drip
of American
fire,
the jungle's
flaming resume,
a heavenly,
precise
rainbow:
the hummingbird is
an arc,
a golden
thread,
a green
bonfire!
Oh
tiny
living
lightning,
when
you hover
in the air,
you are
a body of pollen,
a feather
or hot coal,
I ask you:
What is your substance?
Perhaps during the blind age
of the Deluge,
within fertility's
mud,
when the rose
crystallized
in an anthracite fist,
and metals matriculated
each one in
a secret gallery
perhaps then
from a wounded reptile
some fragment rolled,
a golden atom,
the last cosmic scale,
a drop of terrestrial fire
took flight,
suspending your splendor,
your iridescent,
swift sapphire.
You doze
on a nut,
fit into a diminutive blossom;
you are an arrow,
a pattern,
a coat-of-arms,
honey's vibrato, pollen's ray;
you are so stouthearted--
the falcon
with his black plumage
does not daunt you:
you pirouette,
a light within the light,
air within the air.
Wrapped in your wings,
you penetrate the sheath
of a quivering flower,
not fearing
that her nuptial honey
may take off your head!
From scarlet to dusty gold,
to yellow flames,
to the rare
ashen emerald,
to the orange and black velvet
of our girdle gilded by sunflowers,
to the sketch
like
amber thorns,
your Epiphany,
little supreme being,
you are a miracle,
shimmering
from torrid California
to Patagonia's whistling,
bitter wind.
You are a sun-seed,
plumed
fire,
a miniature
flag
in flight,
a petal of silenced nations,
a syllable
of buried blood,
a feather
of an ancient heart,
submerged

### 5G Canary — 2023-07-30T15:43:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christines-journal-ehs-mctd-5gcanary.22624/post-324471>

I read this the other day and just thought I’d share...

“A great way to poison a man is to remove any hardship or struggle from his life.

To give him all the comforts and conveniences he could need.

To give him a lukewarm 9-5 that pays him just enough to be comfortable so he doesn’t feel the need to grow but wouldn’t be able to survive without it.

When you take a way a mans edge, you take away his power.
This is the moment men begin to lose meaning in their life.

They grow restless and unsettled.

We are built to fight, built to challenge, built to overcome.
Men come to life when we put ourselves in situations that require our full attention. Where we fight both physical and mental battles with ourself and prove that we can push beyond who we thought we were.

How many of you have felt despair when you were lost and directionless? When you didn’t know your mission and didn’t feel like your life had a purpose?

It’s an unsettling feeling because men need to be in a state of doing. We need to build, create, accomplish.

If you haven’t experienced these things, you have not yet fulfilled your purpose.

If you’re unhappy it’s because you haven’t tasted the pride and satisfaction of completing something difficult that you’ve set out to do.

You need to take on a mission now.

It can be leveling up at work. Attaining fluency in a foreign language. Creating a difficult piece of art. Training to fight.
Challenge yourself. Focus your mind and get it done.

This is how you gain mastery over your mental and emotional states. You won’t truly feel fulfilled as a man until you take this step.
You’re only enslaved if you can’t control your mind.”

This ^^^ reminded me a little of Jack’s explanation of why we are creative. In a bad environment creativity can pull you out of depression. Creativity I believe helps strengthen the mind and keeps us curious. In the right environment creativity, curiosity and imagination bring endless magic and miracles!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christines-journal-ehs-mctd-5gcanary.22624/post-326042>

Mastery is just as illusory as it is useful. It should be a point of view with a pivot. Don’t allow it to kill your quest for truth. Let the life-death-rebirth process come alive inside you. Resurrect Beginner’s Mind. Keep the flow state flowing. Keep the fountainhead resonating. Keep the truth quest always ahead of the “truth.”

For a true seeker, there is no settled state. There is no final stage. There is always something more to learn. There is always some “answer” that needs to be questioned. Mastery is always recyclable. The journey is always the thing. The sword is always sharpened dullness. The diamond is always pressurized coal...“the pearl is also always grit, an irritation as well as a luster.”

Mastery must be discarded on the funeral pyre of muscle memory lest it become the dogma that kills your journey. Feel the mastery, love it, relish being in awe and overwhelmed with gratitude for it. But then let it go. Surrender it to Cosmos. Practice detachment. When you’re attached to nothing, you’re connected to everything.”

“Awareness is about unlearning. It is the recognition that you don’t know as much as you thought you knew.” SA

“The 2nd law of thermodynamics.

Is the direction of energy transformation processes that can occur spontaneously. It says nothing about the rate of this process. Catalysts and enzymes control how the chemical substrates experience time in the quantum world. The network is never in the red, so the 2nd law is never violated by life in a metastable state.

Energy transfer via heat which is what the science of thermodynamics is based on is by far the least efficient and non-specific form of energy or information transfer

This is why using a 'calorie' for any metric in biology is absurd. Absurd when you understand what QED says about heat transfers.

Life does not make a living by absorbing heat from the environment. **It uses light to control electron motions.** No organism can live like a heat engine, nor can it obtain its negative energy or entropy by feeding on carbon or diamond pulp and burning it with oxygen. Calories are for people who just live in a classical world of the past's understanding of biology and chemistry. Calories are a unit of measure that can only be used in a closed thermodynamic system. Living things are open thermodynamic systems, therefore calories are the wrong unit of measure to use. Most of the bro science on Twitter have no idea this is FACT. **Understanding that energy and information are synonymous is the first step in letting go of the calories in and calories out myth from medicine.”** JK

### 5G Canary — 2023-09-23T19:54:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christines-journal-ehs-mctd-5gcanary.22624/post-326737>

It’s that pivot time of year again... today 9/23 is the Autumn equinox with 99 days remaining in 2023! It reminds me of Jack’s story of the wildebeests trek with risk and change each year. My hummingbirds are starting to look like little butterball turkeys. Which means they are intuitively fattening up and preparing to take the long flight across the gulf in search of a better environment for winter. Sadly many don’t make it but must try or the outcome with winter is certain.

To sense the change in the season their brains, like ours I believe, need to connect with the natural light/sun and magnetic effects in the environment. The better the connection, and wiring, to the natural light/sun and magnetic effect... the better our clocks run. A clock with a low voltage battery will still run but, slower... until at some point, it stops. Those small hummer wings are like the second hand sweeping on a clock... tic tock!

It is 99 days until 2024... is it time to start screaming?

I just thought I’d share something else here... maybe it will help you/myself pivot and get those wings moving to keep our clocks synchronized with nature’s golden web.

My husband many years back was interviewing for a job. The new position would be a big change and he was apprehensive about the changes it would bring to our lives. More money but more travel. It would put him in a position that he would have to make a lot of family time sacrifices early for a goal on the horizon. It was 7 interviews. He finally got to the last interview- the big one with the head of the company. At that interview he was asked what type of animal does he see himself as? He quickly answered “a screaming eagle.” I asked him why in the world would you say “screaming?” He replied that they had had Screaming Eagle wine the night before and it just rolled off the tip of his tongue before he could stop it. We laughed about it but also had no idea what the head of the company would think of that answer. He ended up getting the job and the head of the company commented on what a brilliant answer that was. Curious why he said that... we both had to think a little deeper. Why was the eagle screaming? Why was “screaming” the noted differentiator in an intense New York interview where all new the global position meant more travel and family sacrifice.

It actually was a brilliant answer be it not really planned...

We all are going to have challenges and change in our lives. Nobody leaves this rock without some scars. How do we handle it? We can learn a lot from nature... for example how hummingbirds and eagles in nature handle their changes in environment... and choices made:

Time keeps on slipping into the future... let my spirit carry me... Fly like an Eagle through the Revolution... till I am free!

### 5G Canary — 2024-06-19T16:57:01+0300

“Elevate yourself above the battlefield.” ~Robert Greene

Have they raged war against us from the inside out?... Is it a battle for complete control of the mind and body? To eliminate the soul? Is it a battle against entropy?

On any battlefield, it is always wise to elevate yourself above it. To gain a bird’s-eye-view. To go big picture to see how all the small pictures clash. To see how everything connects to everything else...to feel the entanglement. To gain perspective despite your fallible perception.

You elevate to prioritize what matters and what doesn’t. To understand the difference between healthy and unhealthy. To get ahead of the curve. To tune into your own conscience and your own intuition. To realize there is all this light you cannot see but you are connecting with either consciously or unconsciously. To figure out what to care about and what to let slide. As Rumi said, “The art of knowing is knowing what to ignore.”

When you elevate yourself above the battlefield, the world opens, the fountainhead overflows, the Philosopher’s Stone reveals itself. It launches you past cultural conditioning, indoctrination, and brainwashing. And as the world opens up, so do you.

I went to my hairdresser yesterday and it got me curious... she hit that spot behind the ear on the back of the neck with the hairdryer. It immediately gave me goosebumps and a chill sensation on the spine... a piloerection. A kiss or tickle in that same spot will also do the trick... but can you focus on that spot and cause it yourself? Can you train it like a muscle turning it on and off? Having mental control over the electric nervous system in the brain sending electrical currents through the body. Could this be a good hack to activate stem cells and help the immune system?

“Stars cannot shine without environmental darkness.

**Light has no relative power without understanding darkness.**

This is why you must embrace the suck the life.

Life has a tension between comfort and discomfort. Discomfort affords us the perspective to ask better questions about our current assumptions. In my opinion, discomfort emerges from our minds when our choices are suppressing our true identity.
A great way to poison a human is to remove any hardship or struggle from his life. One of the most essential things for any leader is never letting anyone else define who you are and what you choose. Being unconventional does not mean the idea of a mito-hack cannot work. Unconventional means the prescription has not made it into conventional textbooks. This is what quantum biology is and the recent melanopsin data show us; you better pay deep attention to Black Swan mitochondriacs as your environment is made unfavorable by the trends of modern human networking.

Darwin gave us the false meme that life is about the survival of the fittest and that competition is critical to success. It is not. Why do I say this? It is about the survival of the wisest. Unless you know the epigenetic code, which is our software program that runs the genes, which are the hardware in cells, the gene of DNA has no fundamental meaning. That has made Darwin and Watson Crick's perspective meaningless for us and for medicine. This paradigm of "genomic belief" has been utterly toxic for success with illness.

Letting go of toxic environments you put yourself in can be one of the hardest things to do in life, but it is one of the most wise things one can do to change their life. Life is much less a competitive struggle for survival than a triumph of cooperation and creativity. Build your career and network around this concept and you'll win. The most important thing in understanding life's cipher is hearing what isn't being said aloud. It is analogous to what we've found in genetics. Ignoring the genes and paying attention to the junk is where the rainbow really lies. Nature never gives up her secrets easily. We must learn to ignore the noise genes create in professional science and focus on the junk DNA.

The same is true with talk. _Words we use are not as critical in human communication as we believe._ Just as it is the silent part of the genome that runs our DNA, in language, it is the things not said in words, but present between words that are critical. _They hold the key to successful understanding in life._ The art of reading between the lines is the quest of the wise mitochondriac. Always pay attention to the pauses, the nonverbal chatter, and the movement that occurs between words to understand what is really going on.

Learning is like the fuel that moves the electrons of your body toward a destination of success. _Learning requires an ear that listens and an intellect that is aware and has the patience to endure mistakes. Errors are the growing pains of wisdom. The invariable mark of wisdom in you is when you see the possible in the improbable and the miraculous in the common. Wisdom is not built by time and does not comes not from age, but from education, learning, and thinking._
The irony of modern centralized science is this: Physicists/astronomers believe that if they want to understand the beginning and end of the universe they should aim understand the life cycle of the stars to unlock the mystery of the evolution of the universe. They believe if they can work out how stars change and evolve over time they believe they could begin to understand how the universes changes over time. The irony? Amazing contraction to me. The lesson to be learned and tattoo'd into your brain?

Stars cannot shine without environmental darkness.

**Light has no relative power without understanding darkness.**

If you get it wrong,

CHANGES IN LIGHT PREDICT EPIGENETIC PROGRAMMING”... JK

### 5G Canary — 2024-10-03T20:58:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christines-journal-ehs-mctd-5gcanary.22624/post-340379>

Hopefully this is more helpful then cutting your hair which although would be nice it isn’t the easy solution to EHS. Nature is brilliant when we let her do her thing and stop getting in her way. Look at your EHS as a beautiful gift from the divine. You have divine intervention and protection if you chose to reconnect with Nature. This gift she has given you is filled with many new abilities. Use those abilities to your advantage and begin developing your intuition into a natural instinct. It should be used just as your other senses... instinctively. With EHS you have the ability to decipher quite quickly your surrounding environment.

Hurricane’s should bring out our natural instincts to survive... with critical thinking. There are those that will wait for help and others that will just instinctively help themselves in order to help others.
I am extremely saddened by the loss of life and the destruction of nature that Hurricane Helene has left. Whatever they have reported just know it is 10x worse. Constant prayers go along way collectively... continue to pray. When nature is destroyed around us we also have nature destroyed within us... everything connects. Nature teaches us to continue growing and to keep moving forward and that is what I am doing by sharing this...

A quote from a fable I just read...

“The point of desperation is often the point of truth. When things are wrong, we need to reach rock bottom in order for change to happen. We sometimes need to feel trapped in order to find the way out. We don’t meet ourselves in the light and air. We don’t understand the radio when the song is playing. We sometimes need to smash the thing to see how it is made.”

Since we were affected by Hurricane Helene (HH) I thought I would post the positives of the storm... it’s easy to see all the negatives and conspiracies surrounding what is actually happening here. The difference between a gift and a curse is sometimes just a question of perspective.

We had spent September in Italy for a wedding and traveling to tiny mountaintop towns with low population density, volcanic tuffs and deep well water. It was magical and I felt absolutely amazing... no issues at all. Sometimes what looks or feels like magic is simply a part of life/nature we don’t understand yet. Crazy good sleep and my husband was actually dreaming in a foreign language and knew the language in the dreams...Crazy stuff!

We had a long flight back, lost luggage and had to drive 3 hours from Atlanta. We had know idea a Hurricane was coming our way and very little preparations in place.

The hurricane was insane and completely destroyed our neighborhood/town. It was extremely heartbreaking to watch so many huge trees come down... it felt like a tree massacre. We were very lucky with just property damage as many have lost their lives, loved ones, homes and businesses.

Observations... it has forced living more in line with nature for the whole community/environment and not just the few who were living this way.

We haven’t had power for 7 full days now and it will probably be much longer. We also didn’t have cell service for 4 of those days.... in my world these are all huge positives!
What I have observed is my vision has been improving to the point the reading glasses are actually bothering me... guessing improved vibrational oxygen.

People and animals in the neighborhood are no longer as arthritic/joint pain. If you point it out they believe it’s just adrenaline kicking in... idk

People are friendlier and more eager to help one another. Kids are actually coming outside and playing in the sunshine.

Lots of people out walking the trails and experiencing nature. Granted they want to see the damage but at least they are outside.

It’s completely dark by 7 and people aren’t driving around aimlessly because there is no gas... which means no bright headlights in the windows or burning into our retinas. Yeah!

Windows open all day and night... still pretty warm here. The stars at night have been out of this world! Lots of people sleeping outdoors because of the heat.

Because there is so much cleanup with downed trees and flooding it has forced a lot of people to be outside touching nature, grounding and in the sunshine.

My yard is covered in hummingbirds again... almost like they sent a signal. I’m probably one of the few still feeding them and oddly my yard is still covered in flowers. It’s like nature and the environment are in perfect harmony to provide exactly what it needs. It’s very cool to feel and experience! When humming birds migrate the males leave first, followed by the females and lastly the babies. These little babies not only survived the storm but are thriving. They look like they should be instinctively leaving in a week or two.
Cold showers only... people have know idea how beneficial this is!
Things we are using... gas cars, gas generators, gas barbecues, gas chainsaws and cash only.

Lastly- we have a book box on the walking trails... because the power is out people are filling the book boxes and sharing their favorite reads. A book caught me eye called “The Life Impossible”... wow... what a great read. Highly recommend.

After I read the book I happened to see this picture and immediately thought... Wow! The Divine! Divine also gives you “dive in”... time to dive in and go deep!

From the book... “But that is how we beat death. We beat death by living while we are here. Death may be infinite, but, as we know, infinity is a relative concept. We can create a bigger infinity out of life. By feeling. And every day I feel. I feel deeply and intensely and what I feel is gratitude.”

From Twitter...

“The Silence Between Waves

Life does not run on motion alone. Between every beat of the heart, every spark of a neuron, every photon born from metabolism, there is a pause. A silence so brief it goes unnoticed, yet so exact it keeps the world from collapsing into noise. This stillness is not emptiness. It is geometry at rest, the field taking measure of itself before releasing the next act of light.

Inside every cell, this pause is written into water. The hydrogen-bond network surrounding proteins, what physicists call delay-locked water (DLW) holds electromagnetic tension the way a drum holds tone. When photons or electrons pass through, they disturb its curvature. When emission stops, the network relaxes, letting torsion unwind and coherence rebuild. That intermission, lasting microseconds to milliseconds, is how life keeps its rhythm from shattering.

Mitochondria depend on it. Between bursts of redox activity, they fall into a micro-silence where charge redistributes and structure resets. The same pattern scales upward: neurons between firings, muscles between contractions, circadian systems between dawns. Every layer of biology relies on the space between signals as much as the signals themselves.

_Silence, in physics, is potential. It is the state of maximum readiness, the pause that allows matter to choose its next direction. Without it, light would blur into heat, thought into chaos, and memory into noise. The living world survives because it waits, because it knows how to hold its breath._

In every pause, the universe recalibrates.

The space between waves is where time is built, and where life, for an instant, is perfectly still.”

### JackieNewZealand — 2018-12-07T01:59:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eat-anything-maybe.22626/post-257450>

Dr. Kruse once said "If you have great mitochondrial function, you can almost eat sh--t on a shingle and get away with it." He also talked about an Olympic athlete who claimed to eat at McDonalds every day. I used to be overly careful with what I ate and even wrote a book about hormones, diabetes and nutrition (20 years ago worked as a Diabetes Clinical Nurse Specialist and realized even then that what we were told to teach clients was total BUNK ... did not work at all and just got people coming back for more drugs and doctor visits). Now I can get away with eating almost anything and still feel GREAT.

Have seriously upped the seafood, as mussels, oysters and snapper are abundant here, but other than that have been enjoying white bread (love the stuff, but typically avoided like the plague) and black current jam and even ginger cookies ... pushing the limits of experimentation with some other real tasty JUNK ... AND still no ill effects. Abundant energy! Very weird. I'm no longer a food snob ! Also threw out a huge drawer of expensive supplements because finally realize that health doesn't come in a bottle. Too bad I didn't figure that out 20 years ago ... could have taken a few more swanky Mexican vacations .

Today is day 2 of burying myself in the sand at Tahunanui beach, then swimming my butt off in the not so warm surf. One can almost feel the electrons dancing into the body. Also still drinking loads of water and adding a 1/4 tsp of bentonite clay and tumeric to maximize EZ water. Here is my favorite Gerald Pollack video about water (WATER IS LIFE, DANCING TO THE TUNE OF LIGHT - Pollacks version is a bit different, but the meaning is similar) :

I could basically get away with anything, as long as I kept a good fasting window and optimized my sleep. To this day I still can, even moreso now and with MUCH less exercise. I'm generally pretty strict keto in winter as I live at the 45th latitude, but come summer I usually have fun with home-made baked goods like chocolate chip banana bread, cinnamon rolls, pancakes, apple cinnamon cake, etc.

I cannot get fat even if I tried, as long as I feast/fast versus snack, get my daily sun, do a bit of CT, and generally eat a high seafood intake.

Dr Boros makes a reference to his high ADH making him able to drink less.

When thirsty, do not drink right away, wait 20-30 min.
The thirst may go away.
We get thirsty because ADH level trends down.
But the felling of being thirsty causes ADH to raise (in anticipation).
Overall, not hurrying for water makes one drinking less.
\-----------
Dr Cooper advised to aim at drinking 3 cups=(0.71Liter)

,,,,,,,,
ADH - Anti Diuretic Hormone = vassopressin
Desmopressin = synthetic ADH

ADH rising in anticipation of not quickly satiated thirst may be protective acutely, but what are the effects of chronically higher ADH levels in terms of circadian rhythm of urinary function? From the paper below:

_"Thus, it has become increasingly evident that circadian clocks play crucial roles in the orchestration of life according to time of day, and most physiological functions are tightly locked to an organism's activity and rest phases to ensure optimal performance._

_Like many other physiological processes, fluid intake, urine production, and urine storage display distinct daily and circadian variations as discussed below."_

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3138846/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3138846/) Circadian Rhythms in Urinary Functions: Possible Roles of Circadian Clocks?

### MattD1995 — 2018-12-07T20:58:38+0200

> [JackieNewZealand said:](/goto/post?id=257450)
>
> Dr. Kruse once said "If you have great mitochondrial function, you can almost eat sh--t on a shingle and get away with it." He also talked about an Olympic athlete who claimed to eat at McDonalds every day. I used to be overly careful with what I ate and even wrote a book about hormones, diabetes and nutrition (20 years ago worked as a Diabetes Clinical Nurse Specialist and realized even then that what we were told to teach clients was total BUNK ... did not work at all and just got people coming back for more drugs and doctor visits). Now I can get away with eating almost anything and still feel GREAT.
>
> Have seriously upped the seafood, as mussels, oysters and snapper are abundant here, but other than that have been enjoying white bread (love the stuff, but typically avoided like the plague) and black current jam and even ginger cookies ... pushing the limits of experimentation with some other real tasty JUNK ... AND still no ill effects. Abundant energy! Very weird. I'm no longer a food snob ! Also threw out a huge drawer of expensive supplements because finally realize that health doesn't come in a bottle. Too bad I didn't figure that out 20 years ago ... could have taken a few more swanky Mexican vacations .
>
> Today is day 2 of burying myself in the sand at Tahunanui beach, then swimming my butt off in the not so warm surf. One can almost feel the electrons dancing into the body. Also still drinking loads of water and adding a 1/4 tsp of bentonite clay and tumeric to maximize EZ water. Here is my favorite Gerald Pollack video about water (WATER IS LIFE, DANCING TO THE TUNE OF LIGHT - Pollacks version is a bit different, but the meaning is similar) :
>

Posts like this give me a tremendous amount of hope, not because I plan to go back to eating junk, but because the typical diet/supplement approach has failed me for so many years.

The Leptin RX, specifically Oysters, is keeping me afloat right now, but even that hasn’t been enough to get me back to 100%. The more I read of Dr Kruse’s work and the more posts like this I see, the more I realize that optimizing my environment truly is the only long-term solution.

More than ever, I’m convinced of the importance of the non-dietary biohacks. Namely:

\-CT
\-Sunlight
\-Grounding

CT alone has brought about massive improvements for me, moreso than any diet or supplement ever did. I’ve tried every superfood and every supplement. I’ve eaten liver, kidney, and heart daily for the last year. I’ve probabky totaled 2000 oysters in the last 6 months.

Time to really zone in on my environment, especially EMF mitigation. Stetzer Filters for both work and home are my next purchase.

### kris90 — 2018-12-07T21:14:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eat-anything-maybe.22626/post-257528>

[@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) don't get too caught up on the whole water restriction concept so we can make more DDW in our mitochondria. If you source "good" (i.e. relatively low deuterium) drinking water, you should drink plenty of it.

The way I see it is that at the equator, food (and one's bodyfat) is likely more important than drinking water, because the water is higher in deuterium, and food (via photosynthesis and/or cellular respiration) is more depleted in deuterium, and food is abundant at the equator. At the poles, especially in winter/darkness, food is sparse, BUT we have deuterium depleted water from spring/glacial, hence water is more important and food is less so (remember cold induces uncoupling -> fat burning).

At the end of the day, we want less deuterium. Sometimes its easier to source better water than better food. If that is the case, drink more water (and drink it cold). Water significantly increases metabolism, because the amount of energy required to split water is tremendous. Water can therefore help you burn more fat, and more fat = DDW in your matrix.

I believe water is more important than food, and food really is a water + light story. The only reason food is important is because it takes water and depletes it of deuterium. Food is a supplement for light and water. When good light and good water is present, you don't need food.

My 2 cents.

### Sue-UK — 2018-12-08T17:58:36+0200

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=257539)
>
> \---------------------------------------------------------------
> reptiles were first, from them mammals developed
> \---------------------------------------------------------------
>
> When dr Boros was discussing water
> he started with saying that
> Reptiles were not drinking water they were making their own water from food

Dr Boros analogy about camels didn't take into account the camel's intake of water from the environment, and reptiles not drinking water doesn't take into account that there was presumably an evolutionary advantage to mammals developing a sense of thirst that would drive them in search of water, and a circadian rhythm for ADH.

I've recently watched a 3 part series, the first was on our "inner fish," the second episode was on the inner reptile, and the third was our inner monkey, from first mammals to primates and early humans. A musing .... Its almost as if the sense of thirst and ADH rhythm allows us to take advantage of our earlier aquatic past (with the sea now within) in strong light cycles that control deuterium, and our reptilian past (water from lower deuterium stored/eaten fat) in cold or dark and boring.

### Sue-UK — 2018-12-09T10:43:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eat-anything-maybe.22626/post-257634>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=257606)
>
> I agree.
> Hopefully you would agree with this:
>
> In today's life of anyone of us
> under-drinking is natural process
> does not require new hardware or technology,
> everybody can do it (specially the underprivileged)
> while
> there is nothing natural in drinking Qlarivia or Preventa or traveling once in the while to the tropics.
>
> …
> .......

No I don't agree that under drinking is a natural process.  Animals either have to drink water (and will often travel great distances to get it, it is that important) or they have special adaptations. It isn't a natural process if I ignore the ADH circadian rhythm/thirst mechanism adaptations I have. Wild animals don't make decisions to ignore the seasonal latitude variations of water intake and overeat fat or carry round bottles of EVOO to compensate for under drinking water.

I see commercial DDWs as a tool to either buy time in the case of something like cancer, or things like while someone is making plans to get out of a shit environment, and gets out into nature in a good environment.

But at the moment I don't see deuterium as the enemy - if aeons ago the cosmic wand had been waved and there was no deuterium on the planet, would life as we know it have even evolved?

### Brent Patrick — 2018-12-10T08:54:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eat-anything-maybe.22626/post-257708>

**"Wonder if that is the quality of the water or the quantity ... don't know".**\[/QUOTE\]

Consuming water with fluoride becomes a dielectric blocker. This discharges the DC current that is normally stored in cell water and the extracellular water around cells. Yow are unable to build the EZ, thus you are not able to charge your mammalian battery so sourcing non fluoridated water is a must.

The other issue is the deuterium content of the water consumed if it is high which if I recall several waters from new Zealand when tested came back above 147ppmm you want to be below 130ppm especially given your latitude.
I would suggest reading the following [https://www.amazon.com/Defeating-Cancer-Biological-Deuterium-Depletion/dp/0759692610](https://www.amazon.com/Defeating-Cancer-Biological-Deuterium-Depletion/dp/0759692610)

Actually the more I learn the more I realise Jack was talking about Deuterium without using the word itself.

Members on the forum may have parts of the three legged stool that may be lacking and need to be hacked or possibly migrate to elsewhere to make up the deficit, for example UK could possibly be insufficient regarding UV our issue in Australia /New Zealand is our water.
This is what I focus on!
