# Thread: Greetings from Los Angeles
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-los-angeles.22676/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Scott Vincent — 2018-12-12T23:27:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-los-angeles.22676/post-258017>

**Hello everyone! My name is Scotty. I am a second year neuroscience PhD student at UCLA, where I study the basic mechanisms of sleep. I am also a recovering drug addict (10 years clean, 10/22/2008) and have undergone a remarkably bad accutane treatment (June-Sept, 2013).**

I started using drugs when I was 14 and got clean at the age of 18 (2008). My journey with health optimization began at that moment, with abstinence from drugs, alcohol and cigarettes. After getting clean, I went from 160lbs to 240lbs over the course of 9 months.

In 2012, I made the decision to lose the weight. I adopted a strict paleo diet and lost ~50lbs over the course of 9 months. I am 6'2", for reference. Since 2013, I've maintained a mean weight of 200lbs ± 10lbs.

In 2013, after dealing with acne for many years, and having undergone many long antibiotic treatments (9 months of Doxycycline at one point), I surrendered to my doctor's recommendation and began an aggressive course of isotretinoin (accutane). The first two months were "OK", but as the dose was increased, I quickly lost my mental acuity. I went from being a perfect 4.0 student to failing simple classes. I was severely depressed, contemplated suicide, and was sick (with what I thought was a bad cold) for nearly 3 months. I stopped taking accutane when I finally realized that it was the drug that was causing all of this. It's worth mentioning that at the same time I began taking accutane, I transferred from junior college to UC San Diego, moved across the state and got out of a relationship. Everyone around me told me that I was just stressed out because of my life changes, that accutane was totally safe, and that I was making a big deal out of nothing. Those people are no longer in my life.

When I stopped the accutane treatment, nothing got better. It was until ~9 months later, after using all of the tools I had at the time (exercise, meditation, "nutrition", socializing) to recover, that I finally began to improve, slowly. At the time, I knew nothing of circadian biology, health optimization, nnEMF, melanopsin, leptin, etc.) Around this time, I developed tension headaches that would torment me from 2014-today, though they've resolved somewhat.

I graduated from UC San Diego with a BS in neuroscience and am currently working toward my PhD in neuroscience at UCLA. Over the last two years, as I've delved deeper in circadian biology and the pursuit of biologically relevant science, I've become less and less popular among my peers. Unlike the rest of my cohort, I am genuinely uninterested in pharmaceutical interventions and am so so grateful to have found Dr. Kruse and, now, this community.

**Current, known health issues:**
1\. Sporadic headaches/migraines
2\. Brain fog
3\. Emotional volatility
4\. Chronic inflammation (slow healing, and muscle rigidity)
5\. Gluten allergy

**Liabilities in my environment:**
1\. Living and working in LA
2\. Limited time for exercise/movement with PhD student lifestyle. Constantly sitting.
3\. Lifestyle that demands I am inside, under fluorescent lights (and being bombarded by nnEMF) for 8-10 hours/day
4\. Extremely limited budget (graduate student budget of ~$2,000/month in LA)
5\. Unsure of deuterium content in water (I purchase from a water store nearby)
6\. No cold exposure

I eat seafood everyday and try to wake up to watch the sunrise often (and am successful ~3x/week). I wear True Dark blue-blocking glasses after sunset and stop eating within an hour of sunset. I have what I think to be good "sleep hygiene" and am constantly reading. I do a good job of getting to work early and leaving work early to avoid the stress of traffic and limit my indoor light exposure to when it is also light outside - I wear "yellow"/daytime blue-blockers when I am inside at my desk. I get the sense that I am doing some things right, but I still feel incredibly high amounts of tension throughout my body and don't ever feel like I can think clearly or articulate my thoughts like I used to (before accutane). I sense that it is more than just the stress of my job.

I have a long family history of diverse health "stuff" on both sides of my family, though I'm not sure I should dive into that here.

At the end of the day, I feel like I've learned enough to know that I know nothing. I am grateful to feel curious and engaged (I've lost that many times in my life) and am hungry for more information. I look forward to diving deeply into the resources available here, learning a lot, and hopefully contributing in some way.

### Anne V — 2018-12-14T00:44:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-258065>

Hi, My name is Anne.
my main problem is an aortic valve getting calcified.
it started in Feb 2013 going for medical (airline pilot) i got sent to cardio (Arteries )
medical removed, i need stenting if i want to keep working...
i was very naive then . so i went along thinking the good doc would cure me
after op prescription with statins, BP pills aspirin etc...
luckily after 10 days i got a frozen shoulder. looked up all contre indications of meds and
it all went in the bin. i was still smoking , cigarettes went in the bin too.
I did raw vegan for 9 months , gave that up too impossible in winter in the UK
to keep it short CAA sent me for another stent 9 months later,,, i finally regained my medical 19 months later
went back to these lovely really long days shift early/late up 3:30/4am week after finishing midnight /1am
1st of March this year, Aortic valve not acceptable to be a pilot . i need an op urgent or i am going to drop dead.
He was lucky i did not punch him .i do not like their scare tactics
anyway so here i am today

i am taking Carnosine, CoQ10 , K2, magnesium malate, Epsom salt baths 3 times/week and japanese knotweed.
i have been following Dr K for a while so: go look at the sun am eat mainly fish and local veg (very restricted) have done cold showers and have blue blocking glasses,
have booked a week in the Canaries on the beach and bought tan through swimsuit.
getting better at sleeping earlier and sleep better.

That s it for now. grateful for Dr K and this website.

Hi Anne

Table 1 of this document might be useful - it gives the levels for several UK spring water sources, and the figures for Evian, San Pellegrino, Vittel and Gerolstein. Gerolsteiner is what I buy when I'm Fuerteventura, (thinking of your trip to the Canaries), its readily available to cater for the German tourists. If I buy UK spring water from a major supermarket (about 1.10 for 5 litres) I do a first freeze method on it as well to drop the number further.

[https://pdfs.semanticscholar.org/515f/b1ea83358150da00e16f3fe966c0bea8727d.pdf](https://pdfs.semanticscholar.org/515f/b1ea83358150da00e16f3fe966c0bea8727d.pdf)

### Sue-UK — 2018-12-15T13:55:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-258151>

> [Anne V said:](/goto/post?id=258122)
>
> The only house i could rent have guess what??? a pink bedroom!!!
> so i will repaint cream colour after Xmas without asking permission
> will repaint pink when i leave if landlord wishes
> asking is allowing a possible no
> what do u say

I'd check the lease agreement, and ask the landlord if necessary, particularly if the landlord has the right to do a periodic landlord inspection, or calls in because you've reported a fault. We've let property before and if a tenant had painted any room pink and left it like that we'd have repainted a neutral colour before trying to get another tenant. Potential new tenants might be put off by certain colours, and with neutral they could better imagine putting their own belongings in there, and it looks lighter and more airy. It reduces the time it takes to relet, (financially a bonus). It opens up the potential market, not limit it to people who could live with the pink (or whatever other colour). We always agreed for them to paint a room the colour they wanted, provided when they moved out they repainted to a neutral cream before they went. We wanted good tenants to stay as long as possible, and often the paint wanted a refresh anyway, so even if they hadn't done it, it wouldn't have mattered. It never happened, possibly because they didn't want the cost of a commercial painter and decorator being deducted from their deposit.

As long as its done to a reasonable standard, if you were our tenant, we'd happily give you permission and be privately thanking you.

### Sue-UK — 2018-12-15T15:55:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-258156>

> [Anne V said:](/goto/post?id=258155)
>
> I have found some Qlarivia water 25ppm in 500mls at a shop called the real food Cie they excpect a delivery next week
> from Wednesday or later £7.95 a bottle

Good find.  From the website

"RECOMMENDATIONS FOR USE: Drink around half a litre of Qlarivia a day. Even a single bottle of Qlarivia, preferably in the morning, helps to balance deuterium in the body.
It is common practice to mix Qlarivia with other good quality water and drink through the day.
If, for example you choose to mix it with Icelandic Glacial water with a deuterium content of approximately 148ppm, it will be possible to mix a water to a desired deuterium level.
1L of Icelandic Glacial and 500ml Qlarivia would give approx 115ppm water, or 4L Icelandic Glacial and 500ml Qlarivia would give approx 137ppm water."

I mostly do a first freeze on well water I get for nothing, which I guestimate gives me around 142 ppm (but I've not had it tested). I noticed a difference when I first did it. But a first freeze on a lower ppm say Scottish water which starts lower at around the 147 mark gets me closer to the 137. I've chosen not to use commercial DDW as I'd rather spend the money on a trip to the sun, and also because I'm looking to improve my ability to reduce and clear excess deuterium naturally. Naturally more lower deuterium fats in the winter from seasonal food, cold/CT and REM sleep. If I wasn't getting 8 /2 to 9 hours sleep in the 16 hours of winter darkness, with vivid dreams, or my starting point was worse, I'd think of taking it up a notch. My n=1 doesn't include a heart condition, that would be a completely different ballgame to consider.

### Anne V — 2018-12-15T18:29:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-258165>

the heating is off at night and i open the window
heating goes on 2hrs at 8am and 3 hrs @5:30
cold shower everyday , cold bath 2 /week . i last 10mn
went out without gloves today .fingers usually turn white/blue bad circulation
today no

### Sue-UK — 2018-12-16T12:12:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-258206>

> [Anne V said:](/goto/post?id=258152)
>
> i woke up this morning feeling good , more energy less brain fog

> [Anne V said:](/goto/post?id=258165)
>
> went out without gloves today .fingers usually turn white/blue bad circulation
> today no

Good signs  My personal experience is that whilst light levels are so low, keeping chronically cold (slightly challenging, not miserably stressful or a too cold shock) prompts my mitochondria to adapt. There's a circadian rhythm to core body temp, reaching its highest at around 8 p.m. so if the heating is off coming up to that time, it seems to help maintain that rhythm, at a time when temps outside are naturally lowering. Its like a circadian mitochondrial pilot light freebie.

On the first cold snap getting nude into a cold bed with summer bedding and open window took a few minutes for the mitochondrial pilot light to turn up the volume, now its almost instantaneous. Its still not cold enough to wear a coat if I'm out for a walk, the first time I did it I finished up taking it off in the first few minutes and then having to carry it. Living in the cold lane in winter seems to suit me, with the odd acute colder stimulus (such as a cold shower or cold bath) getting my mitochondria to respond quickly. I like to think of it as retraining them to respond as anticipation machines, the better the circadian rhythms, and the better they respond to the information they get and anticipate what is needed, and respond quickly, (whether the cold of winter or the heat of summer), the healthier (and more matrix DDW producing) the mitochondrial "swarm." Having had raynauds through most of my thirties and forties, at 61 living like this is a revelation.

> [Anne V said:](/goto/post?id=258165)
>
> what ppm does 1 tries to achieve?

I think I hold excess deuterium in my subcutaneous fat, when I've reached a certain point where I'm delivering better fat from storage to my mitochondria to be able to make matrix DDW, particularly at night or when intermittent fasting, good quality water will probably be enough rather than any deliberate lowering. For me, if my body is looking to maintain 150 ppm deuterium in blood plasma, slight lowering of normal blood levels is safer than going at it full throttle without knowing my n=1 risks, for example going too low increases my risk of infection, or slower wound healing. Accepting that risk might be right for my own n=1 if my starting point was worse (say cancer), but I don't think there's a one size fits all ppm DDW target. The nuances of deuterium may be important, the more I learn the more I realise I've barely scratched the surface.

what is n=1 ?

### Sue-UK — 2018-12-17T11:57:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-258297>

> [Anne V said:](/goto/post?id=258222)
>
> what is n=1 ?

If 100 people do the same experiment/hack, its n=100. So n=1 is that the subject size of the "experiment" is 1. So my n=1 is my experience, my results. Someone else doing the same experiment may have different results (better, same, no change, worse), because their context is different. The different contexts might include haplotype, mitochondrial heteroplasmy rates, gender, age, different environmental stressors, seasons etc etc etc. and their initial starting point. So whatever my results, your results may vary (YRMV).

Its one of the reasons I put _"..._ in order to correctly calculate the probability of an event , one must be very careful to _define the complete event clearly -_ in particular, what the initial conditions and the final conditions of the experiment are." (Richard Feynman)" as part of my signature. Having been bitten in the arse more than once and learning the hard way  that what was a great idea for someone else to hack was a poor decision for me, it reminds me to look at someone else's n=1 results through the lens of my own context. That, and understanding the biophysics behind a particular hack/experiment means I am better able to make an informed decision about whether its something I could try now, or leave until later, or modify it to suit my own starting point, or not do it.

### Anne V — 2018-12-17T13:15:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-258298>

Thank you Sue for your time and explanations.
i wanted to order the 3 tests recommended

### Sue-UK — 2018-12-17T15:53:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-258302>

> [Anne V said:](/goto/post?id=258298)
>
> did you do any? which labs did you use?
> What a glorious day again

I don't do labs at all. I'm not saying that's the right decision, but my decision is based on a) I haven't been to see my GP since my last post natal check (and my youngest is now 21). You rocking up and asking for some labs on the basis of an occupational medical is likely to be a different exchange than my rocking up after 21 years with a list I want done based on my own research, when its not my current symptoms or an existing diagnosis that have sent me there.  b) I don't want to get labs from my GP that might get me caught in the current medical paradigm. c) If I manage to get labs privately, I'm not knowledgeable enough to interpret them in line with Jack's teaching, and I wouldn't ask or expect him to outside of a paid consultation. I'm on this site for Jack's teaching, so I wouldn't want feedback from anyone else that is outside of the teaching, however well intentioned someone is. What they don't know may be more important for my n=1, and if the advice includes getting more or different tests, or trying to supplement myself out of the hole I'm in, I'm risking wasting valuable time and money.

It also goes back to context again, for example hormones naturally fluctuate in line with season, as can receptor sensitivity. Vitamin D from exposure to sunlight is not equivalent to vitamin D from a supplement. If my labs are crap according to the current paradigm but I feel great, or the reverse where I feel like shit but my labs are good, I would want to understand the nuances, or find someone else who could. If I say booked a skype consultation with Jack and he asked me to get labs, I would go all out to try and get the ones he wanted.

Without a consult, my gold membership, with access to all past webinars and Q and A's is a better investment for me than labs I'm unfamiliar with, or fiddling around with supplements to improve a set of labs that represent one moment in time. Learning myself how to nurture regeneration is more useful to me.

YMMV.

very well said Sue! my thinking exactly only you said it way better and very succinctly.

when I had consults with Jack he always said my testing here was crap anyway so I don't bother unless I have a serious issue. I do now have a very good Doc here who is on board with Jack.

### Sue-UK — 2018-12-18T11:45:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-258350>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=258336)
>
> I do like the saliva hormone testing - but only out of curiosity to see where my hormones are at. For example - I don't need aPHD science degree to know when my cortisol is too high at night time and I don't sleep well.

Sleep is so much better in mid winter than midsummer for me in the UK. I think it has something to do with lowest core body temp at 4.a.m and the lengthening phase of REM sleep - less easy to get the longest phase of REM when its already getting light in mid summer. Alexander Wunsch in one of his videos said he thought that we needed to get more than 8 hours total darkness to regenerate. In a low light cycle, my minimum of 10 hours total darkness at night (11 is my target) when sunrise is not til nearer 8 a.m. gives me that last phase REM cycle, that I often miss in summer, and REM is a deuterium depleter. Its a musing …. but I think REM can influence the mitochondrial pilot light. A normal dream seems to take the mitochondrial pilot light to one level, but I recently had a last phase REM sleep dream where I was climbing up a wall, like a military training wall, but much much higher, and I was struggling big time, once I got to the top I had to climb through a window, but was too big to get through it (ho hum ). I woke up just before sunrise and my pilot light was on full rev, but I wasn't sweating. It was as if my mitochondria were doing some "as if" responding to the intensity of the dream, and a lower core temp in line with circadian rhythm meant I didn't overheat. There's also the BAT activation that may have influenced my result on waking.( [https://www.cell.com/cell-reports/pdf/S2211-1247(18)30318-8.pdf](https://www.cell.com/cell-reports/pdf/S2211-1247\(18\)30318-8.pdf). A Diurnal Rhythm in Brown Adipose Tissue Causes Rapid Clearance and Combustion of Plasma Lipids at Wakening). I might be way off - but no huffing and puffing, muscles inactive and not getting tired .. deuterium depletion…. sounds like my kind of exercise.

### Anne V — 2018-12-18T11:46:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-258351>

Another question: what happens if instead of swallowing ddw , we swish it in the mouth for 2 minutes first.and then swallow
there is absorption there but uncertain of where it goes

### Sue-UK — 2018-12-18T13:34:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-258353>

> [Anne V said:](/goto/post?id=258351)
>
> Another question: what happens if instead of swallowing ddw , we swish it in the mouth for 2 minutes first.and then swallow
> there is absorption there but uncertain of where it goes

I think there's nuances with doing that, even with normal water. If the brain registered hydration only after cells received water, people could be drinking more than the body needs. So if its the communication between the brain and mouth that avoids over and under hydration, my holding water in the mouth for 2 minutes could be telling my hypothalamus that more water is entering the body than its actually going to get. That has the potential to interfere with the thirst mechanism, and anti diuretic hormone release. So for me there's that to think about even with normal water. Added to that, I don't know enough of the science to know whether swishing say 25ppm water for 2 minutes could interfere with the deuterium depleting pathways by registering a risk to blood levels going too low. I'd need to understand the potential non linear effects before I did it.

There might be a good reason to do it with DDW (for example an oral cancer), but that's a whole different DDW ballgame.

is it good at blocking artificial blue light with blue blocking glasses as well in the evening?

### Sue-UK — 2018-12-23T13:19:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-258833>

> [Anne V said:](/goto/post?id=258770)
>
> other question : i am shortsighted since 15 y o wear cl but not much during daylight nowadays.
> is it good at blocking artificial blue light with blue blocking glasses as well in the evening?

I need glasses to read, which don't block any blue, and if I put blue blockers on over them past sunset, its too dark to read. For me there's also the issue as to how much NIR is blocked by glasses and blue blockers, and how that affects the retina. I aim to be off the computer before 6 pm at the moment. My choices are based on trying to regenerate one retina, and protecting the undamaged one. In winter, using blue blockers when the tv is on in the evening isn't as good a choice as accepting the darkness. 16 hours when I live with someone else is tough, but 11-12 hours total darkness is doable. Whoopee we've had the shortest day.  Roll on more natural daylight.

bottle fed

no concept of death , and waste of time
mother since i was born ,on pills
valium, sleeping ... name it it was there

married 18
divorced 21

24 /27 coke/heroin

### Anne V — 2019-01-15T18:30:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-260401>

33 years old :in hospital for biopsy (breast).
met the lovely lady who was going to mark where to do the biopsy
big needle in hand ! i refused to let her touch me.
they were fed up with me. (wasting their time and schedule!)
so they did another radio, and realized i did not have to be there as wrong reading!!!

then nothing special until 44 where i had very heavy periods. i could not go anywhere for 24 to 48 hrs.

2001 1st pleurisy beginning of the year then pneumonia end of August.
was given masses of antibiotics , repeatedly over 2 years
Asthma:was given inhalers .used for about 6 months .

2013 blocked arteries about 50% angiogram, angioplasty,6 months off
got checked out . they (CAA) found another blockage @32% . max allowed for pilots 30%
another angiogram nothing found . CAA insisted.another stent , another 6 months off (nice)

finally August 2014 medical back back to work in September

after 10 days of taking all their meds i had a frozen shoulder. looked at what was written on the contre indications
and put it all in the bin. i have never taken the statins, blood reducing..., and cigarettes went in the bin too.

looked up on internet and went on a raw vegan diet. lasted 9 monthhs then reintroduce eggs, fish, meat about 2/3 times a week

had a few nose bleed on aircraft in the last year, and gums when brushing my teeth.

2018 March : Aortic valve stenosis disqualifying me from flying. (i am grateful i should have moved on long ago)
birth defect 2 cusps only valve.
i can see that i might have to get surgery, but i do not get how my arteries are in reasonable shape, but the valve get more stenosis

my understanding is calcium deposits so i have been taking k2 with D3, magnesium malate , epsom salt bath Carnosine CoQ10.

as not being paid anymore, i have sold my house, i am renting @the moment. have to decide where to live.
i am lucky that i am totally free to go anywhere.

doing sun rising, grounding, blue blocking ,replaced the bulbs to incandescent, switch off all wifi...@night, cold bath and showers
eating fish and oysters .

i have been 8 days to Lanzarote , did tan well with nothing on (lotion and kiniki)
amazing how well i felt there and so much happier than in the UK

by mid February i will have finished heart/brain coherence course.time to relocate.
just ordered 23andme ancestry for haplotype.

### Anne V — 2019-01-26T21:26:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-261494>

Mother:
born in Bayeux Normandy 1931
in pretty good health except back scoliosis . @7/8 years old seems doctors said they could not do anything about it
1939/45 pretty hard time due to circumstances.
Best seats for D Day
parents divorced father remarried one of her friends
father arrested for collabo as kept his trucks working during the war.great admirer of german efficiency in factories.

she went @15 to Paris and started training and working as a beauty therapist b4 becoming a model /and actress later
i think she was healthy until she married my dad. 2 C with us being born
from birth as far as i can remember there was always something wrong.
on valium, sleeping pills temesta spagulax ...cant thin kthere was so many, we could have opened a chemist shop.
sinus problems.

i believe she had a nose job, (looking at pictures)
then she had veins troubles . had them removed (legs)
phlebitis, and at 38/39 uterus cancer. so chemotherapy ...

45ish facelift new husband.
back pain, bad circulation ... still on even more pills

period of cortisone osteoarthritis, rhumatisme

Then back operation
not good,!
for the last 10 years 10ml morphine am/pm
78ish 2 stents,
so adding usual meds

her hands and feet got so deformed she had an op on her right foot 1 week before passing away.at 84yo

Grand mother from Normandy
very short sighted , circulatory problems too
passed away 92

Great grand mother
from Normandy
circulatory problems, hypertension,lipedema
passed away 94

### Huck — 2019-01-27T16:38:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-261541>

If you have aortic stenosis at a young age, it may be that the valve is bicuspid - which is genetic.

Another thing no one has mentioned is that if this is severe aortic stenosis with symptoms, it requires immediate attention. I haven't found the source for the survival rate below, but I have found it on several reputable sites.

**"After the onset of symptoms, patients with severe aortic stenosis have a survival rate as low as 50% at 2 years and 20% at 5 years without aortic valve replacement." [https://newheartvalve.com/hcp/about-aortic-stenosis/](https://newheartvalve.com/hcp/about-aortic-stenosis/)**
​

It's a balancing act. On one hand we want to do everything we can to stop and reverse the disease, but on the other hand there is only so long before valve replacement is likely going to be necessary.

Cleveland Heart Clinic has a lab panel that measures inflammation specific to heart disease. I don't know if the panel is available outside the US, but I would think at least some of the labs are available on an individual basis.

[http://www.knowyourrisk.com/](http://www.knowyourrisk.com/)

### Anne V — 2019-03-28T04:24:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-266623>

ok 1 st love you guys
then went there feeling like shit with bronchitis
to be picked up by JK at airport was lovely

diner and wine fab Jack really a kind person so is Sandy
breakfast and sunrise then clinic
with Michelle i liked very much. wish i could stay in contact with her.

then CT etc ... and back farm for treatment
looks like pic on FB on that should have asked i have none myself . forgiven

what to say ? i walked in there with much breathing difficulties on Monday walk up feeling fantastic as 15 years younger. could swim without being out of breath etc...

my sister came to visit . not seen since my mum died 21 months ago.
we got on well. i have learned i cannot control people . it is only painful
live and let live
i have emotions going through me . i do not block anymore i feel them accept them, and then they go.
i tried to block them for years . never worked except making me miserable.
grateful for my new ways.

was freaking out since yesterday about having so many changes i have to do in 5 weeks on my return.
saw JK web . have to thank him . i can do that and more.

celebrating my new life as work in progress

had guys in the bar this evening singing and dancing , i ended up with them as they pickedi me to join it was great fun
remember b4 the farm i could not have done that

so my heart is full of love and gratitude.@caroline,[@Sue](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/854/) UK@CarolandNick [@drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/)

41 latitude (same as Rome)
83%of corsican territory is natural park not urbanised
plenty of small villages with about 100 inhabitants

they have the best pigs, bred in the wild . bacon anybody?
plenty seafood too
wines
sparkling water called Orezza

central mountain range divides Corsica in 2
120 peaks up to 2000m

in the mountains, natural pools carved in rock...
i like the sound of it so i am trying it

### Huck — 2019-04-25T16:22:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-268889>

Anne's echo was for her aortic valve. They want to see how much the aortic valve is closed off (stenosis) and how much leakage happens when the valve is closed (regurgitation).

Anne, do you know what your Aortic jet velocity (m/s) also called maximum aortic velocity, Mean (pressure) gradient (mmHg), AVA (cm2), and LV ejection Faction (EF) are? Those are measurements you can track from echo to echo to see how you are doing.

My AVA, according to last 2 echos, got larger which isn't supposed to happen. I think it was probably a misreading on the 1st echo that had my AVA smaller than it actually is. That's one thing to be concerned about - echoes are interpretations and subject to error.

### Huck — 2019-04-26T02:40:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-268949>

> [Anne V said:](/goto/post?id=268919)
>
> thank you. i will get these tomorrow as i went after hours,
> how is your breathing ?
> for me it makes a big difference how i breeze
> and how are you now?

I think I might have been sick the last couple of days. For the 1st time I had a dizzy spell, felt clammy, and a little nauseous. All symptoms of a heart attack, except I had no pain at all in my heart area. I also had real bad sinus congestion and sneezing, so I fought off my fear and told myself to just try to sleep it off, but keep the phone nearby just in case. When you have heart disease, every pain seems like a heart attack!

I started taking MSM about a week ago and it got rid of the heart discomfort I had been having. I mowed about 2 acres with a walk behind mower, worked in the garden, started digging a drainage ditch, replaced a window, etc with no problems. The one thing that makes me out of breath is swinging an ax. Three or four hard swings and I have to rest. Most days I feel good and have plenty of energy. My next echo is in June with follow up appointment in August. They don't want to replace the valve until it gets worse, even though I have severe stenosis now. The doc said it was because the average life of the new valve is about 15 years, so every month it can be put off is 1 month more before it has to be replaced.

### CarolandNick — 2019-04-26T13:33:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-268979>

We have been listening to Jason Bawden Smith on a few podcast recently. He has a mission to help large numbers of people to get on to this path.
[https://podcast.nourishmeorganics.com.au/jason-bawden-smith-on-light-water-and-magnetism-simplified](https://podcast.nourishmeorganics.com.au/jason-bawden-smith-on-light-water-and-magnetism-simplified)
He is trying to cut through the science and just get people to act and do the things they need to do. Love it!
Don't get me wrong, I love stretching my brain with Jack's work, and we listen to him every day  - but I do struggle sometimes to hold on to enough of what I am hearing to put the message across concisely to the people we talk to in our shop. I end up wanting to say too much...
Thought you might like to listen. x

### Anne V — 2019-04-27T16:13:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-269095>

[@drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) effect of metal roof on a boat with 5g?grazie

### drezy — 2019-04-27T16:28:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-269096>

> [Anne V said:](/goto/post?id=269095)
>
> [@drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) effect of metal roof on a boat with 5g?grazie

There are lots of factors involved (think crime scene analysis).

In general, I like metal roofs. Keep in mind that you do not want a cell phone on under that metal roof with you thinking it just needs to max out in order to regularly "touch base" with outside transmitters. Some people miss this detail.

Your medical reports lately look great [@Anne V](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/22299/) ! If I remember correctly you need to harness lots of sun to manifest your inner sun goddess superpowers. I'm sure you've got that factored in those plans.

Also, maybe not too related to your question, you can scour the marketing side of people lamenting poor signals. The following article focuses on product solutions to low-e glass since it blocks (or at least severely attenuates) signal strengths:
[https://www.rcrwireless.com/20180206/5g/5g-and-getting-through-low-e-glass-tag6](https://www.rcrwireless.com/20180206/5g/5g-and-getting-through-low-e-glass-tag6)

### Jack Kruse — 2019-05-04T12:58:42+0300

### Anne V — 2019-05-10T11:03:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-270091>

### Anne V — 2019-05-10T21:06:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-270133>

caterpillar,butterfly

we have a cocoon that will restructure as a butterfly, using the same cells
caterpillar cells break down turns into a mush = chaos
as its falling apart many start to assemble into the next structure
cells move from A to B
the movement part looks chaotic,
but all the cells know where they are going to go.

that was so clear this morning am

not a saucepan to be seen (short of going in the garage)
will have to find somebody to cook my asparagus love them lol

### Anne V — 2019-05-10T21:15:07+0300

### drezy — 2019-05-10T21:27:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-270137>

> [Anne V said:](/goto/post?id=270136)
>
> @ Drezy do you have a saucepan i could borrow?

A few although more and more I'm going old school and using good old cast iron to cook pretty much everything.

I don't know if it's that I feel a connection with past cooks, like the testosterone-y heft, or have just given up of fancy non-stick coatings du jour but it's just a pleasure to cook with solid old cast iron.

On the menu tonight(an into a cast iron dutch oven) is Lamb Shank with garlic gravy. The bones will be thrown in the freezer for my next big bone broth batch.

### Anne V — 2019-05-10T21:36:03+0300

### Anne V — 2019-05-12T19:36:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-270273>

[@drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) thanks for the video

had a great lunch , oysters and prawns in the sun .

been thinking about starting coaching.
i was very uncomfortable with the fact that to keep records of clients
and let them see how they are doing ,( unless they have an apple phone)
they uses a bluetooth device.
I will not do that.

luckily, i had a chat with the head of HM South Africa who told me we do not need to use any devices

so they can journal their experience... and see progress .

my vision of coaching :
is not about having answers, or providing info.

it is about being with the person the best i can be and as coherent as possible.
listening for the essence of what is said.

and as JK says in the sun
possibly we do not need blue light either

i welcome anybody s comment ...

### Anne V — 2019-05-21T22:40:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-271003>

letter to myself

i need to be selfish, take care of myself, be joyful about my own existence.
help myself first. cant yet take the drain of helping others.

too many thoughts,mental process, wears me out,
and i think dehydrating.

Emotions: they are not me. i will not participate.
if i recognise them ,i can have a discussion with myself.

i will observe myself , and how i interreact.
listen to what i say,
listen to the sound of the universe

i will take the time to stop
give myself time

"Every act of creation, is first an act of destruction"
Pablo Picasso

just got out the river of life to recreate my path
as the river is constantly flowing,, i can look at the past
and rewrite it and look at the future.
i am an observer . even an observer is not a fixed thing
the I is not fixed.
no label on anybody or anything nor what i am or who i am
i will not get caught in the river
i have no identity.
i just witness, with no attachment

will let u know how it goes
eventually one has to walk the talk

### Anne V — 2019-09-16T23:13:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-277986>

well it has been a while. as [@caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) says , lovely plans migt not work
so after loads of inside looking , i have decided to go for the aortic valve replacement.
as for now i cannot have a normal life, and i had enough of it
so put my name down for minimally invasive procedure
the valve being made of cow heart lining tissue, last 30 years and no med to take.

just had exams heart in good condition , only aortic valve being the problem.
thats the update for now ... more later

### caroline — 2019-09-18T15:25:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-278146>

We are loving Scotland. The people are so lovely and kind.

I can’t get over how lightly dressed Lots of people are. They are getting C/T daily!

12c this morning with an icy wind off the sea and overcast. We are surprised we are not the only ones in shorts.

### Anne V — 2019-10-07T22:07:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-279787>

week 2 + in hospital.
could not breeze . due to water going into the lungs b cause of the aortic valve not workiing properly.
i am being sent to Basildon, end of the week . unsure for what procedure yet as back via emergencies
not in the queue !!!

so being creative , part from wearing blue blockers, i am everymorning in my mind @ the beach , feet in water
watching beautiful sunrises.
i swear it works

masses of medications

### JanSz — 2019-11-15T02:21:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-282295>

> [Anne V said:](/goto/post?id=282292)
>
> heart beat too high
> masses of medications
> what the fuck do you do when that happens to you (unlikely)
> u probably have not the foggiest

heart beat too high------------->potassium low

/////////////////////////////////////
sit down with pulse meter, teaspoon, potassium bicarbonate, and water
measure your pulse
take 1/2 teaspoon potassium bicarbonate, chase it with a sip of water
20 min later measure your pulse again

evaluate
.....
**BulkSupplements Potassium Bicarbonate Powder**
[https://www.amazon.com/BulkSuppleme...=2025&creative=165953&creativeASIN=B075GVLR27](https://www.amazon.com/BulkSupplements-Potassium-Bicarbonate-Powder-Grams/dp/B075GVLR27?psc=1&SubscriptionId=AKIAILSHYYTFIVPWUY6Q&tag=duckduckgo-ffab-20&linkCode=xm2&camp=2025&creative=165953&creativeASIN=B075GVLR27)

### Huck — 2019-11-17T02:34:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-282397>

I feel for you Anne. Could EMF be causing the fast heart rate?

### Anne V — 2019-11-18T23:40:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-282464>

> [Huck said:](/goto/post?id=282397)
>
> I feel for you Anne. Could EMF be causing the fast heart rate?

i do not know Huck. i ve been in hospital since 27 th Sept .in ICU they did not know they would pull me out, because of so much water in lungs, bcause of aortic valve stenosis.
what saved me was pressured oxygen. i could not do without for 2 days.
stayed in cardiac section after 3 days in icu
until 13 oct in a room on my own to make sure i got rid of all water , and lungs were good for op
13 oct went in ambulance to basildon, specialist heart op .
op booked for 15th
u have to be ready at 6 am in case they change their mind ...
by 4 pm no news . no food no drink.... i went to see the nurses and said i am out i need water. that forced them to call doctors ,...
who said let her out ... complications with early one and no icu bed .
2 days later lunch time i went
so much stress put on me until i went...
dying of not being able to breathe is awful
do not know . had a consult
bought a sperti vit d light
as unable to travel now
thanks for asking ,
hugs

### Anne V — 2019-11-18T23:42:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-282465>

and u stay in icu because your organ are shutting down
in my case heart and lungs

### Anne V — 2019-11-18T23:55:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-282469>

and for 24 hrs they thought i would not make it
i was thirsty but they could not take me out of pressured oxy
u cant drink
pressured oxy brought me alive again .

### Huck — 2019-11-19T02:20:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-282479>

> [Anne V said:](/goto/post?id=282465)
>
> and u stay in icu because your organ are shutting down
> in my case heart and lungs

I wish I had some words of wisdom, but I'm afraid I don't. I will pray for you.

Sometimes when I feel overwhelmed, I close my eyes and envision a river of energy. Then I will my body to start absorbing energy from the river. I lay still with my eyes closed feeling the energy flowing into my body until I start to feel calm and stronger. As you breath in, feel the energy flowing into your cells. Breath out and feel your body relaxing. I guess this is probably a type of meditation and I find it works for me. I used it nightly on the week leading up to my procedure.

Big hugs to you.
\----
As for a heart rate,
try this:
get all ready, do the whole test sitting
measure your pulse
take 1/2 teaspoon potassium bicarbonate (amazon.com)
measure your pulse 20 minutes later
draw conclusions
\-------
Above not only lowers my pulse but also nightly muscle cramps disappear.

potassium (RBC) test is hard to get

.....

### Anne V — 2019-11-29T23:29:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-282906>

[@drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) my kickboxing career start again in March.we ll keep u updated
went Wednesday for 5 weeks check to Basildon.
they were happy with most xcept heart rate, but decided they had been very successful so downgraded me to
common cardiologist and my own surgery  better for me. if they had not like they would not have released me .
they were a bit pissed off i was back living on my own, but that poor lovely lady who took me in , probably
had enough after 2.5 weeks
cant do anything for another 6 weeks . i saw radio , i have rings 6 or8 holding the sternum. thats what they worry about
seems it takes 2 years to heal
and i cannot catch anything as they worry it goes on the valve and have to reopen (feck that) teeth are important .

giving u as much info as i can would interest [@Huck](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1100/)

had consult with uncle J advice:
go to the sun, cant fly
Sperti vit D

you know as much as i do which is not much xxxhugs

### caroline — 2019-11-30T03:26:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-282911>

I love your spirit Anne ...xo

Anne, have you asked your doctor if sleeping inclined could help your heart heal?

>

> I did read some people with heart isues got help from that....at least it would be an easy thing to try. You just need someone to help you lift the bed and put some books under the feet as you cant lift anything for now..

Thanks Inger, i am having it done as and when i get the help.i would think before Christmas

### Anne V — 2019-12-02T19:54:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-282999>

[@caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) , thanks for note about gratitude.

i received this evening email... to do with lawyers of mother estate. the friend who helped us (payingnow, for 10% of estate
now wants out.
so just received a demand for 60000 CHF (same as $)
got really stressed and angry for 15 mn.

then thought i am grateful for it. why always be grateful for the good. why not be grateful for what we perceive as bad.
i am already a lot less stree.

change the way i think, i can change my life .

bought myself a beautiful bunch of flowers.

### Anne V — 2019-12-12T23:39:02+0200

last week , whoever looked after my mom s estate have been sent on their bikes
fed up with 30 months , huge lawyers bills, no results
i have to pay back 40000 euros sometimes,
but had enough of their shit.

it s like a huge weigt been taken off my shoulders.

feel better slowly
need a chat with dr K

mainly working on my mood.
sometimes not so good , as in 2 years should run out of monies.

need to start a new business , from mid april .
cant do anything to end of january,

much on my own . good luck you , i love you. message from me to me xxx

### 5G Canary — 2019-12-13T16:45:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-283506>

Caroline has wise words here ^^^

Anne you are an amazing fighter or you wouldn’t still be here.... you came this far for a reason. It takes a strong person to go through the things you have had to experience and remain standing. Don’t focus on the money or the future because it is all really irrelevant and out of your control.

Focus only on your “Now time” which is right now today. Today you are alive, you are healing, and you have money. Those things along with the fact that the sun is still shining are all pretty amazing things to be grateful for. Turn those gifts into your tomorrow... the Universe provides what you ask for.

The healthier you become the easier it is to think clearly, create income and pay it forward. Stay positive and use your positive thoughts and actions to make wise choices. You got this and we are all cheering for you!

[At A Cellular Level, Every Single Human Depends On Positivity To Survive
\[URL\]https://blog.sivanaspirit.com/hh-cellular-level-every-single-human-depends-on-positivity-to-survive/](https://blog.sivanaspirit.com/hh-cellular-level-every-single-human-depends-on-positivity-to-survive/?fbclid=IwAR3kxGFqIzXojlVcJS_WZnMbE0eXU4r8Lbv0YUIY5mTYdYI2B5ncR2EoJPw)\[/URL\]

### Anne V — 2020-01-16T00:31:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-284672>

went to rehab today did well .
what tired me was arms /biceps horizontal /up
i suppose this is where they cut the sternum

bp and hr good when i started 130/73/ 75
10 mn after finish hr still 95/103 heart rate
back home 35mn later
123/72 75heart rate

i am happy with the result
they were worried as too high
considering where i was
it is amazingly good that 35mn later it is great.

might takes a little longer to settle
but looking at where it was it is amazingly ok (view by me) as i have nobody s help that could say otherwise

i am now 100% in charge of myself

### Inger — 2020-01-28T12:30:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-285262>

Anne, I cant find the place where you asked for ideas to help dry skin!
Maybe you already resolved the issue, but here are my ideas, anyways

This is what I would do for from the inside;

\-Fatty fish and seafood
\-egg yolks
\-liver
\-beef fat/animal fats

From the outside,

I love how soft my skin gets when I do a scrub/peeling with honey, mineral salt(Base salt is what I use but I guess fine sea salt might work as well) and olive oil(coconut oil might work as well). Mix it all and scrub whole body in the shower. Rinse the shower walls after because it makes a little mess . You might want to try add some H2O2 too, I have not tried that yet - but I do use H2O2 sometimes for my hand scrub with mineral salt and honey together, and it does make the hands so soft

### Anne V — 2020-03-18T05:21:08+0200

i am a lucky girl !
day 6 tomorrow.
2 hrs in water . i suppose [@shah78](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1152/) would call me a tourist.

of course sunrises , they are beautiful here.
then in the sun 8ish to 11 then in the shade until 4pm wth 30 mn between 12 and 4.

solar callus slowly coming back
not as good as last year ( maybe, probably too long in hospital) i think Jack would say my redox is crap.

sleep 6,7 hrs so less too

i had lower backache in London
always goes with watching sunrises . quite amazing

difficult to make any plans as nobody knows what happens from 1 day to the next.

my sister cancelled as she needs to look after her handicapped daughter, and cannot afford 2 weeks quarantine.
no more schools opened in Colombia!

anyway , i feel really blessed to be here .

### Inger — 2020-03-26T11:18:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-287996>

Caroline, I think you will be fine! You are out in nature, you know how to take care of you
Have you nicotine gum and high dose thiamine and high dose vitC in your medicine bag? That would be wise
And stay off any sugar and all alcohol except red wine.
Eat more seafood and less meat (you do not want any extra iron right now- also sugar, fructose is worst, and all alcohol except red wine, increases iron uptake from the gut).
You might be in fact lucky to be where you are

### Inger — 2020-03-27T11:37:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-288039>

sleeping a lot is great Caroline   just not stressing and being relaxed and happy is great for the immune system

Here where I live everything is peaceful.. shops have lots of food, just streets are more silent and yes, everything except food stores and other important shops, is closed.

I found a letter in my mailbox from the mayor in town, he/she had left their number on the paper and a nice caring letter encouraging anyone that needed help to call them and they will help you. That was very moving indeed.

I wanted to go donate blood in a hospital close to here, but as I called them, the phone line did not work.. they are overwhelmed apparently. I guess more from the work of trying to prepair for everything. So going to hospitals might be a bad idea right now.
Thankfully red cross arranges blood donations in school etc close to me next week so I will donate then instead. Hopefully it works out! I have been a good girl and not eating red meat lately at all, so it will have helped lowering my iron too.

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=288032)
>
> Thinking of you - take care xo

Thanks Caroline - thinking of you too
I am taking care in the way that I am outside in my bikini 10 hours every day now, we have gorgeous weather and I am not going anywhere.... I start to get some nice tan going on
I am vaccinating myself a la Jack
I do see forward to when oyster beach restaurant opens up again..... hopefully in a few weeks.

### Anne V — 2020-04-19T00:33:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-288809>

latest

on Wednesday night , woke up around 3am , unusual for me. went for a wee then in the living room for a while to read (red light) .
@ 3:30ish got up to go back to bed .
2 steps , i fainted.was on the floor for a while. when i came back , could not move.
it took a while ... i was back in bed around 4am
took nicorette 4mg, vt c , zinc and quercetin.
woke up late for me 9amish.
continued next day on protocol adding propolis spray every 2 hrs.felt not great.but no sorethroat or difficulty to breathe ...

next evening took xtra melatonin 3mg.
slept until 10:30
felt good
better and better today felt amazingly good.

what was that??? suspect wifi even mine switch off when not in use.
blood pressure being too low???

does it then takes that long to coming around
\+ that long to be able to get up again ?

i dont know. @ jack Kruse could you comment or anybodyelse.
Thanks

### Anne V — 2020-04-19T02:00:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-288811>

### caroline — 2020-04-19T03:16:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-288812>

### Inger — 2020-04-19T10:16:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-288820>

Oh man, Anne  good you are doing great now!
What comes to my mind... maybe do not go and read books before sun is up, just stay in bed instead? Maybe it was too much for your body, even the red light has a different effect than darkness.
I myself never go up and do something if I wake up in the night. I just lie in bed in darkness. It is fine, a beautiful silent cozy time with me   I sometimes get really cool ideas and thoughts in the early morning hours in bed

### Sue-UK — 2020-04-19T13:35:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-288824>

Glad you are feeling better Anne.  I think there's a difference between using a red light for therapy, and as a lifestyle choice. As a reading light it is a lifestyle choice and that's where the problem may lay - if I think of the "recipe" of light varying throughout the day, and then the frequencies reflected off the moon at night, or candle light, it is never monochromatic. Visible red is stimulating if its intense, it can also raise intraocular pressure, which makes me think that in theory it could also mess with circadian rhythm if its used during what should be total darkness. Then there's flicker .... Using a small torch with a red or amber filter to find the way safely to the bathroom and back for a pee is one is one thing, but I've trained myself to do that in total darkness, and I keep a bottle of water within easy reach.

### caroline — 2020-04-20T06:28:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-288859>

for me .....the flicker is almost the bigger problem....

XOXO

> looked into pineapples

I am buying lots of my food at Costco.
They sell containers of freshly cut pineapples there.
Usually, I eat one or two pieces at a time.
This time I ate the whole thing all at once, about 1.5 of the whole pineapple.
By the end, I felt a burning in my mouth.
Looking into the mirror my mouth was indeed burned and all red.
It took this and the next day to get over that burn.
That was the first time in my life I ate so many pineapples at one sitting.
What gives? Too much acidity?
No problems with the end of processing it, bristol #4.
.

Hi Anne
so glad you went for vacation   I need one too now!!! I have only October left to work in the restaurant. With mask. And then... finally, the torture is over. I hate it - I love my work but hate the mask. It traumatizes me. I cant b ett enough breath and at times the work is very physically and mentally demanding.. and then.. not getting to breath freely in very high stress is torture for me.
I am proud I have made it through these months. With way too much wine, going too late to bed, etc etc. But I am still alive   . And after I am off, I will decide what to do next. Germany gets worse and worse it seems. But I have hope in our justice - time will tell. Very interesting times. I will write more as soon as I get more time on my hands. Have been working so much. Two jobs all summer.
Thinking of you

Anne, I'm still eating, but easing off pineapple, for the bromelain, rather than take a supplement, after my leg injury. I was late starting it, talked myself out of it, it doesn't grow here blah blah, but bromelain is actually a potential covid protection strategy. In enzyme therapy its used for swelling and inflammation due to injury, but its uses are far reaching .... Jan, bromelain breaks down protein, which is why they say don't use fresh pineapple if making jelly, it won't let the gelatine set. In cosmetics it is used for its exfoliant properties, which might account for the burning - over exfoliation of the tissues of the mouth?

I've spent 3 nights in a UK airbnb this week, and at the time of booking it was made clear that if I booked, I was agreeing the terms and conditions, which included confirming my travel and stay was allowable under the government rules. The hosts had the right to ask for evidence. The airbnb hosts accepted the booking on that basis, and contractually had to do covid specific special cleaning,  and kept well away, which was easy as the accommodation was separate with no shared areas.

If at the time of selling the ticket an airline has publicised about the wearing of a face mask on the plane or within areas controlled by them at the airport, that proof of exemption will be required if an exemption is claimed, or that the covid rules, quarantine, vaccination, landing form rules etc of any airport I was taken to applied, it could be argued that consent has already been given, because the contract - their terms and conditions - is already in place. I think I would have consented when I bought the ticket, and its not something I would want to spend hours arguing about.

My time will come - whether its the airline or country of destination, there will come a time when I, the traveller, will be voting with my wallet ....

### Sue-UK — 2021-08-01T13:40:58+0300

> [Anne V said:](/goto/post?id=301772)

> Hey all, may i ask the ladies in the group, how they see the concept of Comparing yourselves to others, in business. why is it important not to
> and hw they can take action to get a positive result? i am interested in other people views of this concept . Thanks a million.

I'll stick my head above the parapet.  In principle there is nothing wrong with comparing ourselves to others in business. If the comparison leads to improvements (whilst maintaining ethical boundaries, and without putting ourselves or anyone else down in the process), its a good thing. The competition is a valuable resource for ideas, and for mirroring what we are doing right, and where we could make improvements.

### caroline — 2021-08-02T04:20:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-301837>

I grew up comparing myself to others .....I wasn't smart enuf, I wasn't pretty, I wasn't skinny ....I suppose all the usual BS.

I was so incredibly lucky - My dear first husband was the wind beneath my wings, He always lifted me up - to more than I could be
He was the epitome of kindness and generosity and forever love.

I think that anyone can be whatever they want to be....when they have some one who loves them unconditionally and is 150% all in.

I was so lucky to have that......and once again I am so lucky to have that again.

For some women it is a dear parent or friend or mentor ........someone that believes in them so they can soar.

It is about not being afraid to make mistakes - to jump without a parachute - to push our boundaries and be more.

Maybe it comes with age? But I really think it comes with self confidence -to march to our own drummer no matter what anyone else thinks!

Now - I like being different and I have so much respect for others who are flying their freak flag.

About two months after my dear husband passed - I found Jack kruse........

Enough said......

### John Schumacher — 2021-11-23T20:57:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-306572>

> [Richard Watson said:](/goto/post?id=306569)

> I see there's no groups here in NZ, I'm trying to learn meditation and have been using videos from Gaia

[@Richard Watson](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/25275/) - A couple meditation biofeedback ideas

-   [https://www.heartmath.com/](https://www.heartmath.com/)
    -   [https://store.heartmath.com/innerbalance](https://store.heartmath.com/innerbalance)
-   [https://getlief.com/](https://getlief.com/)
-   [https://choosemuse.com/](https://choosemuse.com/)
-   [https://omnipemf.com/improve-meditation-practice/](https://omnipemf.com/improve-meditation-practice/)

As you know, your sunrise therapy is most critical for programming all your hormones. Make sure you do not expose your skin nor eyes to another light before the sun rises -> this includes your devices. This first light exposure sets your biological clock, light is at the top of Dr. Jack Kruse's protocols.

May I suggest, fully exposure yourself towards the sunrise. The moisture on the ground or grass will conduct Earth's voltage. You are positively charged; you need Earth's negative charge. Human myelin sheaths begin in your feet and travel throughout your body into your head. These myelin sheaths conduct not just electrical potential but photons. Allow the light to enter your eyes, look up, down, to either side. Spend time in each eye location, allowing the light to hit different aspects of the back of your eyes. Feel the chill of the morning over your body, allow the chill to move up your spine and into your head. With your palms relaxed at your sides, turned towards the sun, take a deep breathe through a partially open mouth to a count of around eight seconds, then exhale gently through your noise. Your chest is high, your shoulders rolled back behind you, your feet are hip width apart, your knees slightly bent, your hips directly under you, your lower lumbar is in a natural curve. Take 30 deep breathes through your partially opened mouth, exhaling naturally through your noise. You can forcefully inhale, but calmly exhale. When you have completed 30 breaths, take a powerful complete inhale and hold it, squeeze the muscles in your pelvic floor, drew you belly bottom to your spine, move & feel your "energy" move up your spine, push it into your head, focus your attention to the middle of your head and squeeze. Hold your breath, squeezing as hard as you can. When your body's had enough, let go, gently exhale, allow the "energy" you pushed into your head to flow up out the top of your head, breathe in deeply and exhale gently. When you're ready do it again, do another for a total of three sets of 30 breaths. When completed, become aware of how your skin feels in the morning air again. While keeping your focus on the mild of you head and the sensation you pushed there, allow yourself to be grateful. There are two ways to do this -> (first) think of tangible things you are grateful for, (second) think of those in our life you are grateful for; your goal is to feel gratitude <- this is healing. It is the Energy-In-Motion (emotion) that has the power to restore you. As you inhale, imagine you're pulling up through your feet - Earth's energy into your body; when you exhale imagine you're returning this energy back; with each inhale, imagine you're able to pull more; with each exhale, see with your mind's eye your energy moving deeper and deeper into the Earth; allow it to completely fill the Earth <- you are connected.

[@Richard Watson](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/25275/) - I don't typically sit, expect when I'm eating a meal.

So my morning routine is done - standing.

I will also do meditative walks, including walking backwards.

When you focus your visual attention onto the periphery (rather than what's in front of you) with your head up, chin level with the ground (not looking down at what you might trip over) -> you will increase your cognitive function.
[https://www.cognifit.com/science/cognitive-skills/width-field-view](https://www.cognifit.com/science/cognitive-skills/width-field-view)

### Jack Kruse — 2023-01-22T18:01:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-s-optimal-journal.22679/post-319141>

> [Richard Watson said:](/goto/post?id=306576)
>
> Thanks for taking the time to post John. I read Wim Hof's book just over a year ago and have been following his breathing techniques most mornings before getting outa bed. I will add to my routine when I sat bare footed facing the sun once it clears the trees to the east

Really bad idea Richard and it is clear you never read CPC #16. Anyone else reading this thread thinking Richard is right in his assessment needs to stop here and read that blog post. If you don't you might harm yourself.

[https://www.patreon.com/posts/cpc-16-quantum-17015529](https://www.patreon.com/posts/cpc-16-quantum-17015529)

I wrote in that blog this: How does cold improve immune regulation??? One fallacy I'd like to slay from the Internet is that it is brought on by breathing patterns. This is a false narrative built by Wim Hoff. It works on a bio-physical mechanism within the WBC which is normally loaded with deuterium to help boost immune response. This means that WBC has more deuterium in them to raise temperature to affect immune function and proton flows. So today I'd like to give you education on both sides of the equation. I truly like Wim Hoff and I love that he brings humans back to nature in the cold I just am not in love that he has no earthly idea why what he does works.

Wim apparently doesn’t know: that heavy “in and out breathing”, as he teaches is beneficial. It is not. This method significantly reduces carbon dioxide in the blood making the blood more alkaline and that hemoglobin in alkaline blood has a harder time releasing oxygen to the tissues than in normal blood (which is slightly alkaline).

Due to the Bohr Effect, hypocapnia causes cerebral hypoxia which sounds dangerous and opposite to what Wim Hof states in his classes. It causes headaches and cognitive haze that submariners and astronauts get. Just two parts per million increase in CO2 can cause this in space. NASA uses 2 ppm for its CO2 scrubbers and cosmonauts use 6 ppm for their scrubbers causing problems for astronauts who return from the ISS in a Soyuz space capsule like Scott Kelly did last year. This is why cosmonauts use much less water during re-entry and take a ton of salt instead. Astronauts drink a ton more water with salt but they need to rely on wearing a diaper during re-entry because of the fluid shifts that occur when they come back into gravity and inside the magnetosphere from the vacuum of space. Ironically, this difference in technique between space workers in Russia and the USA is a big tell why some people urinate too much at night and those who do not. Wim’s breathing technique resembles what Kelly faced in his space return and also sounds similar to the childhood fainting game that aims to create euphoria or giddiness.

What mimics this effect ^^^^^ on Earth?
Daylight savings is today = light stress event. Every year I get ready for a higher number of brain bleeds and heart attacks over the next week. The incidence of these events is higher at high latitudes. No MDs in ERs realize it and none of them know to check vasopressin levels to see the evidence of light stress from POMc. Normally there is a reported 24% increase in heart attacks on Monday after the clock change. I bet we will be over 30% in 2023 considering how many of our neighbors in our attic (Canada) rolled up their sleeves the last 2 years. [https://www.patreon.com/posts/25331846](https://www.patreon.com/posts/25331846)

### sailplane — 2018-12-14T23:16:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clarification-for-the-redox-page.22686/post-258123>

Hi Jack,

When you say:
"Antioxidants, loaded with electrons, provide water with these new electrons, and water transfers those electrons back to the T and B cells. In apparent contradiction to the need for antioxidant electron abundance, powerful free-radical oxidants are precisely what an immune response relies on for optimal functioning. **It takes armor-piercing singlet oxygen to destroy pathogens and unwanted molecules to clean our cells.**"

Does that mean it is good to eat some bad things in our diet, like fried foods?

Thank you very much. It's amazing that you're dedicated to helping others and putting this information out. I have intuitively felt many of the things you talk about, and you make them all make sense.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-12-20T13:22:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clarification-for-the-redox-page.22686/post-258524>

No it does not. It means that the environment inside of WBC's is built to be harsh........to get rid of pathogens.

# Thread: Hello from Toronto
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-toronto.22687/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sailplane — 2018-12-14T23:44:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-toronto.22687/post-258127>

I've worked for 10 years as a computer programmer in downtown Toronto until I became electrically sensitive. The nnEMF levels in the downtown area are very high. A lot come off the CN Tower, as well as the commute. In one train car about 200 people sit together, surrounded by metal and energy efficient windows, all surfing the internet. It is no wonder more and more people are getting sick. It's impossible to commute without getting fried, and even homes and workplaces are all surrounded by cell towers.

I intuitively used to go out every lunch time to get sunlight, and felt I needed it. I'd stay in the sun as much as I could, but as work became more stressful I stopped my routine, due to more demanding work. I think it's great how Dr. K emphasizes how important light is, and WHY it's important. I knew I need sunlight, but without knowing the reason why, it was easy to put work first, even though I really felt I needed more light.

On a typical day, all workers would stay within the building, with tinted energy efficient windows, for about 8-10 hours, with fluorescent tube lighting, and barely any sunlight reaching the cubicles. At lunch time, I spent time in the lunch room, unknowingly getting zapped as people microwaved their food constantly. I always felt sick after eating there, but had no clue why it was happening.

I was able to reverse the ES partially, and hope with the information from Dr. K and what I can gather from this forum, to be able to reverse it completely.

Eating mostly an organic vegetarian diet with no gluten, only goat dairy, low amount of phytic acid foods (beans, soy), grass fed meat once a week. However, Jack points out organic California food isn't the greatest, which was interesting to hear, since most produce comes from California. I was also eating a fully vegetarian diet when my health got worse, including a lot of lettuce which likely contained perchlorate, since it was all organic and likely most from California. I also eat some nuts on occasion especially peanuts.
Drinking spring water from the blue mountain area here, which is one of the lowest in TDS compared to others.
Sleep is sometimes good and sometimes not the greatest, depending on how much nnEMF is around me during the day. I have an RF shielding bed canopy I built, which I think was key to improving my health. Sleep was very terrible before that, including nightmares and waking up with the skin on my face red and burning like sunburn. Libido is not too bad, however the energy level of the body is not enough. Similarly to doing any sort of exercise, it would completely wear me out to do just a few minutes of exercise, instead of helping. I have improved a lot since I've shielded and avoided EMF, but depleting a lot of the energy from my body in a short burst of exercise causes the nnEMF issues to become worse.
Live in Whitby.

Even if I go to Yucatan for a few weeks, I doubt I can move there if I feel good. Do you know anybody who has done that and stayed there?
I'm catching up to Jack's info, but each time I watch something I want to know more and have questions about it.

### sailplane — 2018-12-20T18:23:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-toronto.22687/post-258557>

> EHS with no DHA from seafood = no reversal. Why? You cannot fix either loop of the Bazan effect in the eye or liver.

Thanks Jack. I will try to get more DHA in my diet. Hopefully I can start a journal, but right now I'm having a pretty big problem.

The neighbor across my backyard is in the wireless business. Shortly after I discovered that I have ES, within 2 months, and I felt much better by shielding my walls, he installed an outdoor range extender called Ubiquity UniFi AP. His device can be extremely powerful, similar to being close to a cell phone tower, I measured it when he was installing it with my Cornet ED88T meter. Upon talking to him, he turned it down to the lowest setting, however I am still very much affected, even though the level measured is extremely small. You mention non-linear effects, and this definitely seems to be the case. It is incredibly weird. One night he left it on and I didn't know, I woke up with my left leg half numb and it took weeks to get the feeling back. The primary problem is circulation. My girlfriend is also affected. We wake up with our arms completely fallen asleep. I also get heart pain, and I thought I was going to die of a heart attack until I figured out the cause in what was basically the last minute.. The worse it gets the more I hear my heart in my ear when trying to fall asleep.

He has kept his device off at night and even day for the past year and a half, but recently he refused to turn it off any more. He said he needs it to have internet in his back yard, but he's very rarely in the back yard.
I have been working on home repairs for a year to sell this house and move out of the city. Since I can't work anywhere, I thought I'd work on the home myself instead of hiring someone, but it has taken too long. I was supposed to sell a month ago, but it looks like I'm stuck here until the spring.

it seems like as good as winning the lottery that I ended up having such neighbor. I picked this house specifically to be away from towers (1 mile away), and yet the level is still higher than I'd have ever thought We're in a normal subdivision with quite small lots, they are 40 feet by 150 feet or so. I don't think any other home has such device in our entire neighborhood. Perhaps your mind control articles can be related to my neighbor.. I have no idea, but surely, if it is a coincidence, it is a

Thanks again.
