# Thread: On the mend in San Diego
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-mend-in-san-diego.22688/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Annie Dru — 2018-12-15T02:38:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-mend-in-san-diego.22688/post-258131>

I was introduced to Dr. Kruse September 1, 2018 via a 5G interview he did with Nick Pineault. The next day was my 55th birthday, and I rose early to view what may have been the first consciously witnessed sunrise of my life. Needless to say, I haven't missed one since.

Like many of you, I discovered this information at the culmination of a decades-long search for answers to health questions great & small, past & present, fairly intuitive & absolutely baffling, but it was the terrifying onset of sleep apnea that spurred the investigation which ultimately led me here. It began shortly after the first of two freak head injuries (upended picnic table & boating incident) followed by a whiplash (rear-ending accident) I sustained between 2013-2016, and exacerbated by installation of a new wifi router directly over my desk in 2017.

Not understanding the connection between TBI, spinal cord injuries, EMF & the Qualcomm 5G deployment, I had come to the conclusion that menopause was simply trying to kill me. Overnight it seemed, I gained thirty pounds, and developed a whole host of bizarre symptoms; crippling insomnia, high blood pressure, GERD, periodontal disease, deafening tinnitus, pelvic prolapse, brutal hot flashes, nocturia & of course the dreaded sleep apnea, just to mention of few. I was literally coming apart at the seams and had no idea why.

Since I'd been serving my community as a Weston A. Price ancestral cuisine educator for a decade, I was fairly certain it wasn't my diet. I'd been drawn to an ancestral dietary approach in 2007 to heal a longstanding battle with inflammatory joint pain, which it did; beautifully & permanently. In fact I experienced such a dramatic healing that soon my neighbors, friends and friends of friends were calling on me to share my protocol. Before I knew it, I was abandoning my music career to spread the gospel of ancestral cuisine far & wide. Throughout the course of my teaching tenure I was blessed to witness dozens & dozens of disease reversals, recoveries & remissions, such that I was fairly convinced that Hippocrates had been dead-on when he said "Let food be thy medicine." It all seemed to make so much intuitive sense, and at least for ten years or so, it worked... until it didn't.

Concurrent with my own menopausal health crisis, I began to witness other "food gurus" and ancestral diet devotees succumbing to illness. When my dear friend, colleague and Vice President of the Weston A. Price Foundation was diagnosed with grade four glioblastoma, I had to admit that-- clearly-- even the cleanest, most traditional diet in the world wasn't going to save us from whatever insidious forces were now silently, invisibly at play. But what the hell was making so many of us-- health-conscious & otherwise-- so sick???

I'd already come to terms with the autism epidemic. I'd worked with lots of families who had children on the spectrum, and my brother and I have three out of six children clinically affected with ASD between us. I knew from watching my own four sons struggle with neurological issues, that something had begun to go terribly amiss in the early eighties. Even though I chose to give birth at home, declined to vaccinate, breastfed for years and purchased organic food, they still manifested these strange neuro-disorders like PANDAS, tremors & OCD in their grade school years, followed later by epilepsy, bipolar disorder and even psychosis in young adulthood. I studied the work of Dr. Stephanie Seneff and it seemed plausible enough that Monsanto's glyphosate was driving the gut microbiome destruction that resulted in an epidemic of brain disorders in young & old alike, but my exposure to Roundup was arguably minimal, and moreover, I'd personally felt pretty darned good between 2007 and 2015. So what had changed during that time frame?

When my husband asked me to describe how I felt, I told him I felt like I was being poisoned. In fact, I would often joke about how he was "gaslighting" me like Charles Boyer & Ingrid Bergman in the classic 40's movie... "Listen, if you're tired of me hon, just say so... no need to drive me crazy, okay?". I almost began to think I was losing my marbles, but I knew that waking up multiple time per night gasping for breath was no figment of my imagination, and I was scared.

One morning I stumbled into his office, groggy & disoriented as usual, after another hellish night, and he said "God sweetie, you're still feeling that bad?" "Yes, I'm dying. I'm not kidding hon, I feel like I'm being poisoned." "But we've gone over this entire property with a fine-toothed comb... there's nothing poisonous here..." Then he shouted "WHAT THE HELL IS POISONING YOU???" and startled by his outburst, my brain suddenly saw an image of the box sitting on the shelf above my desk. "IT'S THE ROUTER!" I shouted. "THE NEW WIFI ROUTER!!!" With a look of dawning recognition on his face, he jumped up, ran into the other room, and yanked the bloody thing out of the wall, where it had been connected for months & months. As farfetched as it sounds, I almost immediately began to get better.

At first he replaced the new router with our old one. We put it on a wall switch, and flipped it off before bed every night, and I made some progress. But it wasn't until we hardwired with ethernet and I replaced my cell phone with a landline that I really started to turn a corner. We had a building biologist come and sweep our property, and since we'd already done the wireless remediation, we were in pretty good shape on the home-front. Using my laptop's keyboard, even wired, still caused me to have weird burning & buzzing sensations in my finger, hands & arms, so I got an external keyboard and kept everything as far from my body as possible. I thought I was good to go, and it would just be a matter of time until all my symptoms were gone for good. I was still dealing with the apnea though, and it was still messing up my life.

Early last Summer I stumbled upon the work of neurologist Dr. Stasha Gominak, who hypothesized that vitamin D deficiency was at the root of all sleep disorders; including apnea, which seemed plausible considering the numbers of people suffering with both conditions. I downloaded the Dminder app, and religiously sunbathed naked in my backyard during the hours the the app told me I could make vitamin D. I was getting tan as a coconut, but still the insomnia and apnea continued. I was feeling desperate.

At this point, I was aware that we were on the verge of the 5G deployment, and was seeking out all the information I could find. As my two channels of inquiry aligned on September 1st, and I listened as Dr. Kruse blew the mind of EMF journalist Nick Pineault, I knew I'd stumbled into something BIG, and I knew it held the seeds of my recovery.

I started with the sunrise. I added CT & prodigious amounts of seafood. I told my brother and he jumped onboard-- rising early to see the sun in his backyard-- which conveniently faced East, overlooking the mountains. He began wearing blue-blocking glasses at work, eating salmon, oysters & sardines, and replacing lightbulbs with fire at night. Our spouses were a slower to adopt our enthusiasm-- especially the lightbulb thing-- our children not at all-- but he and I became mitochondriac partners-in-crime, and I felt really and truly hopeful for the first time in ages.

I had an educational consult with Dr. Kruse in October, and he gave me reason to believe that given a "reset" my mitochondria could eventually recover... even in San Diego. He said I'd need to be naked in the sun all day/every day-- which I was already doing-- and get to the Yucatan asap-- which I've yet to do.

I had my vitamin D tested in November, and was relieved to discover that it was 64 ng/mL... whew! My dear friend with the brain tumors told me that when she went in for her first surgery her vitamin D level was 11... which really scared me. Watching the 5G towers go up all over my beloved city is not only breaking my heart, but making me even more conscious of the necessity of this protocol.

Ironically I know many folks here in sunny San Diego with vitamin D levels under 20. Parkinson's took another (relatively young) friend in February, and now his wife has it too. They both had/have atrial fib, and he had lasik surgery twenty years ago... she had cataracts surgery recently. Coincidence? Another friend has MS, yet another recently diagnosed with Alzheimers, and earlier this week I attended a funeral of a former student who died of ovarian cancer. Sobering to say the least.

At this point I'm happy to report that I'm feeling so much better in general! I've lost all but five pounds of the 30 I gained at the outset of my crisis, and slowly my sleep is returning. I have apnea episodes-- ones that I'm aware of-- more infrequently now, and my blood pressure is normal again. I'm still dealing with periodontal issues... which is frustrating. I've been lucky enough to have never had a cavity in my life, but sore gums & plaque are no fun either, and I started experiencing both along with all the other symptoms a few years ago. The tinnitus has yet to disappear, and I still have a few hot flashes at night, but this constitutes small potatoes compared to insomnia, so I can't complain.

All in all I feel so blessed to have been led to this information. In my gratitude, I enthusiastically bend the ear of any & everyone who'll listen. I find there is a great deal of resistance & cognitive dissonance-- especially in those who need the information most-- unfortunately. That includes my grown sons, all of whom continue to suffer & inflict their misery on those closest to them. The stress of their situations is the greatest obstacle to my own health & happiness at this point, and I'm trying to take to heart what Dr. Kruse says about longevity being tied to the quality of energy coming from those in your inner circle.

It's so hard for a mother to disconnect from her children... even when they're grown and dragging her down-- the little energy vampires! I pray especially hard these days, as I think things are destined to get worse before they get better-- if they get better. I'm sorry to say that I'm grateful not to have any grandchildren-- which is something I greatly anticipated once upon a time. Family life in general is especially sad these days, but the better I feel, the more hopeful I am.

Blessings, and thanks for reading my story.

### Sheddie — 2018-12-15T03:15:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-mend-in-san-diego.22688/post-258132>

# Thread: Advice on breast health
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-on-breast-health.22697/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jen N — 2018-12-17T01:17:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-on-breast-health.22697/post-258261>

I'm mostly read and learn from this forum and I'm grateful for all the expertise, so I figured you all would be able to help me with questions about breast health.

Here's a bit of my health history
Age 43
No children yet, actively trying to conceive, 3 chemical pregnancy's this year so getting closer!
History of birth control bill (age 16-25) and IVF in my early 30's before I knew better!
Diagnosed with late stage Lyme disease and co-infections in 2012
I've been healthy and free from the infections listed above since 2014
Paternal aunt had breast and lung cancer, she was also a smoker, worked night shift & had a terrible diet
Been following Dr Kruse's advice regarding LWM, diet, etc since 2015

In Nov. 2016 I had what I though was an inflamed milk duck that caused moderate swelling in my left breast that came on suddenly. I could feel a "hard spot" and it subsided within 72 hours after I used massage plus comfrey and castor oil topically. I had my first thermogram a couple weeks later to establish a baseline and have been using that to track any changes. I now know that I was likely in early pregnancy and that the sudden change was due to hormonal shift.

When this issue occurred, my OB/Gyn also suggested I have an ultrasound to check it what may have caused the swelling but I couldn't find any doctor willing to do only an ultrasound, so I was forced into a mammogram. Of course they "saw something" on both breasts during mammogram but it was not in the same area as the swelling occurred and then that led to a recommendation of a biopsy on my left breast. The biopsy came back negative.

Since 2016 my OB wanted me to have a mammogram every 6 months, I told her I wasn't doing that and asked for ultrasound monitoring instead since I have dense breasts. During the ultrasounds they have been tracking an area of micro calcifications in my right breast. They have not been concerned about my left breast, which was originally what flared up.

Today, I had a follow up ultrasound and the radiologist said the area has increased in size slightly (they never tell me the actual size!) and there is now vascularity so they want to do a needle biopsy on the area. I should note that during this ultrasound I was in my luteal phase and was likely pregnant.They said that wouldn't matter. I honestly do not want to do the biopsy!

Two weeks ago, my follow up thermogram said this "there is increasing activity in both breasts since the previous study, especially in both upper breasts. This is non-specific, and may be due to fibrocystic/hormonal variation, though more serious developing pathology is not excluded. Further close interval follow-up is warranted." During the thermogram, I was on cycle day 1.

I would like to hear from other more educated folks on breast health, on what to really be concerned about, and if I should have the biopsy. What I've read about calcifications is that they are often due to magnesium deficiencies, I've been working on increasing magnesium. I'm also open to reading any books/blogs on the topic breast health from a holistic perspective.

Thank you!

### Jack Kruse — 2018-12-17T01:58:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-on-breast-health.22697/post-258264>

This chain of events is concerning. I think you need to get several breast surgeons opinions but I think a biopsy is going to be wise. I also think a pregnancy right is not wise with this history.

### Jen N — 2018-12-17T02:33:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-on-breast-health.22697/post-258266>

Thank you for the reply. I am having difficulty finding anyone in my area where I can get 2nd opinion. I live in a fairly small town near Santa Cruz, CA, so my options are limited. I was hoping to have seen another doctor for a 2nd opinion by now. I am willing to travel to see a good surgeon. Do you have one you could recommend?

I also find it strange that they only look at one area in my one breast instead of comparing both of my breasts. They also are not very good at explaining what vascularity is. Can you possibly elaborate on that?

I have a core biopsy scheduled for tomorrow, so it sounds like you'd recommend going with that rather than waiting on a 2nd opinion.

I thought sunbathing nude was supposed to help with breast health

Appreciate your time,

Jen

### Inger — 2019-01-17T18:37:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-on-breast-health.22697/post-260629>

These are things I do for my breasts. The topical use of iodine took care of a lump I have had since teenager. Otherwise I do not have any breast issues but I still do all these things to try avoid getting any!

\-**I avoid using BHs.**
At home I never use. At work sometimes, depending on my clothing.
And my BHs are never too tight, never any metal in them or other strings that suppress blood flow. Also I try to use cotton only.

\-**I avoid using deodorants.**
If I use one, it is a natural brand with no aluminium in it. But I very rarely need any, my armpits normally do not smell.

\-**I keep my breasts in the sun as much as it gets**.
Always when I tan. And in winter, when there is no sun, I expose my breasts to the light when sitting in my open bedroom window eating breakfast.

\-**I do nipple massage almost every morning** (very occasionally might I forget)

\-**I paint my breasts with Iodine** 5% Lugols solution, mixed with some carrier oil if needed (often I do not even need it) maybe once or twice a week. 5-10 drops/breast. To make sure they always have enough Iodine.

\-**Dry brushing the breasts** also feels really good, so if I dry brush my body I always brush my breasts too gently in circles to improve the lymph flow.

That was it

twice that= 125mg once or twice a week

[@Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/)
does all that lugols stains skin of your breasts?
I sometime put drops of lugols on my heals, they get stained for days.
What that may mean?

⬆️ Artificial light at night ups breast cancer risk by 73%.
Women have more melatonin sensitivity, so they're more impacted by light at night.
Just 0.2 lux (that's less light than 1 candle or the moon) can up tumor growth and bone damage.

# Thread: nnEMF exposure/military bases - moving?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-exposure-military-bases-moving.22708/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kerry Harvey — 2018-12-18T01:04:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-exposure-military-bases-moving.22708/post-258335>

I'm concerned about nnEMF exposure in my area. I live in Cocoa Beach, Florida on the Space Coast. We have an Air Force Base at each end - Cape Canaveral to the north and Patrick to the south -- both less than ten miles away. On top of that we have Kennedy Space Center which Space X and the other space companies operate out of.

How exposed are we? I know from listening to Jack that living within ten miles of a big airport is as bad as being in a 5g city. I imagine military bases are not any better.

I have the option to move in a couple of months when my lease is up. Trying to figure out a safer area. St Petersburg, Florida or maybe St Augustine?

Hi Kathy,

For now I ended up back in St Petersburg on the west coast of FL. I used to live here and have friends here. This is likely a temporary relocation for me and for now it's good enough. Had the smart meter removed from the house I moved into and I'm definitely sleeping better.

The areas in FL that showed the best promise when I investigated were the Crystal River area -- no close bases and no close cities with 5G but still close enough to bigger places if you need to go get things. Another area that I thought was very promising was St Augustine Beach -- particularly the south end of the island near Crescent Beach. Even though Jacksonville is 5G St Augustine is far enough away and is not and there are such large areas of State Parks and thousands of trees.

I'll be flying home to Sydney for a couple of months later this year but thankfully my folks live out of the city in a more rurual area and a cell phone dead zone which is great. I keep telling them that it's great but they just smile and think I'm a tinfoil hat wearing crazy when I say it.

# Thread: December 2018 Q&A....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/december-2018-q-a.22712/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2018-12-18T09:52:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/december-2018-q-a.22712/post-258348>

I didn't think that Dr. K. could surpass the emotions we all felt when listening to the June 2018 Q&A.

I was wrong........

Jack's intro was moving and heart breaking and full of hope for all of us.

Jack told us to - do something! make small changes everyday, just jump - without a parachute if need be.

Become the CEO of our own life, don't let anyone or anything hold us back.

Nothing will happen unless we start somewhere.

There was so much here to think about and ponder and put into action. It is all about baby steps and slowly moving forward every single day.

And ....of course....it is about the three legged stool!

Jack gave us some points to think about and then give ourselves a score........ I hope I got these right....

#1 our overall life
#2 our relationships
#3 our health and well being
#4 finances
#5 career
#6 attitudes and beliefs
#7 emotions

I feel so proud to have made the effort to meet Jack in person .... and even more proud of myself for being here from day #1

I, of course, need to re listen ....but I am going to make sure I have some tissues this time.

what say you?

### Jack Kruse — 2018-12-18T14:52:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/december-2018-q-a.22712/post-258355>

Why does daily small change matter most to a Black Swan in training? It promotes thinking about your current status quo. You must challenge that status quo daily to make sure you are not a small elephant being tethered by a false narrative or belief.

A small stimulus, like an idea/thought, leads to massive amplification in life = a non-linear effect
1\. A nuclear weapon can be thought of as non-linear action. It is the size fo a small car yet it can level a city.
2\. A star like the sun can be thought of the same way. It is relatively small yet it can warm 9 planets over billions of miles.
3\. Small amounts of UV light from the sun can leach into the eye (1%-3%) yet they have the greatest effect on the RPE dense core granules to drive the central retinal pathway that connects to the SCN and leptin receptor to control the growth and metabolism of every human on Earth.
4\. The change in networking power from 1995 went from 1G to 5G today. That change can lead to the sixth extinction level event on Earth.
Those are some examples of non-linear effects.
All epigenetic effects are also non-linear. For example, blue light and nnEMF cause HYPERMETHYLATION. <------- That is the major change agent of the epigenome. Fasting, Vitamin C, RBC transfusions, and sleep can help mitigate these effects in non-linear ways ------> November 2018 webinar.

### caroline — 2018-12-19T03:44:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/december-2018-q-a.22712/post-258402>

I can't get this Q&A intro out of my head ......How many years have I been like the small elephant tethered by a false narrative or belief?

I believe I have always challenged things because of my uncomfortable existence when I was young....but how far am I willing to go?

We have made huge changes this past year and it feels fantastic altho, at the time, we wondered what the heck we were doing to ourselves ......but we need to do more.

We are now in danger of being too comfortable........

### Jack Kruse — 2018-12-19T04:25:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/december-2018-q-a.22712/post-258403>

**THE QUALIFICATION TEST FOR THE CHARTER MEMBERS:

**
Who should consider the Farm? I think how you think right now answers this fundamental question.

Divide your life into seven categories and label them as:

1\. Life View

2\. Relationships

3\. Health & Well-Being

4\. Finances

5\. Career

6\. Attitudes & Beliefs

7\. Emotions.

Under each category write down what is taking place in your present-day experience and score it 0 to 10.

Zero indicating undesirable and 10 indicating highly desirable. Your score will be a helpful beacon in whether you should join me or not at the Farm.

If you’re on the fence, tell me what your numbers were for each category. I will encourage or discourage you based upon how these numbers came to fruition.

The exercise should take you only 10 minutes to complete before you begin to realize if membership at the Farm is wise for. For those of you who are coming to the open house, you can hand me your paper and score and we can talk about it there. For those who are not coming, you can do it via PM on the forum. This decision should be independent of any marketing you get from me or my team.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-12-22T03:54:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/december-2018-q-a.22712/post-258702>

I've gotten some of your answers.......I am sending out emails to those who I need more information on if this Farm opportunity is right or wise for you. Please get them back to me........ASAP.

### caroline — 2018-12-24T06:40:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/december-2018-q-a.22712/post-258866>

It seems appropriate to post this here .....

This morning we were at our Christmas Eve organic market. I have been feeling lately that nobody is listening to us about the three legged stool .....but ....we sat beside a Mom and Dad and their 12yo son to have a coffee. They get all of this intuitively! they wouldn't know Jack Kruse if they fell over him but they do know that iPhones etc. have always bothered their 2 sons. They would notice big changes in their behaviour.

They sold their property because they didn't feel well there and moved to a really small rural area. We talked for about an hour about all this. Their young son is very bright and I gave him Jeremy Thomley's website to have a look and get inspired.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-12-24T17:17:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/december-2018-q-a.22712/post-258900>

### MattD1995 — 2018-12-18T20:51:22+0200

I’m keeping my carbs low for the winter. However, I still aim to get 50-100 grams of carbs a day to support serotonin. Otherwise I have horrible anxiety. I’ve been using white rice since it’s hypoallergenic, but I know it’s not optimal. Any good winter carb sources? Vegetables?

Jacksonville is going 5g in early 2019 which means that they already are because of testing before the official roll out. If those are the two choices I would go with Gainesville. ### Roman Leija — 2018-12-19T00:30:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-roman-leija-n-1.22722/post-258386>

Male, currently 25 years old. Born on September 23, 1993 at 9:44 am in Palm Springs California (33 N degree latitude). Was diagnosed with myopia at the age of 5 years old and been wearing glasses every since. Current prescription is -7.25 for both eyes. Was diagnosed with psoriasis and eczema at the age of 12, don't take anything oral for them and rarely apply steroid creams, overall condition is mild these days. 60% European with 27% of that from Iberia (Southern Portugal and Italy) and 18% Southern European with the rest scattered throughout Europe. 30% Native American. With the remaining 10% scattered around the globe. Health has been on a declined since December of 2014 for my hip surgery for a torn labrum, didn't receive any physical therapy help and have been battling undoing a lot of scar tissue and compensations, which leads to ripping like sensations within my connective that have occurred nearly every week since my surgery (very emotional taxing). Went on a heavy 3 month parasite cleanse in the summer of 2016, leading to a trip to the emergency room due to slug (gall baller polyps), have developed food sensitivities since then to wheat, gluten, dairy, nightshades, eggs, legumes, grains, which I' never felt before the parasite cleanse. Currently live in Santa Monica (34 N latitude). Did an OATS test recently and have been told that I have SIBO, mainly with clostridia. Have been getting much more AM and Daytime Sun, grounding, and minimizing my exposure to non Native EMFs for the last 6 weeks so yes I have been feeling better but I need some help to feel great again. Dr. Jack Kruse or anyone out there in the form, what tests, protocols, or devices should I purchase? Or what other information do I need to provide in order to give me better guidance. Any help would greatly be appreciated. Thank you.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-12-19T02:22:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-roman-leija-n-1.22722/post-258395>

As a primer Roman you might consider this now to get up to speed:

This podcast has a lot of copy and pictures to adorn it and explain the Black swan manifesto. It is not a regular podcast. It is pretty epic. There is a “revolution on the surface of the earth” called technology, and it is causing a new evolution of free radical signals in your mitochondria causing diseases. The result of these collisions creates your "health reality". Your health manifests from these collisions and creations.

Let’s use an example from human metabolism for a thought experiment: it is established that all so-called "chemical reactions" are merely the movement/expression of electrons/energy. Matter, the nucleus of the atom, the "nutrient" is inert in chemistry and biochemistry. At least that is the current belief of the biologic paradigm. I showed in my Vermont 2018 talk this is also false. This can also be found on Patreon. It is believed by the great majority in science only the electrons of atoms are dynamic, capable of change, force, movement. This is also not true. Protons move with the help of red light from the sun and the hydrogen heat furnaces of our mitochondrial colony. That is the first law we will call on to verify our thought experiment. Secondly, we call on this: it has been established that electrons gather/collect and only interact with photons. This is the basis of the photoelectric effect and is further understood as a cell’s Jablonski diagram. Electrons are excited by sunlight and fall back to the ground state. This process is capable of increasing their energy and delivering information to the system by changing the spin state of electrons, protons, and photons trapped by the organelles of a cell.

It turns out, electrons emit/yield photons to the demand of the body when the electron moves to another atom or molecule when it falls to the ground state. That light captured by electrons drives many processes in our cells. Electrons are more or less reactionary to the power density in the UV spectrum and use this information buried in these photons. This is the only part of the visible spectrum that is capable of using non-linear optics. Having established these two irrefutable principles, one must conclude: when there is a deficiency of some sort of enzyme, chemical, or substrate in a cell there is a resultant physical reactionary change. That change might be in a hormonal or enzymatic or functional aspect of the tissue in question.
Taking the exogenous substrate will never repair the deficits of having excited electrons creating these substrates. The reason is simple. Feedback loops use the substrates they create as their interactive controller of the cycle. Loss of this control causes the cycle to uncouple and heat is thermalized to the local environment and quantum coherence is lost. As coherence is lost redox power drops exponentially. There are physical manifestations of this in the matrix of size and shape change of the mitochondria, along with spacing out of the cytochrome proteins.
This can occur in a muscle or an organ within the organism. The skeptic will need to admit, the specific deficiency in this system of control is due to a lack or loss of photonic power within this quantum system. If one adds and moves electrons and protons properly, light within the visible spectrum is capable of creating the matter a cell factory needs to operate. That is precisely what free radicals are.
They are foremen in the factory that tell other worker proteins how to act. No one needs any supplement when you understand how a cell operates using light water and magnetism. To create matter a cell needs the cell must have redox power to do it. Optimal redox power is -400mV. Most humans live between -200mV—400mV.

# Thread: Did 37.9 Degree Water For CT & Have A Question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-37-9-degree-water-for-ct-have-a-question.22725/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AdamF — 2018-12-19T21:17:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-37-9-degree-water-for-ct-have-a-question.22725/post-258460>

Today:
37.9 deg. F water for 10m 30s
\--Wearing only Kinniki. No foot or hand covers.

Towards the mid-point of session, intuitively I fell in to a Wim Hof like breathing pattern.

After 2-3 minutes of this, I started _getting warmer in the water._

Especially when I did breathing retention.

After the session, the temperature of the water _raised_ by 2 deg.

_Did I really raise the water temperature myself_ or was this more likely due to the temperature gradient between ambient air and the water?

Ambient weather was 48 deg F.

### drezy — 2018-12-19T21:40:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-37-9-degree-water-for-ct-have-a-question.22725/post-258464>

> [AdamF said:](/goto/post?id=258460)
>
> Today:
> 37.9 deg. F water for 10m 30s
> \--Wearing only Kinniki. No foot or hand covers.
>
> Towards the mid-point of session, intuitively I fell in to a Wim Hof like breathing pattern.
>
> After 2-3 minutes of this, I started _getting warmer in the water._
>
> Especially when I did breathing retention.
>
> After the session, the temperature of the water _raised_ by 2 deg.
>
> _Did I really raise the water temperature myself_ or was this more likely due to the temperature gradient between ambient air and the water?
>
> Ambient weather was 48 deg F.

We often have a series of 3 people lined up for the 75+ gallon cold tub at my place.
I'm not too surprised with a 2º rise in temperature.

My son and I like 50F my wife still prefers around 58F. If she goes last we can usually add about 4 - 6º.

### AdamF — 2018-12-22T05:54:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-37-9-degree-water-for-ct-have-a-question.22725/post-258710>

Wtf, happened again.

From 43 deg. to 46 deg. at 32 deg. ambient weather?!?!

### GringoPerdido — 2018-12-22T14:55:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-37-9-degree-water-for-ct-have-a-question.22725/post-258720>

Awesome that you’re having success with this.

My protective Wim Hof Instructor side wants to say:

\*\* Don’t do the Wim Hof breathing in water. It’s easy to black out with that breathing style. \*\*

Several of his students have shallow water blacked out and died from doing the breathing in a tub and Wim always says don’t mix them.

Keep the breathing calm (inhale nasal/exhale mouth) and don’t push it while in the tub.

If you want to add the breathing... do a couple rounds of the breathing, then eat something DHA rich, then go in the tub when you feel settled.

### drezy — 2018-12-22T21:02:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-37-9-degree-water-for-ct-have-a-question.22725/post-258755>

I'm with [@RobInDC](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21869/)

I don't know shit-all what "Wim Hoff Breathing (TM)" means and could care less.

Get into cold water. Let those mitochondria do the work.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-12-23T00:30:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-37-9-degree-water-for-ct-have-a-question.22725/post-258784>

I have a blog on breathing in the QT series......READ it.

Wim method is really off.

### Butters — 2019-01-21T20:38:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-37-9-degree-water-for-ct-have-a-question.22725/post-260947>

Basically Wim Hof breathing is breathing that is forbidden before every diving test. Because you just breathe our more Co2 therefore lowering Co2 pressure in the blood and delaying the breath reflex, despite your body is actually deprived of O2.
Watch free divers, they NEVER do that for a reason.
And my tubtemp is also raising, normal effect of conduction. And getting warmer is I think combination of constriction of surface capillaries + warming surrounding water. Because when you move it gets damn cold, when cold water flows in. Because of that swimming is CT maximum. And because of that cold showers are often harder, because you have constantly cold water running down on you.

### Christine_L — 2019-02-11T20:00:17+0200

# Thread: Hospital admissions and surgery and nnEMF risks.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hospital-admissions-and-surgery-and-nnemf-risks.22729/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2018-12-20T13:08:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hospital-admissions-and-surgery-and-nnemf-risks.22729/post-258521>

I have found the most common radiation in a hospital is in the RF range and after the final tally of the NTP on 11/1, 2018 this concerns me even more than ever. Excessive RF appears to be correlated with serious levels of melanopsin dysfunction.

Why do I try to get all my patients out of the hospital quickly when they have surgery? To avoid the circadian mismatch they will get if they stay = poor recovery. Within each of our cells, an intrinsic biochemical “clock” that sits in front of every gene that codes for these proteins. The peripheral clock gene orchestrates gene and protein expression over an approximately 24-hour cycle.

To better understand how this circadian rhythm influences cell function, researchers in the MRC Laboratory of Molecular Biology have used a type of connective tissue cell that has a well-studied cellular clock. These cells are critical to all surgeons. They are called fibroblasts and are mission critical in surgery. They work best in sunlight. These cells, migrate PHOTOTACTICALLY to wounds and promote healing. Just as plants move their leaves toward the sun your cells move toward sunlight when a wound is created. This is another reason why I am no longer a fan of bandages and braces.

The scientists found that many of the rhythmically expressed proteins in fibroblasts regulated polymerization of actin under the power of sunlight. Actin is, a structural protein important for building migration paths for cells. To heal cells need higher redox power to accomplish it and fibroblast build the roadways in cells to get those parts to the light they need. This is why I try to guide the sick to the tropics. In a sense, I am a version of a fibroblast.

In experiments with cell cultures, tissue samples, and mice, fibroblasts migrated more quickly to a wound received during the active phase of the circadian cycle than to a wound received in the dormant phase (Sci. Transl. Med. 2017, DOI: 10.1126/scitranslmed.aal2774). The researchers analyzed data from burn clinics around the U.K. and found a similar phenomenon occurred in humans, with burns sustained during daylight hours healing about 60% faster than burns sustained at night. When I learned this long ago I changed how I did surgery.

Modern hospitals and ICU are the antitheses of these environments and in my opinion, the light we use and the way we awaken patients for nonsense is part of the reason why infection rates spike in certain places.

Most healthcare workers and Infectious disease experts never look to the light and circadian signals in hospitals as the etiology of outbreaks.

THIS SURGEON DOES.

[https://www.nytimes.com/2018/12/03/...BjED-yyiVku2tB9nekYM1FhJFR5L0KH-dfxnvUktFAagA](https://www.nytimes.com/2018/12/03/upshot/why-hospitals-should-let-you-sleep.html?fbclid=IwAR388Eb5W1NNT9BjED-yyiVku2tB9nekYM1FhJFR5L0KH-dfxnvUktFAagA)

### Jack Kruse — 2018-12-20T13:13:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hospital-admissions-and-surgery-and-nnemf-risks.22729/post-258522>

When surgeons do surgery and they know the patients mobility will be limited we usually consider DVT prophyllaxis with chemical or mechanical methods.

I was a big fan of avoiding the chemical drugs that accomplished this task and would default to the mechanical options but now I think I need to be even more careful. Why?

So I had the opportunity to use my RF meter to measure SCDs (Sequential Compression Device) commonly used on patients. It’s a sleeve that wraps around the calf, that supposedly prevents DVTs (blood clots).

The theory is that it alternates inflating and deflating to stimulate blood flow and prevent clot formation in bedridden or surgical patients. After measuring the pump with my Cornet meter....I wonder how many clots, arrhythmias, or other issues these cause??
I knew it wouldn’t be pretty...as expected my Cornet readings were‼️‼️⚠️

I should’ve gotten a video of the readings each time it cycled, but I didn’t want to turn it back on in an already crappy hospital environment.
Saddens me how much harm hospital staff and the high EMF environment do to patients...many think they are helping....yet are clueless how much harm they are doing because they are not educated in the biophysics of care. Most hospital staff are also clueless about the health risk for themselves. I showed a couple of them and of course, they didn’t get it.

Bottom line, there are times when hospitalization or surgery are necessary in acute situations. If you find yourself needing surgery or in a hospital, be an advocate for yourself regarding these. The old standby of TED hose, getting your a$$ out of bed (of course there’s EMF from that too), walking, getting out of the hospital ASAP and back into the sun and cold, while grounding is ☀️ would be MUCH wiser clot prevention.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-12-20T14:39:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-redox-potential-fundamentally.22730/post-258530>

Now that you understand how the sun and water create redox in unison you might ask, well Jack, if water is so critical where did all the water on Earth come from since most of the inner rocky planets in our solar system have very little, while the outer planets seem to have a lot more? NASA has put forth the solar system formed by accretion discs in the nebular hypothesis that is now consensus in cosmology. Black Swans know that any consensus opinion is usally a time stamp for pseudoscience. What follows here might shock some. What if I was to tell you that the story of deuterium biology is linked to the electromagnetic force and its ability to pinch deuterium with is electric and magnetic fields to create the spark of life that the aromatic amino acids that form melatonin and NAD+ in your cytochromes needed and the planet Saturn, and its water rings.

Wait a minute........Jack are you trying to tell us that water in Earth has a different solar system zip code and that zip code is some how imprinted into the memory of water here on Earth? You might want to suspend your disbelief now, but it is now no longer hyperbole, it may be a plausible idea because of the 12/3/18 paper in Phys.org on the Saturnian system and its isotopic fractions of water from the rings and moons of Saturn. Titan, one of the moons of Saturn is known to have massive frozen oceans on it. I spoke about this moon long ago in the Cold Thermogenesis series and told you back then that data from Titan might someday be linked to the story of life on Earth. I said back then I believed that sleep was the primordial state of life and we evolved wakefulness as a result. I believed this then because I think it is the UVC light that the brainstem needed to innovate wakefulness in neurons using aromatic amino acids that make up the sleep hormone mechanisms in all plants and animals. Back them I went into little detail because we had sparse data sets to really make total quantum sense of it.

The link I was referring to back in 2011 was to the isotopic breakdown of deuterium to hydrogen in the water of Saturn's rings, Titan, and Phoebe oceans. They seemed to match Earth's oceans in identical fashion of 150ppm. Sleep is highly linked to redox and as you have seen in my threads Redox is a function of the interactions of water and sunlight. When redox drops sleep efficiency is also affected. It appears poor sleep and high deuterium fractions are deeply related inside of out mitochondrial matirx. This further provides more clues to me what is really behind sleep apnea syndromes in man. The probes we sent out to Saturn left Earth in the 2000's and the data from these trips has now been analyzed and published.

The nebular theory tells us that the isotopic fractions of deuterium in the outer planets should be ten times higher than water on Earth. It turns out this is false. We know know it is identical. This has some interesting implications. I cannot say I buy all the things in the video below hook line and sinker, but I can now say that the story of water on Earth and inside your mitochondria and in pant chloroplasts just took the most interesting turn in my lifetime.

Deuterium content and its ability to make full spectrum UV light under pressure marries up wonderfully with the 20 amino acids that DNA codes for on Earth. This link is present but not fully understood in Decemeber 2018. We know in nature everything is linked in some fashion and it is our job as Black Swans to figure out what nature is up too. This new bit of data is exciting to me because it opens the door to new ideas of how life formed on Earth and why life acts as it does on Earth today.

I have been carefully and painstakeningly been laying out the deuterium biology story on Earth in my Quantum Thermodynamic series on Patreon. This new paper out this month substantially pushes many of these ideas forward and it may mean that UVC light is among the most important non-linear mechanism in all of biology. This makes what I said in my Vermont 2018 talk even more important to the Black Swan in training. Enjoy this link. Nature never ceases to amaze me when I follow her clues for us.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-12-20T15:52:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-redox-potential-fundamentally.22730/post-258533>

It’s been said that the greatest power of the human mind is its ability to concentrate on one thing for an extended period of time. It turns out what we focus on is tied to our redox power in our brain.

If you’ve ever held a magnifying glass in the sun, you know how scattered sunlight can be focused to start a fire.

Did you know that sunlight in kids increases their focus and decreases their lack of attention?

Is this why John Ott's Sarasota school experiment of 1977 showed sunlight helps kids focus better?

Can you imagine what you or your kids could accomplish if you could concentrate your brain power into one bright beam and focus it like a laser on whatever you wish to accomplish! You do know that AM sunlight does this huh? You do know that UV light in sunlight can actually make a laser in us huh? (Vermont 2018 talk alert)

You may be looking to improve your concentration to perform better at work, to ace your exams, to increase reading comprehension, or to simply make everyday life easier. Sunlight does this for free.

Fortunately, anyone can develop their ability to concentrate if they know how. It is like brain surgery without a scalpel.

There are skills you can learn and steps you can take that enable your brain to work better. I teach them all the time. Most do not realize it.
Sunlight increases focused-attention span in kids, whereas fluorescent light decreases it dramatically.
The answer is simple.......sunlight increases the ATPase spin rate while man-made light reduces it in your matrix. It also reduces the water and CO2 the matrix makes too.

Normal redox power is -400mV at cytochrome one = NAD+/NADH. Just pasts cytochrome 2 it drops to -200mV. That -200mV is the limit of cytochrome C oxidase which is the 4th cytochrome. The 4th cytochrome is where humans make water from metabolism. Water absorbs all red light from 600 nm- 3100nm. The fourth cytochrome sits right in front of the ATPase which is the 5th cytochrome. The ATPase is also a red light chromophore. Cytochrome 4 has 4 red light chromophores. Are you seeing a trend here yet with RED LIGHT? Sunlight is 42% red........did you know that? When you drop below -200mV redox power in your matrix you are in deep shit. For example, blue light overexposure causes high insulin and low glucagon in blood plasma without any food input needed: Here is where the nnEMF and blue link to the destruction of redox power from beta-oxidation on the inner mitochondrial membrane to foster a Warburg metabolism. This means blue light and nnEMF only allow you to use glucose for fuel and as a result, your ATPase spin rate slows.........still feel good about technology, screens, and the light that it forces you to use? The take home? Put your kids in the sun...........

### Jack Kruse — 2019-01-29T23:55:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-redox-potential-fundamentally.22730/post-261761>

This is a podcast I did early in 2018 that was never released in the USA. RF/blue/microwave nnEMF blocks our ability to learn and it MAKES MAN EASIER TO CONTROL for those who employ it to their customers. Humans are not aware their sovereignty has been usurped by light waves. It was released for use in first in Isreal earlier in 2018. Now it is been released here on YouTube on Jan 23rd, 2019.

Nature built organisms on Earth are adapted to the 24-h solar day. the entire food web was also built this way.

Predictable daily cycles of light and dark allow for the synchronization of behavioral and biological processes to the external environment. For nonhuman animals, restricting activities to the appropriate temporal niche is crucial to fitness and survival. For humans, the temporal organization of physiology is PRIMORDIALLY important for health and wellness.

Over the past century, the boundaries of day and night have been obscured by the widespread adoption of electric light at night.

Circadian disruption has become dominant and is now prevalent among wildlife and humans. A growing body of ecological research points to nighttime lighting near urban areas as a major disruption to wildlife migration, foraging activities, reproduction, and immune function.

For humans, health consequences of circadian disruption by light at night are becoming increasingly apparent unless you are brain dead. Exposure to artificial light at night is linked to risk of breast cancer, metabolic disorders like diabetes, and psychiatric and behavioral disorders.

nnEMF blocks our ability to learn and it MAKES MAN EASIER TO CONTROL by algorithms broadcast over screens. This is done by blue light and by RF and microwaves. We focus below on human exposure to light at night and its consequences for circadian rhythms and mood to regain how we can think and make decisions for ourselves once again.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-redox-potential-fundamentally.22730/post-261829>

Screen time increases the likelihood of Adrenal Fatigue.
How?
Screen time is linked to AF via the butterfly effect of light on heme-based chromophores/ferroptosis/POMC/dopamine.
Most people have no idea it is a topologic defect caused by nnEMF in our environment that alter how the photoelectric can interact with our cells.
More proof that getting AM sunlight to program your RBCs is the key to avoiding AF?
This podcast gets into this photoelectric problem.
[https://drsherrillsellman.com/podcast/audio/wwmk/WWMK\_012419.mp3](https://drsherrillsellman.com/podcast/audio/wwmk/WWMK_012419.mp3)
When your eyes, skin, gut, or lung surface are afflicted by nnEMF trauma it induces cell damage in heme-based chromophore proteins that liberate something called CpG Islands from our nuclear DNA/RNA or our mtDNA from the cytochrome proteins like cytochrome c oxidase.
It has also been shown in the literature that RBCs homeostatically bind mtDNA, and RBC-mediated DNA scavenging is essential in mitigating tissue injury after CpG-DNA is liberated from heme-based proteins from destroyed cells. What kind of disease states should we expect to see cf-mtDNA elevated? Any disease where inflammation is induced by heme protein destruction = blue light hazard, ADRENAL FATIGUE, depression, nnEMF, sepsis, trauma and most mitochondrial and RBC diseases tied to alterations in circadian biology. All neurodegenerative diseases fit this bill too.
What protein controls circadian cycles in our surfaces I mentioned above? Sunlight induces changes in tissues below these surfaces. What gets programmed just below these surfaces? RBCs? What is in RBCs? Peroxiredoxins, cytochrome c oxidase, hemoglobin, catalase. All of them are heme-based proteins. Most of them are inducible proteins too. This means that the environmental EMF or oxygen levels induce their production. This is how biochemistry varies in tissues. Biochemical pathways are not foundational. The electromagnetic fields around us induce the biochemistry in our cells.
What do heme-based proteins due to the circadian mechanism controlled by PER1 protein? Peroxiredoxins affect the PER protein function by way of ferroptosis in the circadian clock gears of cells and this is where ADRENAL FATIGUE begins in human PVN in the brainstem. If you do not do this, eventually your PVN will become hypermethylated and be recalcitrant to most therapies. Why does the PVN get hypermethylated?
Tissue damage in the nervous system induce ferroptosis and this liberates Vitamin A in the retinal aldehyde form and this aldehyde destroys the heme-based proteins I mentioned above. What causes methylation problems in the brainstem? The liberation of CpG Islands from our mtDNA and DNA/RNA in damaged cells.
Red blood cells (RBCs) below our 4 surfaces need to be programmed by sunlight and no other electromagnetic fields. In this way, RBCs are the key modulators of the innate immune responses by scavenging these chemokines from blue light and nnEMF fields and damage.
Keeping your RBCs in tip-top shape is one of the best treatments for adrenal fatigue I know of. Seeing the AM sunrise, avoiding nnEMF day and night, and STRICT avoidance of ALAN post-sunset is MANDATORY to get better.
This Black Swan perspective helps explains why parabiosis studies in the literature have shown why young blood shows so many health benefits in disease states. Blood that is younger from a circadian standpoint, has the ability to clear these fragmented heme-based molecules from damaged cells.
I have hypothesized in my Patreon blog series that RBCs may attenuate CpG-induced inflammation in multiple organs through direct scavenging of CpG-containing DNA released from damaged cells.
How does our blood plasma link to this perspective Uncle Jack?
The innate immune system appears to use TLR9 on our blood cells for detecting unmethylated CpG dinucleotides which are liberated during the blue light hazard and nnEMF exposure associated with mental disease. The CpG Islands help tell the immune system how much damage has been done by pathogens and how to react. Why? CpG islands in pathogens vary compared to our own from our DNA/RNA and mtDNA. CpG islands are relatively common in bacterial and viral genomes but are highly methylated and uncommon in vertebrate genomes. People forget mitochondria have a bacterial lineage and this is why mitochondrial damage leads to a hypermethylated state in our CNS and PNS.
It begins even earlier in kids who come from parents who have AF, because those kids who are afflicted with the blue light hazard in their parent's germline occurs before the kid's brain is myelinated. A lack of myelination makes the child's brain more sensitive to nnEMF. When will we wake up to the power of sunlight over man's drugs?
The innate immune system in our blood plasma also has another backup system to help RBCs out clear the toxins. It appears to use TLR9 on our blood cells for detecting unmethylated CpG dinucleotides which are liberated during the blue light hazard and nnEMF exposure associated with Adrenal fatigue and the development of cognitive haze and eventual mental disease if the stimulus is chronically present in the environment.
The CpG Islands help tell the immune system how much damage has been done by pathogens and how they should induce the heme-based proteins.
THIS IS WHY AM SUNLIGHT IS IRREPLACEABLE WHEN YOU ARE AFFLICTED BY ADRENAL FATIGUE, in my opinion. The sunrise is the surprising best drug designed by Nature to keep us from AF. This is why AM sunlight creates dopamine, melatonin, and POMC make 6 other key brain chemicals it does in the eye and skin every AM you have your skin in the game = This leads to BDNF creation that REVERSES the hypermethylation in your brainstem.
This is BLACK SWAN MITOCHONDRIAC WISDOM HERE.

Flicker at this frequency is not as bad, as it is indistinguishable from pulsed light sources (a period of ON and a period of OFF) which are utilised in PBM and have been shown to be more effective than constant light for many conditions particularly those involving the brain. 100hz has been repeatedly shown to yield greater results than constant in many papers.

### Jack Kruse — 2022-06-09T19:44:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-redox-potential-fundamentally.22730/post-313476>

If we put a rat in a cage and give it 2 water bottles. One is just water and one is water laced with heroin or cocaine. The rat will almost always prefer the drugged water and almost always kill itself in a couple of weeks. That is our current theory of addiction. Is it right? Might a tighter connection with our environment that leads to entanglement or connection be the missing piece to the addiction equation? Is this radical thinking?
A wise person came along in the ’70s and said, “Well, hang on. We’re putting the rat in an empty cage under artificial light. It has nothing to do and it is doing it in bad light. Let’s try this a bit differently.” So the wise built Rat Park and Rat Park is like heaven for rats. Everything a rat could want is in Rat Park. Lovely food. Lots of sex. Lots of darkness because rats are NOCTURNAL. Other rats were present to befriend. Colored balls. Plus both water bottles, one with water and one with drugged water. But here’s what's fascinating: In Rat Park, they didn’t like the drugged water any longer. In fact, They hardly used it. None of them overdosed in this experiment. None of them use drugs in a way that looks like compulsion or addiction. What did the wise show? They showed that both the right-wing and left-wing theories of addiction are wrong. The right-wing theory is that it’s a moral failing, you’re a hedonist, you party too hard. The left-wing theory is that it takes you over, and your brain is hijacked. It turns out the connection or lack of connection to our native environment is the missing piece to addiction. The wise say it’s not your morality, it’s not your brain; it’s the cage that becomes your prison that is the key to why you are addicted to something. Addiction is largely an adaptation to your environment due to a lack of entanglement or connection. This leads to a desynchrony of how things operate in your cells and this changes your behavior.
Now, we created a society where significant numbers of us can't bear to be present in our lives without being on something, drinking, drugs, sex, shopping... We’ve created a hyperconsumerist, hyper individualist, isolated world that is, for many of us, more like the first cage than the bonded, connected cages we need.
The opposite of addiction is not sobriety. The opposite of addiction is connection or entanglement. And our whole society, the engine of it, is geared toward making us connect with things, not people. You are not a good consumer citizen if you spend your time bonding with the people around you and not stuff. In fact, we are trained from a young age to focus our hopes, dreams, and ambitions on things to buy and consume. Drug addiction is a subset to living a life without proper entanglement.

What if I told you that your diet and exercise habits are not the most important factors in determining your health & longevity? Timing, specifically circadian timing is the most critical piece of wellness to get right. What if I told you that light from your environment controls the timing mechanisms inside of your cells? Sounds crazy right? Well, according to much of the latest scientific research, it's not crazy at all. It turns out that the lighting that most of us spend the vast majority of our lives in is 'junk light.' And it might just be worse for your health than junk food.
When you are a unique thinker, you chronically put wisdom you find over profit and as a result, your ideas put you on the high tide that raises your ships.
Charge conservation is not like energy conservation ideas found in the laws of thermodynamics. Energy cannot be created or destroyed. Neither can charges. But charge confirmation does not mean that individual positive and negative charges cannot be created or destroyed. Why? Electric charge is ONLY carried by subatomic particles such as electrons & protons. These come from food via photosynthesis. Both of these particles are charged and they carry electrical information and energy can be created and destroyed in elementary particle reactions. CERN does this every day. So does the light of the sun. so does man-made light. Light changes the substrate particles used in mitochondria. This process requires precise timing and this is also disrupted by light and dark in our days. It turns out that light choices vary redox potential in mitochondria allowing for the alteration of electrons and protons inside of mitochondria and many other organelles in cells. That electrical variation creates tipping points in cells.
Be unique and attacks problems in new useful ways. Subtract the old ideas that have failed you. Many have called me crazy along my journey. I embrace it because I have found my purpose and I love my passion for it. You don't need to justify your love to anyone, you don't need to explain your love to anyone, you just need to practice your love in all things. Practice creates the master. I take it as a compliment today when somebody now calls me crazy. I would be offended if I was not considered unique by the skeptic. Blue light liberates Vitamin A which then changes its 3D atomic structure to become an aldehyde and that aldehyde releases its light to things around it. This emitted light destroys photoreceptor proteins inside of us. As a result of melanopsin, dysfunction lowers melatonin levels to destroy sleep. It also lowers DHA in your cell membranes. This does not allow the child's brain to connect to the world around them. As a result, the child's brain becomes HYPERMETHYLATED. This is how insidious brain damage begins.

Once sleep is destroyed in kids then autophagy fails in mitochondria and heteroplasmy rises because we cannot recycle mitochondria via a process called mitophagy. Mitophagy is a recycling process of bad engines that occur in mitochondria. The natural quantum synergies between light frequencies exist and this is what controls and directs the actions of chemicals, heavy metals, and organelles in cells. This includes being able to control or be controlled by radiations emitted by electric grids, WiFi, Bluetooth, cell towers, cordless phones, cell phones. In the Internet of things, wireless everything in total will lead to collateral damages few will predict in a 5G world. Light can do and affect things via multifactorial quantum mechanisms......there is no one path to Rome when it comes to cellular activation or deactivation.

We only think living cells work as a 'snapshot' in time, when in reality they work like a movie. Life is a sequential motion picture of light rays dancing through our optical windows in tissues. It is a very dynamic process driven by the energy imparted to hydrated protein antennae that act as resonant light levers for action sending and receiving information constantly to attune the system and build a fidelity to the signals it senses.

The screen built by nature in kids brain is the liquid crystalline array of topologic insulators designed to capture sunlight and use it in a myriad of ways allowed by quantum mechanics.

Cells are really a playground for solar photons that use quantum thermodynamics to operate their magic. Man-made blue light photons destroy our biology and it is worse in kids brains because they do not have the myelin to protect them from this effect. How often should this person eat oysters and liverwurst in terms of adding DHA/Vitamin A back into the mix? ### Inger — 2018-12-21T16:24:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters-and-liverwurst-how-often.22737/post-258632>

# Thread: Why Black lights?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-black-lights.22738/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ALEXIS TUDOR — 2018-12-21T16:30:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-black-lights.22738/post-258634>

I have tried to find this answer using the forum search engine which unfortunately sucks. Trying to understand where using black lights may be good if you can use fire in the night to light. I got one but want to use it with understanding.

When we say black light it sounds like it is within the violet spectrum. Concerning close to blue.

The colors of the visible light spectrum
****colour**** ****wavelength** interval** **frequency interval**
green ~ 500–565 **nm** ~ 600–530 THz
cyan ~ 485–500 **nm** ~ 620–600 THz
blue ~ 440–485 **nm** ~ 680–620 THz
violet ~ 380–440 **nm** ~ 790–680 THz

Thanks in advance for insights.

### JanSz — 2018-12-21T16:35:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-black-lights.22738/post-258635>

A **blacklight** (or often **black light**), also referred to as a **UV-A light**, **Wood's lamp**, or simply **ultraviolet light**, is a [lamp](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lamp_\(fixture\)) that emits long-wave ([UV-A](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UV-A)) [ultraviolet light](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ultraviolet_light) and not much [visible light](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Visible_light).

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blacklight](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blacklight)

### ALEXIS TUDOR — 2018-12-21T18:08:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-black-lights.22738/post-258650>

thanks for responding. So why would one use UVA in the night when there is none in nature?

### JanSz — 2018-12-21T19:06:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-black-lights.22738/post-258664>

> [ALEXIS TUDOR said:](/goto/post?id=258650)
>

Because workers in Chicago restaurant did the research for us.
And [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) highlighted that breadcrumb for us.
''

### kris90 — 2018-12-21T20:18:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-black-lights.22738/post-258673>

> [ALEXIS TUDOR said:](/goto/post?id=258650)
>
> thanks for responding. So why would one use UVA in the night when there is none in nature?

As a potential hack to absorb more UVA in your system. It doesn't appear to suppress melatonin, and in fact we make melatonin in the eye and skin from the combination of UVA+IRA light in the morning to program tryptophan.

Blacklights emit 380 nm light which seems to be a key frequency for photorepair in Dr. Fritz Popp's findings. It also coincidentally is the frequency that neuropsin works with.

> It is well known from biological laboratory experiments that if you blast a cell with UV light so that 99 per cent of the cell, including its DNA, is destroyed, you can almost entirely repair the damage in a single day just by illuminating the cell with the same wavelength at a much weaker intensity. To this day, scientists don’t understand this phenomenon, called photorepair, but no one has disputed it.
>
> Popp also knew that patients with xeroderma pigmentosum eventually die of skin cancer because their photorepair system can’t repair solar damage. He was also struck by the fact that **photorepair works most efficiently at 380 nm** — the same frequency that the cancer-causing compounds react to and scramble.
>
> This was where Popp made his logical leap. If the carcinogens only react to this frequency, it must somehow be linked to photorepair. If so, this would mean that there must be some kind of light in the body responsible for photorepair. A compound must cause cancer because it permanently blocks this light and scrambles it, so photorepair can’t work anymore.
>
> It seemed logical, but was it true?
>
> [https://www.biontologyarizona.com/fritz-albert-popp-cure-for-cancer/](https://www.biontologyarizona.com/fritz-albert-popp-cure-for-cancer/)

### Jenelle — 2019-04-13T20:22:55+0300

>
> Sounds good. Still using them? Do you use any other light sources accept for natural light in the kitchen? I am looking for a solution myself.

Yes ~ still working well. We have no window in the kitchen, and not a very open floor plan -- so really no natural light in there. It's one thing I've always hated about this house for the past 16 years.

We left one regular fluorescent tube above the sink for when more light is \*needed\* ... but it's so glaring to me. Definitely not on at bedtime.

We have one bulb (instead of two) on the bottom of the microwave/range hood, and we use it on the lowest setting when cooking. That's plenty.

In general, I've found that people are just so used to everything being BRIGHT ... and it really isn't necessary. Throughout our home, we have found ways to have just enough light for what is needed ~ and no more.

Dr. Kruse's book EpiPaleo Rx cited (page 248) that: "If you develop cold urticaria (hives or welts/rash) at this time, this is a sign you have HIGH levels of tissue and serum omega-6 content."

Dr. Kruse recommends: "...following the Paleogenic diet until you have a blood omega-6 to 3 ratio that is below 10 to 1. You can also test your serum for this ratio."

# Thread: The Opiate crisis, Suicides, POMC, B12 and 0G-5G......how did I know?

### Jack Kruse — 2018-12-24T03:57:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-opiate-crisis-suicides-pomc-b12-and-0g-5g-how-did-i-know.22749/post-258857>

Opioids kill more people than pain does.....let that sink in for a moment. My surgeon friends laughed at me in 2012 when I said technology addiction would lead to opiate addiction. Who is laughing now? I predicted we'd have an opiate crisis and I am not happy to have been right. “Opioids have overtaken car crashes and gun violence as the leading cause of death for Americans under 55. The epidemic has killed more people than H.I.V. at its peak, and the death toll exceeds those of the wars in Vietnam and Iraq combined.” #mitochondriacwisdom #BlackSwan

### Jack Kruse — 2018-12-24T14:48:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-opiate-crisis-suicides-pomc-b12-and-0g-5g-how-did-i-know.22749/post-258884>

Now for a Xmas eve DEEP lesson in what being a Black Swan really means:

So given what Uncle Jack predicted above...............what should you see right now in the literature especially with respect to vegans and vegetarians?

**Here is more on the vegan/vegetarian risks in a blue lit 5G world:** In a recent RCT done with no light or nnEMF controls with subjects with mild cognitive impairment and elevated homocysteine, B-vitamin supplementation decreased the rate of brain volume atrophy >50% compared to placebo. In those without elevated homocysteine, brain volume decline decreased by 25%. Since there were no LIGHT controls this allows the Black Swan to really see the effect of tech and light on our brains unencumbered. The researchers do not realize it and neither will most of the people who read the paper but I do not want ONE of you NOT to get the lesson. I want you all to see where the pieces fit by exposing your mind to this reality.
